44.left:
Pukhtoon

The taking of a single pound from England, a handful of barley from Japan, and a small piece of jade from Russia are not considered valuable. Rather, the reverse is true: the spoils of their conquests – pounds, grains, and jewels – are acquired by the English, the Japanese, and the Russians.

Each passing year, every building and every dwelling are transformed.  There is no progress, no improvement, no alteration to the land.  They have no connection to the country, and their paths lead only to its ruin.  This is the case for all nations, and for the Afghan people as well.  The interpreters, translators, registrars, the Commissioner’s assistant, and the tribal elders – all are subjected to poverty, hardship, and the influence of the wicked.  English schools and colleges have not brought prosperity or advancement; they will not, and they cannot. They do not learn the secrets of the land’s fertility, nor the properties of plants, nor the effects of minerals. They merely learn to “come, go” – the meaning of which eludes me. And this – (5+3=8), (5-4=1) – is all they understand. This education has brought no benefit to the land.

They are not interested in the frivolous pursuits, the idleness, the vanity, and the pride of the people. The Pashtuns, the scholars of the provinces, the poets, the lawyers, the traders, and the elders – they are seated on the side of the farmers and landowners, and their loud voices proclaim the interests of the latter. The owners of English property, with their English knowledge and English money, acquire all the land and possessions.

45.right:
**Khushal Khan’s Address at the Meeting**

**Concerning the Duties of the State**

Written by: Copywriter, Typist, Registry Editor,
Commissioner of Istanab, and Pashtun notables like Khanan, and the editors of the Tehsil – Qul is not the son of Baba Adam. The cause of this is the cruelty and inhumanity of the people, who are driven to poverty, starvation, and the clutches of the devil. The educated in English schools and colleges have not brought any blessing or progress to the country – and will not. They do not learn how to use the land’s resources, nor do they understand the progress that can be achieved through them. They do not know the properties of plants, nor the effects of minerals. They only know how to come and go (Come, go), and that is their secret – and that is (5+3=8), (5-4=1). This education has brought no blessing to the country so far. Instead, it has fostered wasteful spending, idleness, pride, and arrogance within the Pashtun people. The Pashtun, Tahsildars, Tehandars, lawyers, Bupers, and Muharras – Qul, by the cultivators and landowners, are seated in their favor, emitting loud, insistent cries. The English gentlemen’s knowledge of English and their English hats and English clothes were brought to the Pashtun –

January 1935

His eyes opened. The kind Florentine is seated on her knees, and on her face, the drops of water flow and darken. Florentine calls out to Saeed. Saeed, what is the use of hiding your secret from yourself and trying? I will not be your enemy. The ultimate truth was revealed – that the sons of Bani Ahmar, princes – now reside in their grandfather’s castle, in their father’s chamber. Alas, oh Bani Ahmar, what bad fortune you have, oh poor prince!

The prince, whenever he has a reason, a secret, turned away. I tell you of the exile of Andalusia until that time, all the talk of grief is hidden within the secret folds of his family. And whenever this story ends, I look at Florentine and he says, “Alas, Florentine, my hand is not yet outstretched to you. Every time I contemplate the future, the difficulty passes through my eyes.” The thing the prince does is more difficult than the present hardship. Saeed lowered his head and raised it again, howling in pain. He is ready for the severe hardships of this world. But Florentine does not see in you the strength of separation. (To be continued)
45.left:
Pashtun

Taking one rupee from England, procuring one barley from Japan, and bringing one idol from Russia is not desired. Rather, the cries of their servants are desired by the people of England, the people of the East are fed by the people of Japan, and the people of Japan are nourished by the people of Russia.

Every demolition and every camera appears to the common man. All the glory and all the wealth are only under the English. Today, the English are going to Hindustan. Therefore, the Paitans and Bupers will be under the feet of the jugglers and the musicians. This education does not free us, and in its place, servitude remains. We are now the English, the Germans, the Americans, and the Japanese – so that we may have the honor and the knowledge of their teachings, and our hearts filled with love and respect for our people. Let us build houses and fields – let us not be the servants of anyone, and let us not be judged by the tools of others. We should work so that we can make cloth from wool, and make pottery from clay, and clothe ourselves with the finest garments, and build houses. Let this be our education – so that we may be free from the need for servitude, and that our youth may be free from the burden of others.

46.right:
Pashtun

It is not pleasing to purchase a single penny from England, to quarry a stone from Japan, or to bring a stone from Russia. Rather, the earnings of those nations are diverted to the pickaxes of the English people, the lessons of the Indian people, and the rents of the Japanese people.

Every surveyor and every telescope reveals itself to man. The blessings of education and all prosperity are solely under the rule of the British. Today, the British are departing from India. Consequently, the Petians and Barristers will find themselves beneath the Dalans and Kalalans. This education does not liberate us from need; instead, it leaves us in servitude. We are employed as if to the British, Germans, Americans, and Japanese. May our hearts be filled with kindness, dignity, and love, and may we cultivate the arts and crafts. We must be freed from the necessity of servitude, and capable of performing our work without the sword or the tools of another. We should be crafting glass from sand, porcelain from gunpowder, clothing from wool, and weaving from wool – tasks that elevate our nation. Otherwise, we require such education, one that elevates us from the dust to the building of homes and dwellings.

What is familiar to the eye?

Now the question is, how can we procure the necessary funds to improve our education? We do not have money for cloth or blankets. In America, if someone becomes an American, or works for the army for three years, he receives a salary of approximately six hundred and forty rupees. This salary is sufficient to buy a small plot of land in America. And in our country, we receive three thousand rupees and live our respectable lives. This is the reason – that the American people are now the doctors of the world, and their knowledge is collected into one.

48.right:
In life, such matters become a source of joy and reflection. It is well-known that the cities of Europe’s sister nations – particularly those in the East – were once dominated by European powers and Christian clergy, who sought to suppress local traditions and customs. However, in the past, these efforts yielded little more than modest results. Now, however, the situation is markedly different. The Turkic peoples, once viewed with disdain, are now the subject of much discussion and scrutiny.  Presently, in Europe, the news of Muslims and Christians is often concealed, yet the West remains inextricably linked to the East.  China has separated from Russia, Iran from Afghanistan, and Turkey from the rest of the region. Europe, for its part, held sway, and this influence manifested in dreams of controlling the Turkic lands.  Indeed, the European powers, particularly those in the West, sought to undermine the Ottoman Empire, viewing its leaders with suspicion and disdain.  Each ruler, in turn, fabricated his own narrative, assigning the task of disseminating information to newspapers, who in turn spread rumors designed to discredit the Ottomans and portray them as weak and vulnerable.  These reports, circulated widely, fueled the perception that the Ottoman Empire was a shadow of its former self.  As a result, the Muslim world, particularly in the East, became fearful and apprehensive, mirroring the anxieties of the Russian and Chinese populations.  Efforts were made to unite the Muslim communities of India, Persia, and Afghanistan, seeking to bolster their strength against the perceived threat.  The European powers, recognizing this growing unity, sought to exploit it, attempting to draw the Muslim nations into their own conflicts.

Current:
It is a matter of great concern that such rumors and misinformation circulate, causing unnecessary anxiety and division. The Europeans, in their attempts to control the flow of information, often resort to tactics that are both deceitful and harmful.  They seek to portray the Turkic peoples as barbaric and untrustworthy, fueling prejudice and animosity.  It is important to remember that the Turkic peoples have a long and proud history, and their contributions to the world are often overlooked.  The European powers, driven by their own ambitions and prejudices, seek to undermine the Turkic peoples and their allies, creating instability and conflict.  The news from Europe is filled with reports of unrest and violence, and it is clear that the European powers are not acting in the best interests of the region.  The situation is further complicated by the fact that the European powers are attempting to manipulate the Muslim communities of India, seeking to exploit their loyalty and divide them.  It is crucial to recognize that the European powers are not acting in good faith, and their actions are ultimately detrimental to the stability and prosperity of the region.  The spread of misinformation and propaganda is a dangerous weapon, and it is essential to be vigilant in countering its effects.  We must strive to promote understanding and cooperation between all peoples, and to resist the attempts of the European powers to sow discord and division.

49.right:
January 1919

Certain voices have circulated a claim – that the reports circulating regarding the people of Hindustan in Afghanistan are largely unfounded. It is understood that those residing under the injustices of their own governments in India, do not grasp the nature of the dealings being conducted with our Afghan people. In Afghanistan, these individuals are treated with considerable kindness, a boon for those striving for the liberation of Hindustan. The rumors concerning the people of Afghanistan, presently disseminated, are, in truth, a source of great hope for those seeking independence within India. It is imperative that such benevolence be extended to these individuals, fostering their efforts toward self-determination. Indeed, the pursuit of freedom by the people of Hindustan relies heavily upon the support found within Afghanistan.  It is undeniable that these reports represent a significant aspiration for the people of Hindustan.

Furthermore, some suggest that the current state of affairs – the King’s capricious behavior, the populace’s reliance on his generosity, the flow of wealth to his court, and the constant shifting of authority – is a cause for concern.  It is wise to recognize one’s friends and enemies.  A virtuous man speaks truthfully.

50.right:
January 1920

It is said that in Afghanistan, regarding the news circulating about the people of Hindustan, some claim it is largely unfounded. They say that the Muslims of Hindustan, in light of what has transpired in Afghanistan, are being portrayed in a manner that is deeply misleading. We understand that the people of Hindustan, under the oppression of their governments at home, do not fully grasp how their affairs are viewed in Afghanistan. There, they are treated with great kindness, particularly by those striving for the freedom of Hindustan. The news circulating about the people of Afghanistan, in truth, is for those seeking liberation for the Muslims of Hindustan. It is vitally important that the people of Hindustan be treated with kindness in Afghanistan, so that they may pursue their aspirations for independence. We understand that this news is, in essence, for those in Hindustan who are working towards freedom. It is not unfounded that the people of Hindustan seek independence in Afghanistan.
50.left:
January 1920

The world is consumed by turmoil, and it seems that Muslims are becoming increasingly pessimistic. If what is said is true, then it is only a matter of time before this becomes a reality. Regarding Afghanistan, when news reaches us of the English exploiting Muslims with force, demanding they pay exorbitant taxes, forcing them to work without pay, confining them to their homes, suppressing gatherings, and destroying religious assemblies, the minds of the Muslims become clouded. Even their reason falters. Khan, however, avoids speaking about such matters; he is past his prime. It is clear that this habit has its roots in a long-standing custom. Indeed, it is a proverb that a donkey that eats grass will destroy the fodder.

This is also the current proverb, and it is not being ignored. News has reached us that the order to worship God in the Turkestan region has been banned, and the instructions to desecrate holy places have been outlawed under the government of Hindustan. Then, in a short time, the price of merchandise in the market has risen dramatically. It is a strange and unprecedented occurrence.  The news is circulating that the Governor, Mr. Dost Mohammad Khan, is now a prisoner of the British, and that the King’s relations with Afghanistan are strained.  The King has not visited Jallabad, nor has his envoy been sent there, nor has he issued any decrees for the region.  Nor has the King been relieved of his duties to the Bakhtiar province.  Indeed, the Afghan Consul General, Mr. General, who is stationed in the bosom of the Persian Queen, reports that this news is spreading through the newspapers of the British Empire, which are filled with such falsehoods. Some say that certain people claim that in Afghanistan, the news circulating about the people of Hindustan is largely unfounded. They say that the Muslims of Hindustan, under the oppression of their governments at home, do not fully grasp how their affairs are viewed in Afghanistan. There, they are treated with great kindness, particularly by those striving for the freedom of Hindustan. The news circulating about the people of Afghanistan, in truth, is for those seeking liberation for the Muslims of Hindustan. It is not unfounded that the people of Hindustan seek independence in Afghanistan.

51.right:
According to accounts from this period of Pashto, a king ruled with a particular concern for his people. He directed considerable thought and effort towards them, undertaking numerous actions in the name of religion. It was observed that this king held a deep affection for his subjects, and that he invested heavily in their well-being, often expressing his sentiments through public pronouncements.  His aim was to ensure the service of his people to their faith, a devotion that extended to every aspect of their lives.  Muslims, it was believed, should strive to uphold their religious obligations, and the name of God should be invoked in all endeavors.  The king’s advisors, including figures like Sheikh Moharim, were deeply involved in matters pertaining to the Persian Gulf, overseeing the administration of the region and seeking to maintain stability.  Furthermore, the interests of Afghanistan and Persia were closely intertwined, and the king’s efforts were focused on fostering amicable relations between the two nations.  News arrived from Russia and the East, concerning the ascension of His Highness Shah Reza Shah, prompting a sense of anticipation and hope.  Finally, it was learned that Sheikh Moharim’s interests extended to the Persian Gulf, where his influence was firmly established, and the effects of his actions were felt throughout the region.  The king’s governor, Dost Mohammad Khan, was highly regarded, and the arrival of Shah Reza was met with great enthusiasm.  The relationship between Afghanistan and Iran was also well-established, and both countries maintained a close connection.  Should any opportunities arise, as suggested by the counsel of Gazi Mokal Kamel, the king might consider ventures into Kurdistan, where the affairs of the region were overseen by the esteemed Sardar Keyl.  The king’s commitment to his people was unwavering, and he sought to maintain their loyalty and prosperity.  He was known for his generosity and his dedication to the welfare of his subjects.

52.right:
January 1929

Declaration of Truth

O Pashtuns! Be aware of the truth!
An enemy from Europe has brought ruin,
Plundering your Pashtun homeland and people,
This people are disgraced in the world.
Ghazi Amanullah, all kings salute him.
The enemy’s twisted schemes have been shattered,
The enemy, driven by his own conscience,
Whether a scholar, a priest, or a governor,
Was corrupted by bribery, destroying Pashtun homes and villages.
The Muslim, in the hands of Satan,
The enemy is consumed by impiety,
O Pashtuns, fulfill the enemy’s demands,
Who will kill you with a spear like a sword?
Guns and aircrafts – a curse upon the world and its time!
Now, abandon this foolishness,
Take up the rifle and direct it towards Ghazi
Khan, and sacrifice yourself for Amanullah.
The enemy desires not to diminish your honor,
There is no loyalty or courage in Pashtun lands,
No challenge, no devotion within this nation.
Do not harbor hatred for these people.
Show the kings your power and prestige,
Increase the value of the Pashtun, elevate their glory,
In a single rupee, the Pashtun’s greatness shines.
Cast off the chains of the devil,
He has bribed all men.
The king’s rebellion has brought a judgment upon Pashtun lands,
The world cries out against you,
Who owns property and whose is the government?
Everything was prosperous, but you have indulged in folly.
Every nation condemns you,
You are a black stain upon the earth,
Blame yourself for your own failings.
You attacked Kabul at this hour,
May the world have mercy on you.
50

First, he did for the victims of Afghanistan
He is one of the descendants of Ghazi Amanullah Khan
He offers thanks to those who have many scooters
The Chief Commissioner of the border, Kushner, has been appointed to the registry and the wire.
Tej Muhammad Sukutri, Reforms of Afghanistan
Atma Teri

Address to the Muslims of Kandahar
The homeland is free from violence, let it be blessed!
Yusuf, in the white of Egypt,
Merchants have traveled far, bringing goods from beyond, nothing impure.
In a single step, it is as if it has risen to the sky.
Yet, your reign has brought loss.

The events of yesterday and today were a difficult test, apparently, you are busy.
I wish you would wake up from this dream, then you would see the injustice.
The truth will not die, even if there are many enemies.
Today is the turn of Kandahar’s Muslims.

If you are in a full fanjah, there is a troubled bed on top of it, like a storm in the desert.

This cable is both a tablet and a pen.
The teacher is sad.

54.right:
The Status of Freedom

What constitutes a certificate of freedom?

The dead lie in the grave, and the slave meets his end; arms and legs, eyes, and a three-legged stance. The value of a free man’s possessions is a matter of considerable debate. A free man’s dwelling is found not in the desert, but alongside it. His voice should rise throughout the night, day and night. When the Ghulam fled towards Peshawar, he was a man of great stature and strength. He who increases, is himself a powerful figure. Power belongs to the free man. If he is free, he may consume almonds as he pleases. If the homeland is a mother, and the father is the homeland, then free dogs and cats roam, and rabbits find refuge. There is a dog in Azad’s estate. In the extended years of the crescent moon, a man finds freedom. Punishment upon the free man is a blessing. A foreign house is divided, revealing its secrets. What was the religion of those who were once enslaved? The purpose of freedom is to instill terror. The Ghulam’s lament echoes in the fire of the furnace. He was once confined within the slave’s dwelling, and now he is released. His identity, his connection, and his entire being are now free. This is his inheritance. He is a citizen of Hindustan and a subject of Jam. His place is in the slave’s meadow. Rundra is upon the slave, and my name is forgotten. This escape is the work of a free man. A slave woman is a bride in the slave nation. Slavery is a presence in Hindustan. The slave is a leader and an Imam in the slave nation. He laughs at the mouth of the slave, and praises him. In the Deccan, a seer is appointed, and Nizam governs. This is the birth of the Ghulam. The Torah is inscribed upon the Ghulam’s estate. And the Ghulam, the month of Mashana, is a reward. 55.right:
Pashtun
January 1920

Kingald served as the Vice-Chancellor at the time. In a previous era, he was known for accepting bribes, but he vehemently opposed such practices. At the same time, he refused to participate in the distribution of bribes. This steadfastness was a testament to his integrity. The reason for his resistance stemmed from the corrupt nature of the marketplace, where such dealings were commonplace. He responded to accusations of bribery with a firm declaration: “A true leader does not engage in such deceit.” He further stated, “This is a new order, and without bribery, no work can be accomplished.” He then added, “The burden of leadership rests upon my shoulders, and I will not allow my clan to suffer for my sake.” He asserted, “If I am to be held accountable, let it be for the good of my people.”

He continued, “It is said that the government is a burden, but I will not allow it to become a source of hardship for my people.” He then stated, “If I were to be imprisoned for the sake of my clan, I would rather face that fate than see my people suffer.”

He was then questioned: “What is the purpose of the King’s authority?” He replied, “Without bribery, no work can be done. And I speak the truth.” He then added, “This is a new order, and without bribery, how can it be achieved?” He paused, considering his words carefully. “It is said that the burden of the kingdom rests upon my shoulders, and I will not allow my clan to suffer for my sake.”

In response, he stated, “If I am to be held accountable, let it be for the good of my people.”

He then said, “It is said that the government is a burden, but I will not allow it to become a source of hardship for my people.” He continued, “If I were to be imprisoned for the sake of my clan, I would rather face that fate than see my people suffer.”

A messenger arrived, informing him of his impending arrest. His jailer also arrived. The jailer was a devout Muslim, but a rogue, and impoverished. The messenger, a haughty Ferangi, spoke with disdain about Kingald’s court. However, he was unfamiliar with the English language. He believed that a prisoner’s life in jail was a miserable existence. Kingald’s lawyer arrived later, and pleaded with him. “Look! The King is suffering. Kingald has been reined in. He will pay for it. And they have not paid.” He continued, “You will be locked in this room for the rest of your life, and Michan will come out.”

He cried out, “If death is dear, self-death is a failure.” Kingald understood this. He was born into the movement, so he was brought out like a grain of mustard seed. It was a difficult task. This would happen every day.

I gave thanks with great joy that day. I was wrong, so I consoled myself. It takes effort. Maqe Zam and Tule, Michan to and Radiyam. and Dham started. The match does not work - and what will happen?

Look at one of the shakurs, and the warkhali abe came running. What and hey! I will be grateful. I offer my condolences from the bottom of my heart. I took it in May. A prisoner came to the dacoit. Eight and seven. That you will not forget it. We raised the source of support a little. Walk me.

Showed Poke it in a way. After that, he left. I was suffering from the ninth labor. The whole of this crime is in the heart of Mazigro. Bakken crossed my arms. Today Jamdan came, by the labor, and he laughed. And Khan with him.

Maybe I’ll bring you some sugar. I’m in Michan and Plow. Let’s start ladies. A few hours later, I see that Daewoo Sapo

56.right:
January 1920

I understand that my actions have an effect on the morals of others – morals are not forged through words alone; rather, they are deeply intertwined with pride, and the perceived harm resulting from my deeds. The grace of God and His kindness prevent my own character from being unduly altered by these actions. Any hardship endured, particularly within a society where moral standards are rigorously upheld, may seem like a commendable endeavor. However, I find myself pondering the nature of this perceived virtue – a tale and strategy designed to enlighten my people regarding my benevolent intentions. It is a matter of surprise to me. What service can I offer to my own faith, nation, and country? It is evident that I hold affection, loyalty, and service foremost for my nation and country. Yet, I recognize that true benefit lies not in undertaking excessive hardship or difficulty, but in fostering goodwill. If I were to appear before a powerful ruler, it would be prudent to consider the potential advantages and disadvantages of any course of action. And if a task lies before me, it is essential to weigh the considerations carefully. 

The second trial, and a profound one, was endured – and it was a source of great misfortune for Meon Sahib. He spoke to me with great affection, expressing his concerns – a state of confinement, a state of being overlooked – two others would not have listened. 

Subsequently, the aforementioned Darugh Sahib ceased his communication. We were forced to silence him, and occasionally, the mischief of Darugh and his own transgressions arose. But then, the rain began to fall again, healing me once more. Others heard my lament. 

A man arrived in the middle, and he spoke to me with urgency. He said, “Mr. Meon has arrived and speaks with great enthusiasm about what you have done.” I replied, “Let us not dwell on this confinement.” Two others would not have listened. 

Then, the aforementioned Darugh Sahib ceased speaking. We were obliged to silence him. Sometimes, the mischief of Darugh and his own transgressions arose. But then, the rain began to fall again, healing me once more. Others heard my lament. 

I expressed my gratitude for this day, for I am alive and well, and I fear that any mistake I might make. I must strive to improve myself, to learn and to grow. I will continue to seek knowledge and to strive for betterment. And so, I offer my thanks. A single thought occurs to me – a moment of solace, as I am alive and well, and not in chains. 

I contemplate the circumstances that led me to this state – to a prisoner’s fate. I was brought here, far from my home. I said, “Let them not think that I will be content with this situation, or that I will be easily swayed.” They told me, “Let them bring you to the jailer’s office.” The jailer came, and he spoke to me. He said, “You are a good man.”

57.right:
The essence of this matter lies in the service to the people and the nation – there is nothing beyond that. Should any individual be prepared to dedicate themselves to this service, the door to them shall always remain open, and it does. Our faith dictates that Allah has ordained us, and we are not inclined to deny the worth of others. We are subjects and servants of our people, and those who contribute to the advancement of the nation and its land – for it is present in all endeavors – yet this is all.

Another matter concerns the misguided notions of certain individuals, who believe that the publication of news and accounts is a means of seeking honor and inflicting insult upon scholars. This notion is entirely erroneous for the Pashtuns. We are not driven by sorrow or malice; rather, we hold our people in esteem and respect. This magazine is intended to promote the betterment of the Pashtun people, and it is imperative that we guide them towards a righteous path, away from error and towards a more prosperous future. Let us, therefore, address the Khan, the Mullah, the Sayyid, and all those who traverse the roads and paths of Kachhar, exposing falsehoods and urging them to uphold the honor of the Pashtun people and the integrity of our land.

58.right:
A respected Mulla Sahib

January 1920

On this path

In a single week, the poor will be starving
How much food will they have?
Now, each man is a Khan, one through usury, the other through force
Yet they blame the people. Sahukar voted for Bahadur Khan.
Then, the owners of the fields ceased all their provisions, reflecting the plight of the people of that region.
Let every one wish well for his people – for both faiths are present in Pashtun society.
Malik Tala has not yet served anything of value.

Dev Khan Fazal Khyal

As for what?

Regarding the actions of the people themselves –

Pass on patience.
15

January 1920

Dev Mulla Sahib

A question:

1.  Disregard the legends of the Qur’an.
2.  Ascend to the inheritance of the Prophet.
3.  Do not bring forth matters of blame upon others.
4.  The heart is always in need of sustenance.
5.  When life is prosperous, the stomach is full.
6.  Worship is permissible in Islam when one is not preoccupied.
7.  Mosques and religious schools are always found.
8.  Listen to the accounts of the world’s disbelief and the Islam of the Fergies.

Those who were burdened with the Torah, and did not carry it as an ox carries bundles of scrolls.

Or, regarding the honor of the scholars – it is not that they sought the meaning of the Holy Qur’an, but rather that the Pashtuns are not like them.  The Khan, the elder, the Syed, those in the valleys and the plains – all are like this.  Let us not speak of their faults, nor should we consider them to be on the right path.  Let us strive to bring our Pashtun nation and our people to the proper path, and let the messages and news from our land be intended for the benefit and purpose of the world.  It is a great thing that the names of Mullahs appear in these publications – let us not cause confusion, let us not insult the honor of the people, and let us not consider that the Pashtuns are subservient to the ignorant.  The Pashtuns are loyal to the righteous and to God.  A further point is that some believe that we are self-sufficient, and that Pashtuns will not be included in the affairs of others.  Other topics will be included in these publications.

Certainly, this is a mistake. The Pashtun is the Pashtun of the Pashtuns.  Anyone who brings a subject to the fore will be included.  But it is necessary that the subject be included.  Now, Mullah and Khan Sahibs have written to the Pashtun – let us first examine their claims.  Let us consider the merits of the Pashtun and the people of this region, and let us strive to bring them to the right path.  The news and publications from our land are intended for the benefit and purpose of the world.  And this is the matter – why do the names of Mullahs appear in these publications?  Let us not cause confusion, let us not insult the honor of the people, and let us not consider that the Pashtuns are subservient to the ignorant.  The Pashtuns are loyal to the righteous and to God.  A further point is that some believe that we are self-sufficient, and that Pashtuns will not be included in the affairs of others.  Other topics will be included in these publications.

Certainly, this is a mistake. The Pashtun is the Pashtun of the Pashtuns.  Anyone who brings a subject to the fore will be included.  But it is necessary that the subject be included.  Now, Mullah and Khan Sahibs have written to the Pashtun – let us first examine their claims.

Let us consider the matter of the King – information for the reform of the nation – is the defense of the nation’s honor, and its prosperity, and its security.  (Abdul Gafar)

59.right:
Henry expressed interest, a simple sadness weighed upon him, as if burdened by cruelty, wickedness, and obscenity. He wondered, “What judgment does the blessed Sharia permit for such a person?” Henry, concerned for the impoverished, did not wish to speak of these matters with the group, fearing their judgment. He believed that this man, like the unbeliever, would announce a bribe, a disbeliever in the ways of the Arabs – that is, one who investigates and does not act. He questioned how defects could be remedied, and if such a state were to exist throughout all of India, and if it were to be consumed and devoured.

Now the question arises: what does this signify regarding the disbeliever? The answer is that there are two types of political trickery among people – one is disbelieving, and the other is deceitful and imaginative. When these are combined, this shamelessness makes the houses of Muslims, and the groups of them are infidels. For what purpose do they wage war? They deny life, and they deny prayer. And this work is an offense to us Muslims – we would not advise them, or if we did, we would pay them with money. This state will continue to exist, as in the width of the money, and the wage earners will be distressed.

At this time, the heaven of Miaf, and Ghazi, and the two are beautiful, and they are blameless. On this, you will pray for the harvest, whether it is the sacrifice or the resurrection.

Oh, simple Pashtun brothers! And oh, do not speak of politics among gentlemen! What difference does it make to yourselves – to consider the world’s affairs in the courts of Khan – this is not the place for worldly politics. Do not be ignorant of the news – read many pages in the Holy Qur’an, may God bless you – the situation of the bard tribes has been described – that the bride, and it was ruined – Monar because of this, the Pashtun tribes were destroyed and destroyed. I will not die from Zdenya, stop.

60.right:
I believe in my words to go—and she rose, and came to the grave, lowering her head—and in a dream, she saw herself sleeping in the same manner—and she said, “Then the people fell silent—and I wondered—and I observed that these, my religious devotees (the sorrowful of our faith), do not approve of what I mean—of the boastful claims of the rulers—and of your law, which you impose upon the kingdom—I offer amends to the enemy, and I give you orders to destroy them with our weapons. On the other hand, Captain King’s cannons and machine guns have not been used in the country for a long time. In order to protect the honor and dignity and to win the freedom of their country, the British government showed its readiness to endure all kinds of difficulties in the way of fighting the enemy, that no obstacle can be created in the way of the enemy’s coercion and tyranny and their struggle and jihad against the enemy, nor can they bow down to the enemy’s oppression and the enemy’s coercion and tyranny, in this way among them Another British Government in a long and never-ending struggle against the enemy’s actions and the enemy’s hostile actions. In order to continue the struggle and jihad against each other, the British government took a step against the enemy that history will always remember. remain as
And Shah—so from her, Captain King Khafeh came to the Khan on the golden night of Besalar Abdul Rahim and Plow—and met and became acquainted with each other—and learned about each other in the events—Captain King asked, “What are you doing, and what is this fresh, mature company in these mountains?”
Helen replied, “These people are too ignorant and simple—they harbor an aversion to our religion—because they see every evil on their path, and they take care of themselves. Secondly, I am not doing all this for my people and country.”
After consulting, Helen ordered the people—and she said, “Loghdar, Pech, Kotaks, swords, and guns—in order to fight Jihad according to the Murshid’s order in Plas, and they were waiting for it.” They surrounded the boat with cannons and machine guns—At that time, Captain King did not appear towards her—Captain King told him, “The people are gathered—and all my people are dark with the machine guns—now is the time to show your loyalty and help the people.”

61.right:
January 1930

Discussions regarding India: Who will destroy Islam in the world?
The enlightenment of the Pashtuns! A brief justice is required. Let us be weary for the sake of God – let us not approach those who sell Islam and offer bribes – let our friends, our enemies, friends of Islam, our enemies, these good human beings, friends, enemies, and let us be mindful. Secondly, these things are not for our people or our kingdom.

I, for my part, after considering counsel, decree that all people be held accountable and speak falsehood – lie, deceive, beat, and throw stones – curse, and throw guns in a single building according to the order of the Mirshah – and await the time when the angels of heaven will order its commencement. On the other hand, Captain King, along with his son Mohammad Khan and Abdul Rahim, are gathered on one side, with cannons and machines guns tightly connected – At this time, the aforementioned (Mirsh) appeared from Captain King’s side – Captain King looked at him, as the people had gathered in the open, and our people were standing ready with the machines guns – “Are you loyal, and a witness to the worth of the tribes – and ready to assist when needed.”

=== PASHTO SOURCE (for reference) ===

62.right:
Be fair – who establishes the standard of a nation’s greatness? Let us remember God. Langar, sustained by money, now that the British possess funds, we do not. Careful consideration is required, for our people, stripped of their faith and clad in foreign garments, are being diminished and destroyed. A non-Muslim, feigning adherence to Islam, will arrive. He will not be considered a Sheikh, but a mere pretender. Wealth and possessions will be showered upon him, and he will disappear, seeking refuge elsewhere.

Let us cast aside lengthy pronouncements and consider the present day. The kingdom of the Turks was shattered by internal strife. The Afghan Sultanate, a brief and disastrous experiment (Blessed be God). This misfortune has befallen us Muslims, a consequence of the Widow Lawrence (known in the West as the Widow Lawrence), and presently, we cannot discern what fate awaits us.

In the service of the Pashtun people, generally, in the service of religious scholars, Sayyids, and elders, we offer this humble plea: a small contribution can resolve divisions between religion and politics. Yet, still, we hold onto hope that this nation may be spared destruction. May this debt be repaid. Let us invoke the names of Islam and the peaceful rulers. In this state, we bear this responsibility—

25
January 1920

And you shall be held accountable before Allah.
We seek the prosperity, happiness, and freedom of Mink and the region, even if it is only a fleeting hope. It will not originate from Khyber. And it is evident from Khyber that this is the source of power. And the authority…
This is the case – if the government of Afghanistan is now under the control of Ghazi Amanullah Khan… O Pashtuns! For the sake of God, for the sake of the people and the country, let us strive with all our might. Let Ghazi Amanullah Khan return to his country with honor and with the spirit of his people. Let this be our aspiration. Peace be upon him. America is approaching Hassan Afghan.

…

Mahmoud Jirga’s Two Representatives (Karzai)

It is necessary to state these matters first. What is the purpose and meaning of the Mahmoud Jirga? Why is this information withheld?  It is generally understood that this is for the imagination of the people – there is no need for this meeting. Everyone acknowledges this. Let us not name individuals without a letter or a formal designation; it is considered an extravagance.  As in past times, in provinces throughout the world, Congresses and Caliphates maintain these associations. However, many people remain outside their influence, and the Muslims of other regions do not even consider them.  They believe that these individuals will bring about reforms for the people of Hindustan. And they claim to have taken action towards this goal, and to have obtained certain privileges.  The situation is as follows: the Viceroy of Hindustan, this is our predicament. The government is considering what to do regarding the people of Hindustan and their rights. And this is our situation – we are awaiting developments.  If we are to be judged, we are in a state of uncertainty.

Regarding the past, the Khyber region has little connection to these matters.  On the other hand, the people of Hindustan believe that the Caliphate is a religious matter of Khyber.  Other people are not concerned with it, besides the Hindus and Sikhs.  It is said that the Caliphate has no need for power, only for faith.  The Pashtuns, however, are not involved in the Congress or the Caliphate, only in matters of faith.  If Hazrat Umer (may God have mercy on him) had acted in this manner, it would have been a wise course.  Many Pashtuns harbor suspicions regarding the Congress and the Caliphate’s true intentions. This is undeniable. They believe that these powers are seeking to divide the people. The result of this is that the Congress and the Caliphate are not drawing people to themselves.  It is as if the Pashtuns are saying that Hazrat Umer and his companions were the ideal rulers.  Let us not be involved in such endeavors.  Let us consider the situation of our people, and let us not engage in these matters.  If the Congress is a committee, or if the Caliphate is a…

63.right:
The situation in Hindustan is developing, as it appears now, and through this committee, the people of this province are told that Muslims, or Sikhs, are sacrificing themselves for the people of India. They received cooperation from the other side – specifically, a gift of thousands of rupees. Consequently, there is more cooperation in the border region than anywhere else, but in reality, India profits from this, and those who have made no effort towards it claim to have brought reforms to India and possess legitimate authority. This discovery has led to the announcement of various reforms (Dominion) to some of the victims. I observe that the Viceroy of India is aware of our situation at this time. The government considers what is best, and does not favor any house other than India. This is the state of our suffering – that yesterday, in our Gwad (Afghanistan), and among the English (British) and some others, we sought refuge in those events. And because of this, the people of our border are divided, with money brought from India to meet their needs. Presently, there are hundreds of rupees available. Consequently, since we do not have a Jirga of our own, we are reliant on the generosity of others.

In Khyber, there is little connection with other classes. On the other hand, the people of India believe that the Caliphate is a religious matter for Muslims. What happens to some of these people – Hindus and Sikhs – besides the fact that there are no Muslims? They claim that the Caliphate requires something of all, and that the greatest benefit for all is the needy people of the Caliphate, who understand the religious aspects of this Caliphate. The Pashtuns, however, do not understand the Congress or the Caliphate. If dear heir were to try, it would be costly. In the hearts of some Pashtuns, there is a great conflict between the Congress and the true purpose of the Khilafat. There is no doubt in their hearts. In this assumption, what has been done by India. The result of this matter is that the Congress and the Khilafat are not close to these people or this tribe. It is clear that these people have constructed something for India. This matter has resulted in the Congress and the Khilafat not being close to these people or this tribe. If the Pashtuns say that the heirs and Qamul have built a Jirga that is used in such works, they do not know about each other’s situation, because our people have their own Jirga. And we have nothing. Others are preparing to work for it. What congress or civil work do you want the word of caliphate. Therefore, the Jirga of the people is strong and the civil work is such that the majority of the people of the nation are Pashtuns. For this, it became clear that the People’s Assembly is not for the Congress or the Caliphate, but rather for the strengthening of it, in which the people outside the Congress and the Caliphate are included. If the second purpose of our People’s Jirga is that in years it will become our own Jirga, if the Congress is a committee. or the caliphate

64.right:
January 1928

I have previously informed my closest friends that our monthly *Pashtun Risala* does not receive subscribers, nor are returned copies sent back.  Furthermore, the magazine rarely reaches its readers, and some even fail to understand Pashto.  However, my associates have not agreed with this arrangement, which I consider a matter of commerce.  I now write to express my concern regarding a delay in receiving a copy of the *Pashtun Risala*, and to request a resolution.

Letter

To the Service of Mr. Abdul Ghaffar Khan

May this letter find you well. I understand from your recent writing that many of our brothers do not receive the *Pashtun Risala*.  I now submit a formal complaint: I have yet to receive a copy of the magazine this month.  We have consistently sent each copy by post, attached to every letter, and in duplicate – two copies per letter – but this has not been sufficient.  I was not particularly troubled by this until I recently examined the December issue in the shop of Hakeem Abdul Rahim.  Therefore, I offer my sincere apologies and request that I be promptly sent a true copy of the magazine.  Should this require an increased price, I will gladly pay it.  However, if it is not possible to send a copy at all, please do not send it through any other means, nor delay its arrival, lest it be delivered to the *Pashtun Akhwan* – the esteemed Mr. Maulvi Sahib of Waziristan – and the Pashtun engineers serving under him, nor to Reddy Pashtun Sahib of Waziristan.  I await your response with anticipation, inquiring whether you will provide me with the necessary Pashto and Pashtu translations, and if you are committed to the welfare of our people.

(Abdul Ghaffar)
Pashtun

And beyond the world?

O, Pashtun men! Do you understand that mere employment or other engagements, without genuine connection, do not lead to advancement.  We have come to the homes of our relatives, and what have we done for the people of our tribe? (Abdul Ghaffar)  Our banners and agreements will not be honored.

Pashtun and Political Agent

It is well known that our Pashtuns are in a particular state of affairs.  News and magazines of their tribes, disseminated through correspondence, foster agreement and progress among those nations, and inform them of the events and circumstances of the new era.

However, our pioneering Pashto publication, the *Pashtun* newspaper and magazine, is not distributed in this manner – this monthly magazine is not sent to the righteous people, nor does it reach them through the machinations of the oppressor.  Consequently, the people are disheartened and lose hope.  It is the imagination of the people that these small matters, roads, railways, and the expenses of books, are maintained for the peace and benefit of the people.

Yet, experience has shown that this is incorrect; these are being used to serve the interests of this king.  In this, their own purpose and benefit are paramount.

65.right:
January 1929

I have long stated to my close friends that the monthly *Pukhtoon* magazine, despite not being purchased or subscribed to by many, and often not reaching its readers, is a matter of some concern to me. Indeed, my friends disagree on this point, and this is the basis of my business. I seek to provide a service, and until now, I have done so without complaint, receiving no objections from the purchasers.

However, I write to you now with a formal complaint regarding a single magazine – the *Pukhtoon*. To date, I have not received a copy of the December issue. I have consistently sent each letter accompanying the magazine to the recipient, along with the main text, but two copies are always sent with each letter – a practice I have followed without issue until now.  I was quite perplexed by this recent occurrence, particularly after seeing the December issue in the hands of Hakim Abdul Rahim Khan. I offer my sincerest apologies for this oversight and will promptly dispatch a new copy. Should this not be possible, I will endeavor to send a replacement as soon as practicable, ensuring it reaches you through a different channel, avoiding any further inconvenience caused by the *Zulam* (tyrant) of the region.  I will also inquire with Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan regarding this matter.
65.left:
In the service of Mr. Abdul Ghaffar Khan,

Please accept this as a formal notification that you have noted in the monthly *Pukhtoon* magazine that many of my close relatives do not receive the magazine.  Now, I write to you with a final complaint, as I have yet to receive the current issue of the *Pukhtoon*.  Until now, we have always sent each letter accompanying the magazine to the recipient, along with the main text, two copies of each.  This has been without issue. However, today I learned from Hakim Abdul Rahim Khan that the December issue was seen in the hands of Mr. Khan. I offer my apologies and will promptly send a new copy. If this is not possible, I will endeavor to send a replacement as soon as possible, through a different route, to avoid any further complaints from the purchasers.

---

Regarding a matter unknown to the Pashtun, interest and loss appear beneficial. And if anyone is fully aware of this, then he should look at it a little more carefully. It is necessary for the people of the border province to once again have a great deal of understanding about this village. In India, there is this group, which is called (All India Youth League), compared to our Zalmo Jirga of Pashtuns, there are many special differences in the culture and politics of Pashtuns. We know that this is a very important task for Pashtuns, that any Jirga, which is a Pashtun province, will be able to discuss and consult with the people of Pakhtunkhwa about the politics of Pashtuns and their demands. At this time, when Pashtuns take this responsibility for their unity, it is clearly a sign of great pride. For the creation of this group, a new and strong unity is needed for the Pashtuns at this time. There is a wide participation of the people in the establishment of this Jirga, and the contribution of each person in it is acceptable as a national duty. In the construction of this Jirga, it works as a foundation in which a new phase is taken for the future of Pashtuns. In September 1929, it was decided to establish such a Jirga for the people of the border province, and in October it was decided to hold a meeting for the establishment of the Central Jirga, and the decision of such a form of this Jirga is in use in the province.

66.right:
There reside in Bonn such noble people – those who serve their nation.
If anyone does not do so, the inevitable result is awakening within the community.
On November 23rd, Bando Abdul Akbar Khan, the head of the Jirga, and the moderator,
along with Lala Bahadur Khan and Pera Ismail Khan, convened – and
Abdul Qafar Khan, representing Farang and seeking a righteous path – all of us gathered on November 25th
at the Tank. Pera Ismail Khan held a meeting on November 26th –
Sardar Muhammad Aurangzeb Khan, a representative of the P.E.L., and Muhammad Ramzan Khan, a representative of the M.A.L.P., were also present – and
speeches were delivered, resulting in a significant political awakening in Dera Ismail Khan, far exceeding our initial assessment.
There were few attendees at the meeting; it falls to the organizers to make greater efforts and cultivate knowledgeable and working people within the community, safeguarding the nation’s interests.  It functions as a foundation, paving a new path for the Pashtun future.  In September 1929, a decision was made to establish a Jirga for the people of the Province of سرحد, and in October, a meeting was scheduled to establish a central Jirga, with a plan for a structure to be implemented throughout the province.
66.left:
There are people in Bonn who do not serve their people – this is the inevitable consequence of a lack of awakening within the community.

---

It is regrettable that Pashtuns do not recognize the value of service to their people – and the inevitable outcome is a surge of awareness within the community.

---

---

On the fifth of November, Bando arrived at the Tank – and the people’s love and loyalty to the Pashtunor were evident.
We will be surprised to see how these people love their Pashtunor – and from that place, they went to the evening meeting at Bannu –
At eight o’clock in the night, there was a meeting in Bannu – Hindus, Muslims, and Sikhs were present—more than nine
The thing was – whether they were professionals, lawyers, brewers
He is also very happy with his brothers in Mongolia
On November 23rd, we did not stop our efforts – we went to the Great Mosque
There were thousands of people present.  Lala Khan delivered speeches – and the purpose was explained.
How much love and loyalty there is among the lawyers and gentlemen of Bannu –

67.right:
January 1920

He possessed a fervent motivation, driven by a desire to honor his lineage. These men delighted in exchanging laughter and good cheer with one another. Mr. Bahadur, it seems, was known for his generosity, traveling from this district to other villages and paying seven rupees and ten rupees per house.

Furthermore, the people of these villages often regarded travelers with suspicion, refusing them hospitality. It is their ancestral homeland, a sacred space, and they would not simply offer it to anyone. They demand payment for its use, a practice the travelers themselves must accept. The people, however, have repeatedly voiced their grievances to the authorities, expressing their dissatisfaction with this arrangement. It is through this collective voice that we, the people, discern the true state of affairs. If this official system were to extend its reach to us, we would be forced to seek the counsel of a wise and respected elder, lending our meager resources to support our impoverished community.

On November 8th, the women of the region paid their respects to education. Along the road, near the village of Bo Clay, they offered their heartfelt devotion. We were astonished to witness such profound loyalty to their Pashtun heritage. Consequently, they convened a meeting in Banun, gathering Hindus, Muslims, and Sikhs. The atmosphere was particularly uplifting, as professionals, lawyers, and elders expressed their joy at contributing to the well-being of their community.

On November 23rd, we did not conclude our efforts at the Jama Masjid. Instead, we held a grand assembly, attended by thousands of people. Speeches were delivered by Lala Khan, elucidating the purpose of the gathering. The outpouring of love and sincerity displayed by the gentlemen of Banun’s legal profession was truly remarkable.
67.left:
Pashtun
30

Within Banun, a diverse and honorable populace resides, all striving to serve their nation. Consequently, the inevitable outcome is the rise of awareness throughout the community. On November 23rd, Mrs. Abdul Kabir Khan presided over the Jirga, and Mr. Lala Ismail Khan served as the moderator. Mr. Lala Bahadur Khan and Mr. Abdul Qafar Khan joined the proceedings, along with Mr. Meki. We had gathered in Tank on November 5th.

Amidst the camaraderie, we enjoyed meetings with many friends in Tank, sharing mutual affection and respect with lawyers and gentlemen. In addition to this, we often contemplated the future.

---

On November 28th, we journeyed to Banun, encountering the people of the region along the way. Their profound love and loyalty to their Pashtun heritage were evident.  We convened a meeting in Banun, gathering Hindus, Muslims, and Sikhs. The atmosphere was particularly vibrant, as professionals, lawyers, and elders expressed their joy at contributing to the well-being of their community.  We then proceeded to the meeting, where we conveyed the message of our elders to all present. With sincere hearts, we expressed our gratitude and esteem.  At approximately 6:00 PM, we held a meeting in Banun.  Again, Hindus, Muslims, and Sikhs were present. The space was filled with warmth and goodwill. It was a joyous occasion, as professionals, lawyers, and elders celebrated their shared heritage.

---

On November 25th, a large gathering was held in Tank, attended by numerous residents of the region.  Esteemed tribal leaders were also present.  During the meeting, we conveyed the message of our elders to all, expressing our gratitude and esteem.  On December 12th, a gathering was held in Lech, with participants also traveling from Kohat.  The organizers had informed the local community of the event.  Prayers were offered and requests were made.

---

On December 8th, we resumed our journey, concluding our tour of the regions of Baderg, Aeru, known throughout the area, Koht, Meena, Koht, and Spin.  It is well-known that these regions are rich in resources.  There is no doubt that the people are prepared, and the work of some laborers is being undertaken.  The goods are being sent to these regions, and it is a matter of record that the people are eager to receive them.  The wealthy landowners of the region have generously provided the necessary supplies.  The people have repeatedly expressed their gratitude to these landowners, and they continue to do so.  If these landowners were to provide assistance, it would be a great boon to the people.  However, if they were to deny assistance, it would be a great hardship.  The people of these mountainous regions have long suffered under the weight of the government’s demands, and they are now demanding a fairer share.  It is not uncommon for the people to be forced to sell their possessions to meet the government’s demands.  They are often subjected to unjust taxes and fees.  It is a grave injustice that the government does not recognize the rights of these people.  Furthermore, it is a grave injustice that non-Pashtuns are allowed to hunt and graze in these areas, while the Pashtun people are denied the same freedoms.  The elders of these tribes implore the government to protect the rights of their people, to ensure their freedom, prosperity, and honor, and to prevent them from being exploited by others.  Let the government not force them to sell their possessions to strangers, nor to deprive them of their ancestral lands.  Let the government ensure that the tribes are governed by their own leaders, who will protect their interests and maintain their independence.

68.right:
On January 25th, a large gathering convened in Tank, attended by prominent figures from the region. These influential Khans were also present. During this meeting, we conveyed the message of the elder, expressing our sincere esteem and appreciation for their generosity. On December 12th, a gathering took place in Lech, with attendees traveling from Kot and many loyal supporters were reported to have arrived from the surrounding areas. Prayers and requests were conveyed to Peshawar.

On December 12th, I resumed my journey, traveling to Badragh, Ero, Rani Zane, Kot, Miana, Kot, and Spin Kharo, and the Alandi Valley. It is undeniable that the people of these areas are prepared and eager. Some workers have established a small trading post in Badragh. In the middle of Shuni, due to the efforts of Mr. Abd al-Latif Sahib Effendi and his associates, an independent Islamic school has been established. The students there are remarkably accomplished.

January 1923

33

The road continued onward, concluding its cycle in the Spin Kharo region. The journey was long and arduous, marked by challenging terrain and frequent rainfall, often impacting the well-being of travelers.

Regarding the region of Miana, the mountains remain steadfast, and the land is well-suited for cultivating various crops. Hunting is abundant, with plentiful supplies of gazelle, fawn, and other game. The people of this area once enjoyed a prosperous existence, and the government’s attention to their needs has been long overdue. It is regrettable that, for many years, the people of this region have endured the hardships imposed upon them by the government. It is not permissible for anyone to sell the rights of their homeland for personal gain.  It is not right for outsiders to hunt freely and exploit the people of this region, and to disregard the well-being of the local population.  The leaders of this nation should guide their people towards prosperity, freedom, security, and honor, rather than selling their sovereignty to others.  They should not prioritize their own interests over the needs of their people, nor should they allow others to dictate their affairs.

On December 24th, I traveled with Mr. Abdul Kabir Khan, Mr. Mohammad Ramadan Khan, and Mr. Tanek to Tank, where I met with many of my friends.  At the trading post, we maintain close ties with the merchants and workers of the area, expressing our affection and goodwill.  Furthermore, we often contemplate matters of mutual interest.

69.right:
Let it be known. That our esteemed Khan Murad is residing in America, a matter of great fortune. It is a blessing to learn of his charitable contributions each year, as he generously provides for the needs of our community. I said, therefore, that the path to our charity should not be diverted. We entrust our benefactions to the care of our leader, Abdul Ghaffar Khan, who manages them with wisdom and diligence, bringing considerable benefit to our family and nation.

Thus, many wise men concurred, agreeing that such charitable endeavors are vital for the prosperity of our nation and people. Indeed, as Khan Murad has rightly observed, a modest investment yields a substantial return, and he instructs us to convey this sentiment. Others echo this sentiment, urging us to follow suit in our own generosity, thereby furthering our collective well-being.

I offer myself to you in service, and I hold such hopes for the future. Should I ever be granted the opportunity to bestow a gift, you are most welcome to receive it. I am sending you a check for six and five rupees, should you find it fitting to accept. Let us strive to emulate the generosity of our American brethren, and proceed upon their path.

And now, as the time for the annual review approaches, I recall the words of our Pashtun brethren in America – that they dedicate considerable sums to the advancement of knowledge and the betterment of their community.  It is a matter of great importance that we, too, contribute to these noble causes.

Let it be recorded:

Receipt
(Map)
The Society for the Propagation of Charity among the Pashtuns residing in America – a conveyance of funds for the dissemination of knowledge. Received, by the Collector of Funds for the Propagation of Pashto, from the Pashtuns of America.

It is not without reason that we have been granted this opportunity, and it is a matter of great importance that we continue to support the advancement of knowledge, and that we do so with diligence and foresight.

70.right:
January 1920

I have heard from my Pashtun brethren in America, a people who are dedicating themselves to a great service and love, and are bestowing considerable sums of money – Amen!

The reality is that in this day and age, one cannot accomplish anything without funds. Observe, when a nation lacks a strong treasury, it cannot support new initiatives, nor can it establish hospitals or schools, nor can it effectively serve its people and its faith.

It would be a mistake to assume that the Pashtuns should be treated as other tribes. Rather, their charitable endeavors, while not immediately benefiting the entire nation, are rooted in the traditions of our past – a past where such acts of service were freely given, without expectation of immediate return. Today, however, this method of charity causes a detriment to the nation.

Times have changed, and it is incumbent upon Muslims to adapt accordingly. They have failed to do so, for the benefit derived from their actions is now a hindrance. In the past, the service of the faith was provided freely to the people; now, individuals collect funds for others, but then distribute them, maintaining a system of service to the community and supporting the stipends of those in need.

Today, we see that everything is conducted through monetary exchange. The times have demanded this shift, and it is no longer possible to establish a new movement, publish literature, or maintain a seminary without funds. Nor can a man be honored for his devotion to God if he does not dedicate himself to the service of his people.

Therefore, it is incumbent upon Muslims today to embrace the spirit of the times. Let the Pashtuns not unite under a single national treasury, based on the wealth of other tribes, but rather, let them support their nation and alleviate the suffering of its people. Let Islam be the guide for the servants and the supported. I trust that our Pashtun brethren will emulate the example of our American Pashtun brothers and follow in their footsteps.

On the occasion of receiving a donation from the Pashtun Welfare Society of the American Pashtuns for the purpose of distributing funds for the benefit of the Muslim poor, I have received this receipt:

Receipt
(Map)
Anjuman Islah Pashtuns – an organization established by Pashtuns residing in America to distribute funds for the benefit of the Muslim poor. Received, by the American Pashtun representative responsible for collecting Pashtun salaries for the benefit of others.

=== NEXT SPREAD ===

71.right:
Afghanistan and Nadir Khan

United Kabul Noor Basr

“In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful,” I say with all sincerity.

Let me express my heartfelt desire to stand with the people of my nation.

It is true that today, without money, it is impossible to achieve even basic necessities. Observe, when a people are impoverished, they cannot afford to fund new initiatives, hospitals, or schools, nor can they serve the interests of their nation, their land, or their faith. And if a people lack financial resources, they are reduced to mere shadows of their former selves.

Some may ask why Pashtuns do not emulate other peoples, claiming this is a falsehood. However, their generosity, though often delayed, is not a permanent solution; it reflects the traditions of a bygone era, hindering the progress of the nation.

Times have changed, and it is imperative that Muslims have adapted as well. They have failed to do so, because their generosity does not benefit the nation, but rather diminishes it. In the past, service to the faith was freely offered, and we would collect funds, deliver them, and continue the service to the nation and to the stipends of the faithful.

Today, everything is driven by money. There is no room for religious propagation, education, or even the provision of a decent burial, unless funded by money. No one can truly serve their nation and God without financial support.

Therefore, it is now incumbent upon Muslims to embrace the spirit of the times. Pashtuns should not, as other peoples do, establish a national fund, but rather support their own people and alleviate their needs, and Islam should continue to provide stipends, support, and guidance. I hope that our Pashtun brethren will emulate the example of the Pashtuns in America, following their lead.

When I received news of the charitable contributions of our American brethren through the “Association for the Propagation of Education” in the name of the “Society for the Welfare of Pashtuns,” I was deeply moved. Let the record show:

Receipt
(Map)
The Association for the Welfare of Pashtuns, established by the residents of America for the distribution of funds for the benefit of Pashtuns. Received, for the purpose of dissemination, by the Treasurer of the Pashtun Welfare Society of America.

It is not without money that one can serve God or the nation, and no one should die without dedicating their life to the service of God and their people. If one is a servant, as our Pashtuns are, they will simply exist, and if they are poor, then…

Therefore, it is now incumbent upon Muslims to embrace the spirit of the times. Pashtuns should not, as other peoples do, establish a national fund, but rather support their own people and alleviate their needs, and Islam should continue to provide stipends, support, and guidance. I hope that our Pashtun brethren will emulate the example of the Pashtuns in America, following their lead.

When I received news of the charitable contributions of our American brethren through the “Association for the Propagation of Education” in the name of the “Society for the Welfare of Pashtuns,” I was deeply moved. Let the record show:

Receipt
(Map)
The Association for the Welfare of Pashtuns, established by the residents of America for the distribution of funds for the benefit of Pashtuns. Received, for the purpose of dissemination, by the Treasurer of the Pashtun Welfare Society of America.
71.left:
The mind of the “Pashtoon”

United Kabul Noor Basr

I hear reports of the activities of the “Pashtoon” in Kabul. The journal “Pashtoon” deserves the pride of our nation. Its reports of progress and the advancement of our homeland and the solidarity of our Pashtun people will serve as a testament to its value.

However, I am struck by a sense of surprise and wonder. When I examine the name of the secretary of “Pashtoon” – Durr-e-Jan and Abdulqadir Khan – I find them listed. But I suspect – may God guide us – that this is not the same Abdulqadir Khan, the “Pasawar Doom,” “Letter Writer,” who is known for his correspondence.

I ponder – if this is indeed Abdulqadir Khan, then I ask the following questions on behalf of my friends: First, the questions I have raised – and second, the suspicion that these questions are not his. If this secretary is not Abdulqadir Khan, then these questions have no connection to him –

For what is worth, if there is no justice, then the just will not be rewarded. Those who are in power should not oppress the people, but should be accountable to God. The people are yearning for justice, and they will not be satisfied until their grievances are addressed.

In the past, the king sacrificed for the people, and he was praised for it. Today, we see pharaohs ruling with arrogance.

The people want freedom and equality. Your own policy is strong.

On the way, the old Bolshevik

If we show our head, we will not let go.

We will not bow our heads, no matter what. The truth is that we will always be Pashtuns, regardless of the problems we face.

We will continue to serve the nation, with discipline and integrity.

Let the people be amazed by the generosity of the nation. If the wealthy people serve the nation, then…

It is a great shame that the nation’s state is so poor. Serving one’s own people and country is no sin. No one should have a bad friend, or a coin, or an Indian, for any purpose. And the poor should not be ignored. While we are commanded by the people, that you are the people

serve Nath will fulfill the necessity of resurrection on our day

And if someone is not late, then read the Qur'an and read it. (festival)

72.right:
Pashtun
January 1920
Turkish Script
(Istanbul)
29th December 1919

I have learned both Pashto and Turkish in Istanbul, and it has become apparent that the voices of the city’s residents are being heard. Therefore, I wish to offer one of Laden’s esteemed pronouncements for your consideration.

I perceive that the sign of high necessity and nobility is clearly evident in you, and that you provide proof of service to the nation, to humanity, and to Islam – a service which deserves the utmost praise. Let me express my sincere admiration for it.

You, possessing a keen and alert mind, and the steadfast friends of the press, are filled with hope. I pray that the Pashtuns will not be subjected to ignorance and its attendant hardships.

Indeed, it is the duty of every tribe to serve their leaders and the interests of the government. It is astonishing to see how the impoverished amongst the Pashtun people react to such service. The government’s benefactors continue to assist the Afghan people in their efforts at reform. This association actively supports education in Afghanistan.

We, the Pashtuns, are aware that this association operates in Herat, under the guidance of the esteemed Khan Sahib. May he continue to progress with his steps, and may he hope that the Pashtuns will not be subjected to the darkness of ignorance and its consequences.

It is known that the service of the people is the first duty of every tribe, and the protection of the poor is a matter of great importance. The government’s benefactors continue to assist the Afghan people in their efforts at reform. This association actively supports education in Afghanistan.

We, the Pashtuns, are aware that this association operates in Herat, under the guidance of the esteemed Khan Sahib. May he continue to progress with his steps, and may he hope that the Pashtuns will not be subjected to the darkness of ignorance and its consequences.

Let it be known that the service of the people, and the protection of the nation, is no crime. Any act of malice, whether theft, bribery, or dealings with the Hindustanis, is a transgression. And to deprive the poor of their livelihood is also a grave offense. Yet, even when we are subjected to the commands of the people, we must not abandon our duty in the day of judgment. And if one refuses to serve his people, let him turn to the Quran and reflect upon it. (May God be with him).
72.left:
It is a truth that without justice, the deserving are deprived of their due. Who is deserving of justice if we ourselves do not grant it? May God grant us a righteous ruler, who does not oppress his people with tyranny. Let the honest and virtuous be appointed to positions of authority, and let the corrupt be removed from power. For many years, the council has been dominated by those who have no regard for the welfare of the nation, and whose only concern is their own personal gain. Let the government be filled with men of wisdom and intellect, who will guide the nation towards progress.  For the intellect is the cornerstone of the nation, and it is destined to be a part of the leadership of the Pashtuns.  Let the wise men of the nation be appointed to positions of leadership, and let their counsel be sought.  It is a tradition that the decisions of the council are made by consensus, and that no one is forced to agree.  May God Almighty always be with every one who strives for good. You will not stray from this path. Like the brave warriors of the past, you are steadfast.  Let no secret be hidden in your hearts. The flames of freedom will burn brightly, but even with this freedom, the Pashtun will always be a Pashtun.  In the service of difficulty, the order of the Pashtun is the source of strength.

---
