Lear on Menaheb. Dear Muhammad Khansaheb. Khan Saheb of Myat. of which
moo sir Mr. Hakeem Abdul Muqtadar. And the king is very late
I am thankful that the car has done more.

Pashtun men were coming, and more work than Dashan in the said period
worked May God reward them all. Amin (Nazim Jirga of Mayan Ahmad Shah)

Princess’s Lament
(Events from a long time ago on Nazm)

All Laos (to slip on the teeth) - your power was thousands
It was born. Who has paid attention to the good things of the world, and worries
got pregnant And not to bear the pain and burden of your delicate mood.
Rise up to the throne. Give your good Zakah, and be with me
It's late!!

108.right:
Employees and others were arrested. I purchased it. His negligence and the resulting failure led to the crown and throne being locked, and a different chariot was commissioned. All conversations were directed to the Moradi of Nakhriag, and it was sought. I cried out to my own ignorance repeatedly, still bearing the same burden. And I count my rights. However, the perpetrators remained deaf to these pleas. And it was thus destroyed, and continues to be.

It is the Quran, it is you, the Muslims, it is the truth, and there is harmony and mutual sympathy. Yet, you, the Oghuz and others, have seized the gardens belonging to you and brought them here. This is my Islam.

Pashtuns! Do you seek to embark on a journey of self-deception and unification? Do you gather in our homeland to deliberate and issue decrees, hoping to forge a single Pashtun assembly? Let it be known that some ignorant and self-serving Pashtuns spread false rumors, attributing great virtue to the benevolent Abdul Ghaffar Khan. These people, steeped in Pashto and ignorance, believe that falsehoods spoken by such individuals will harm the common people and cause them to despair and be silenced. Furthermore, they mistakenly believe that such schemes will bring honor to them, rather than misfortune.

I urge you, therefore, to dispel these lies and to protect the interests of the people. If you do not, may God guide you. After this, the session concluded, and the people were impoverished.

(This is the opinion of Judge Mohammad Hussain Wardak)

March 1930

29 Sha’ban (28 days)

A letter from a friend of Khama
A letter from Mohammad Nader Khan Padshah

To my dear sister-in-law and former friend, Charanki Delia Sheta, peace be upon you!

I eagerly await the arrival of your Pashtun magazine.
My friend, Mohammad Nader Khan Padshah, has arrived in Afghanistan and is now an orphan.
I was your old friend, and I remain with you in spirit.
I am writing to you from Jalalabad, Etihad East, with a history to share.
In the Pashtun press, your magazine shines brightly.
His Highness Nadir Shah is also keenly interested in your publication, and he frequently requests to receive a copy.
I am pleased to fulfill this request. The sword is brought before them, and they read with affection for their father.
Previous subjects appreciate your efforts.

Grandma, I am sending you a gift for Zeri – a young woman with dark eyes.
She eagerly awaits your every service and assistance.
May God continue and direct this message.
I aspire to be a Pashto person who nourishes the heart and not the stomach.
I am ready to serve you.
Your friend, Burhanuddin Munshi
His Highness

109.right:
March 1920

The Pen of Malik Khawar and Varan Karo: A Lament for the Land

The government has a reputation for justice and integrity, free from the corruption and extortion that plague the police and the courts. This is a truth not readily acknowledged by those in power – a reality starkly absent from the higher courts. The wealthy landowners, like building blocks, are systematically exploited, their lands stripped bare, while the impoverished struggle to survive. The savage instincts of man, unchecked, will inevitably descend upon the vulnerable. Let us not over-irrigate our fields, lest we burden the already struggling farmer, whose plight deserves our compassion. His name will not be forgotten; his need will be met. Let the canals flow freely, and the landlord’s water supply secured.

I implore you, heed the warnings of ignorance and prejudice! Let us gather under the leadership of our esteemed elder and Pashtun brother, Abdul Ghaffar Khan, in a grand assembly, and offer every sacrifice for the service of our nation and kingdom. Let us prepare all necessary provisions to support him in his noble endeavor, ensuring prosperity for all within our lands – both near and far.

However, let us not be deceived. Some Pashtuns, driven by malice and self-interest, spread false rumors, claiming that charitable figures like Abdul Ghaffar are being unjustly persecuted. These misguided individuals, fueled by ignorance and prejudice, believe that the common people will be harmed by such falsehoods, and that they will be left to bear the consequences.  Furthermore, I believe it is essential that we warn our Pashtun brethren against the deceitful schemes of certain Arab influences, so that all Pashtuns may be aware of the treachery and manipulation employed by those seeking to undermine our unity.

110.right:
Pashtun

What is the right of death, Manon Ken, compared to the Pashtuns, everyone saw this contempt. Because this is the right and education of the entire Pashtun people. And because this is the fault of her hatred and bad manners of those people, who themselves are not worthy of education. And the truth is, Mashal-Kin is a remarkably small and inferior song of the nation. And the Pashtun people of this land do not crave wealth. Despite these words, there remained a Pashtun tribe in London, and they were bound to it, so all members of the brotherhood curse the head of Toba Sir.

Pashtun brothers! The foreigners boast excessively. Because we are human beings. But their justice and oppressive government are not to our liking. We imposed a fine of three rupees per year on the people, and we were very poor. And this fine was not paid – until now. While many people sold their possessions for the benefit of the wealthy, and entered this revenue – but no one understood. So, let the cruel and unjust government of fines and illegal fines be banished from this land, and let those who have fled this country return.

Let us do this. We have cried out our hearts three times, now let us pay off the debts of three thousand pechaos and one rupee fine – it is strange.

All of us present at this meeting strongly appeal to the government – to relieve us of this heavy burden – or not.

May God bless you.

After this meeting concluded, and the people were exhausted – so.

(This is the opinion of Judge Muhammad Hussain)
Liquid matter

(The red color does not hide in ash bins)

We Pashtuns are a most unfortunate people.  Who can be a model for us, intelligent and worthy, and sympathize with us and love us? And the people of Malik are in the western part of the country – so the Pashtuns love and cherish the best of us. Let us know their value and numbers. We were opposed to their worst enemy. We disbelieved them. And those who live outside their country – they are ungrateful to God – and God, the Holy One, does not grant progress to the nation (if it is grateful).

You can read about this in the newspapers.  The prince of Abli was present – a nation of European kings.

There was Ken Schweick.  And he was the Sartaj of the Pantheon, with a dark eye and a sweet brother Amanullah Khan also sued Sharif and Tali Badshah Warla Khan, and he sat by the fire with her.

We thank the King on behalf of all Pashtuns. We are so proud of the King of Pashtuns.

(Blessed birds are happy to be happy)
(Wizna Khutn is the benefit of the head of the family)

Oh! We do not know our brother, our beloved, our uncle. And we did not value Amanullah Khan. But we admire the people of Europe and their identity. Enough.

110.left:
The following is a note on the translation choices:

*   **Formal Language:** I’ve used more formal phrasing (“is the right and education,” “it is not possible,” “strongly appeal”) to match the tone of a 1928-1939 magazine.
*   **Word Choice:** I’ve replaced some simpler words with more evocative and slightly archaic terms (e.g., “contempt” instead of “disrespect,” “boast excessively” instead of “claim too much”).
*   **Sentence Structure:** I’ve adjusted sentence structure to be more flowing and less literal.
*   **Names and Dates:** I’ve ensured that all names and dates are preserved exactly as they appear in the original Pashto.
*   **Clarity:** I’ve aimed for clarity while maintaining the original meaning.
*   **“Liquid matter”**: This is a difficult phrase. I've kept it as is, assuming it's a specific, perhaps somewhat cryptic, expression.

111.right:
Syed Jamaluddin Afghani
(remainder of last month)

Syed Jamaluddin Sahib possessed this disposition and came to know himself. He intended to travel to Egypt to observe and derive benefit, with the aim of studying books and seeking tranquility, and had no intention of remaining for long. However, upon meeting the King of Riyadh in Egypt, he found the King to be exceedingly pleased and ordered the payment of ten thousand *tanah* (currency) to the Egyptian government. This sum was of no use whatsoever in Afghanistan; such a salary should not be imposed.

After a period of time, more and more people returned to Bannu from the Taliban, and they benefited from his teachings. From them, they began to teach higher sciences, rhetoric, wisdom, theory, natural philosophy, mysticism, and the principles of jurisprudence. They established schools in their own homes and were surrounded by their people. Those who possessed greater knowledge and understanding than the Taliban, and were open to free thought, criticism, and debate, were also discouraged.

The Egyptian writer, Ishaq Noah (correspondent), in his book *al-Darr* published by the writer, wrote concerning Syed Sahib’s reputation in the newspapers of that time: “In those days, many articles were published in the newspapers of Egypt, awakening the thoughts of the common people.”

Whoever comes to the world is surely like him.
The world is full of thousands of black cities after you.
This life passes like a dream.
The great flood of life, on whomever it falls, the mountains are heavy.
No one has demolished the houses in front of the flood.
Because of this fear, the wrestlers are happy.

Dakcha is not dead, he is not wise, he is not slandered.
If you spend a lot of money on it, you are going to have to drink Dajan wine.
Late ancestors believed that the former place had passed away.
The stubborn enemy is not known, it has come from many.
What has Khush Ghulam seen from himself in such a short life?
If you are ignorant, every happiness is the result of your hand.
Why is it not suitable?
Sincerely, Dazad has laughed a lot.
He is ashamed of himself.

Alasira

(Some maqam Sulaiman Neel)

112.right:
The renowned verses of Syed Sahib, bearing the title “... Way,” are well-known.

Examining the description of this book, it appears that the ranks of the officials were also included. And he was elected as Ustad Azam from amongst them. He sought to derive benefit from this form, intending to teach and to rest, and had no desire to remain for long. However, upon arriving in Egypt, he intended to meet with Riyad Pasha, and the Pasha greatly honored him. And the government of Egypt allocated to him a sum of ten thousand rupees. This sum was not to be exchanged for anything. In Afghanistan, such a sum is demanded.

Soon after, at Panjway, a large number of Taliban arrived, and they benefited from their teachings. From them, they began to teach higher sciences, rhetoric, philosophy, natural sciences, the science of ritual, Sufism, and the principles of jurisprudence. They established schools in their own homes, and the people of their area gathered to attend. The scholars, more learned than the people of their area, studied these lessons, and they did not suppress free thought, discernment, or criticism.

The writer, Ashiq Nooh, a Muslim Egyptian named (Karsbandand), has written a passage about Syed Sahib’s time in his published book, “Alderar.” During this time, Egypt was filled with articles published in newspapers, which stirred the thoughts of the common people.

In Pashto:

In these revered elders, everyone is loved and respected.

On this earth, whoever comes, he returns in the same form. The world is only for survival, not for your sake. The great flood will come one day, and all mountains will be humbled before it. Who can escape the flood? This house is being dismantled before the flood. How can one prevent it?  These revered elders are loved and respected. It is not fitting to be complacent and to ignore the world.

(A verse from the poetry of Sultan Nil)

The verses of Syed Sahib are highly esteemed.

The Minister of Iran, Ali Asghar Khan, was stationed at the tomb of Shah Abd al-Azim. He spread falsehoods, saying that he would destroy the property of the common people for the sake of the gain of the corrupt and the deceitful, and the great and pure tomb was tainted by a Armenian. Syed Sahib, in 1890, the Iranian government’s decision to hand over the company of the British to the British Company was a great opportunity for Syed Sahib. Syed Sahib, for the sake of the interests of the Samari Mufti Abdul-Aziz Mirza, he wrote a letter to the people of Afghanistan against the government of Islam and to destroy the property of the common people for the benefit of the corrupt and deceitful.  And he sent his famous letter. In this letter, he said that until now, the gains of the world have been increasing, and the granting of the elections to the people has not been done.  This letter was very effective.

---

It is seen that the government of Iran did not give due regard to his fame and virtue. And Syed Sahib would be forced to lament. The Minister of Iran, Ali Asghar Khan, was stationed at the tomb of Shah Abd al-Azim. He spread falsehoods, saying that he would destroy the property of the common people for the sake of the gain of the corrupt and the deceitful, and the great and pure tomb was tainted by an Armenian. This Armenian, without any other companion, is known as Syed Sahib.

The settlers of the tomb did not even realize it, but they were deceived by the Minister. It is clear that the gain of the corrupt is not possible in a pure tomb. If it is brought back, it is not a Armenian, but a pure one – (absurd, not applicable), but the tomb cannot be restored. The scholars have been buried there.

113.right:
Pashtun
March 1920

Despite the abundance of monetary publications available, Syed Sahib spent a period in Egypt, engaging in discussions with his friends. He subsequently journeyed to India, though his stay proved brief. However, this event is not mentioned directly, and his path led him onward to London. Syed Sahib was received in London with considerable respect, and spent several years engaging in meetings with various individuals.

The British sought to exploit the influence of Muslims in Southern Africa to resolve a dispute, and it is believed they intended to utilize this situation. Until a formal oath was established, it was impossible to fully document these proceedings. During this time, Syed Sahib was present in London, actively publishing works on jurisprudence and ethics, alongside a collection titled “Siya al-Muhanbatin.” He also engaged in debates with the esteemed French scholar, Monsieur Ernest Renan, in a contemporary newspaper.

In 1887, by the decree of the Shah of Persia, Nasir al-Din Shah, Syed Sahib was invited to Iran and subsequently travelled to Tehran, where he was received with great fanfare and honor. However, Syed Sahib’s renown continued to grow day by day, prompting the Shah to seek an explanation for his increasing popularity.  In response, Syed Sahib crafted a letter, and journeyed to Russia, arriving at a time when the Russian authorities summoned him for consultation.  During this meeting, the Shaykh al-Islam of the Muslim population of Russia, Sheikhul Islam, was bestowed with the title of “Grand Mufti.” Syed Sahib responded with a statement affirming his own position as a Muslim scholar.

Subsequently, Syed Sahib returned to Persia, where he encountered his close friend, Sheikh Muhammad Abdo.  The “Urwa al-Wathiq” treatise was published in Persia, representing a powerful and forceful argument for the rights of Muslims. It exerted a significant and often controversial influence throughout the region. Syed Sahib contributed to this publication and simultaneously engaged in discussions with Monsieur Renan, a prominent French scholar, in a contemporary journal.

In 1890, the British East India Company secured a significant concession in Iran, a matter of considerable concern to Syed Sahib.  Motivated by a desire to exploit this opportunity, Syed Sahib penned a strongly worded letter to Mirza Hassan Shirazi, a prominent Samari Mufti, urging him to incite unrest amongst the populace and undermine the interests of the Islamic community.  This letter, filled with inflammatory rhetoric, called for the destruction of property and the impoverishment of the common people, all in service of the company’s aims.  It is said that this letter had a profound impact, causing considerable disruption.

During this period, Syed Sahib was also involved in the conflict between Afghanistan and the British, with Afghan soldiers frequently encountering British garrisons along the river Susa.  Syed Sahib established a base in Egypt, near the Suez Canal, during the period of negotiations between Iran and Russia.  The Russian General Taghavi arrived at this meeting, and a considerable sum of money was exchanged.  Syed Sahib responded with the following statement: “He remains in his place, for he does not abandon himself to anyone.”

Syed Sahib travelled to Persia in the following years to witness the restoration of the monarchy. During this visit, he met with the King’s advisor, Ali Asghar Khan, and was subsequently appointed Governor of the province.  This appointment, however, was met with considerable opposition from the Shah, who sought to undermine Syed Sahib’s influence.  The Shah’s actions were particularly egregious, as he desecrated the tomb of Shah Abd al-Azim, a revered figure in Iranian Islam, and installed an Armenian as a guardian, a deeply offensive act to the local population.  This act, known as “Jamal al-Din Afghan,” is said to have been orchestrated by the Armenian,  Jahangir.

The Governor, Ali Asghar Khan, continued to exert pressure on Syed Sahib, despite the latter’s declining health.  It is said that the Governor, in an attempt to silence Syed Sahib, ordered the closure of his publications and restricted his activities.  This period was marked by considerable hardship for Syed Sahib, who faced increasing opposition from the Iranian government and its allies.  The government’s actions were particularly harsh, and Syed Sahib’s reputation suffered greatly.  The final years of his life were characterized by illness and hardship, and he made arrangements for his own burial, including designating a special mausoleum for himself and several of his Iranian associates.  A story is told of a later incident, in which a manuscript was returned to the government, and Syed Sahib was subsequently referred to a physician.  The physician determined that Syed Sahib was suffering from cancer, but he refused to undergo treatment, and continued to live.  He eventually succumbed to his illness.

Following his death, a large procession of eighty men carried his body to the cemetery near the tomb of the Prophet.

114.right:
There is considerable need in this matter. However, upon receiving an order from Istanbul to seek counsel, Syed Sahib traveled from Basra to London. And upon his arrival in London, the editor of the *Times* newspaper, along with several other individuals, greeted him, and the British Prime Minister himself came to his hotel to meet with him.

Due to the considerable difficulties and complications he faced in Iran, Syed Sahib found it increasingly difficult to publish essays and deliver speeches in London. The Iranian government, in fact, strongly opposed his efforts, and the supporters of reform in Iran and abroad intensified their activities as a result of his dealings. The Iranian government, in any case, effectively halted Syed Sahib’s endeavors in this matter. It is a matter of immense frustration. Beyond these articles and lectures, Syed Sahib was actively working to encourage the supporters of reform in Iran. The clear and obvious effect of this is readily apparent.

In 1890, the Iranian government decided to grant the English Knowledge Company a significant tract of land for Syed Sahib. To capitalize on this opportunity, Syed Sahib dispatched Mirza Hasan Shirazi, a Samaritan Mujtahid, as a traitor against the Iranian government, intending to plunder the wealth of the common people and benefit the enemies of Islam. He sent a famous letter, outlining the belief that the gains achieved thus far guaranteed the right to increase prosperity in the spiritual economy, and that by conceding the exclusive right to the election of Khal, all of Iran would surrender to Europe. This letter, sent to his associates, had a profound impact.

As we observe, the Iranian government failed to respect his renowned and pure reputation. Minister of Iran, Ali Asghar Khan, lectured the residents of the tomb of Shah Abd al-Azim, showering them with lavish gifts and, with falsehoods, claiming that a foreign partner was kept in the great and holy tomb, thereby disturbing its purity. The name of Jamal al-Din Afghani is most familiar here in Armenian. Without Armenian, there is no one else.

The residents initially dismissed his warnings and were filled with doubt, but they insisted upon Ali Asghar Khan’s demands. And they gave advice on the truthfulness of the matter. It is evident that it is impossible to perform a midwife examination in a holy shrine. If you leave Bahi, you are Armenian and not circumcised – (a nonsensical phrase) – but the tomb cannot be entered again. Return the hidden drugs.

At that time, Syed Sahib’s health was also deteriorating. He ceased his discussions with the wealthy and stopped the sacrifices, and the poor, like Dhu Muharram and Mafsad, were chained to the palace of the ruler until they reached the end of the Ottoman period.

Syed Sahib was brought up to Khalika, and there he arrived in Baghdad as a vassal of the Ottomans. And after a brief meeting, he returned to Basra. He remained here for a long time. During this period, Izzat Pasha held the position of governor. He greatly respected Syed Sahib and sent news to Istanbul that there was a very learned man in Basra. What Ottoman sanctity

115.right:
- and a fatwa was issued banning tobacco in Iran -
This caused the government to compensate foreign companies for a significant loss, forcing them to relinquish their rights.
The letter from the Governor of Basra, sent to Istanbul, revealed further details – the government’s own actions, with Syed Sahib placed under the command of the King’s army and granted a grand edifice in Nishan Tash, a lavish display of recognition and honor.  However, this recognition was quickly overshadowed by events.

The Iranian government, seemingly disregarding the esteem previously afforded to Syed Sahib, instead focused on the controversy surrounding the mausoleum of Shah Abdul Azim.  The Minister of War, Ali Asgar Khan, publicly praised the custodians of the mausoleum, spreading false rumors and exploiting the situation to defame the public and enrich himself.  He fabricated claims of Armenian influence within the sacred grounds, disrupting the peaceful sanctity of the site.  This Armenian, known as Jamaluddin Afghani, was, according to Khan, “without Armenian except for himself.”

Despite these attempts to discredit him, Syed Sahib continued to be a figure of considerable influence.  The custodians, under pressure from Minister Khan, refused to allow the return of the sacred relics, effectively sealing the mausoleum and preventing any further veneration.  The relics, carefully preserved, were subsequently retrieved.

During this tumultuous period, Syed Sahib’s own health deteriorated significantly.  He curtailed his public lectures and debates, becoming a prisoner of his own circumstances, chained and dragged from his home to Baghdad, enduring a harsh and degrading existence.  He was placed under the protection of the Governor, Ezat Pasha, who, in Istanbul, lauded Syed Sahib’s erudition and recommended him to the Iranian court.

تاثیر اوکړو - او په ایران کښې د تمباکو د حراموالي یوه فتوه صادره شوه -
دغې په سبب حکومت اجنبی کمپنۍ ته د نقصان غټ بدل ورکړو - او
د حقوقو په بیرته اخستو مجبور شو -
د بصرې د والی هغه خط چه استنبول ته ېې لیږلی وو - هغه څه موره
بی د حکومت ځان ته منه کړو - او سید صاحب د بادشاه د پوځ
را او غوښتنه شوه - او په نشان طاش کښې ورله یو لوئے عمارت خاص
شوه - تنخواه ورله هم او سرله شوه - په دغه ورځ کښې سید صاحب اول
اول ډیر په حق اعتبار او مونږ - مگر د سلطان عبد الحمید خان
په په ضوف ودرکولو سره چه د ایران بادشاه نصرالدین خان
د مرزا حسن خان نوم ېې سری په ذریعه قتل کړو - او عبدالحمید خان
پټل خان او ویدیې - خر چاپیره به ېې په ډیر منبران ګرزیدو -
سید صاحب د ایران د بادشاه د دغې مسلې نه ورستیو کال په
ډیر خراب طرز ژوندون تیر کړو - دغې کال په اخر کښې ېې
جامه کښې و پيدا شوه - د ډیر زیاتوالی په وجه ېې ایډوریشن او کړو -
(دې سیروله) د سیرونو نه در سټو معلومه شوه - چه دائر سرطان
ناچورتیا ده -
سید صاحب د عبد الحمید خان نه د علاج د پاره ویانا ته د تلو
اجازت او غوښتنه - لیکن اجازت ېې ورنکړو - او د پایه تخت مشهور

116.right:
Pashtun

March 1920

Abiyat Pashto from the flower well

I am wounded and burdened with sin,
And I am a fool before the people.
I am clearly situated in the palace,
And each day passes in chastity.
The kings who have come to power,
Bring only emptiness to the courtyard.
I have ordered wine in my heart,
And Asia is filled with its scent.
I believe the meaning of this article is clear,
And the poison has been printed with deceitful methods.
My arm and neck are now weak and frail.
I look down upon others with contempt,
And I will not gaze upon old age.

I am a captive of the people’s plight,
Because I am responsible for this misfortune.
I am neither free nor a prisoner,
I am merely a faded star.
I am impoverished and burdened with the weight of duty,
And I will interpret the good deeds of my life.
Samred is a newcomer to the railway,
And I am neither the first nor the last.
If I am new, I am the right one,
And I am destined to surrender myself to fate.
Che Kari Khan is my minister,
And I despise all people.
My heart is confused and uncertain,
And I buy hay because I eat it.
I am a captive of Afghanistan, a people without honor.

Tap – 31st January 1920

Sister Jalontke is a Pashtun

Today, January 31st, the day of May, the tax day was not observed.
The residents held a meeting in the secretariat of the Caliphate Committee, presided over by Mr. Malik Sher Zaman Khan Sahib.

—

The meeting was lively and full of discussion,
With residents gathered in one or two places in Nal – but no Pashto was heard,
As if the attendees were secluded in their homes.  It was a grand and important meeting, and the complaints about the rally were evident.
Maulavi Habibullah Saheb, the leader of the meeting, presented Mr. Malik Sher Zaman Khan as the chairman. Khan was unanimously approved, and the meeting commenced.
Mr. President: Addressing the audience, he declared that the Pashtun people must unite, overcome discord, and be free from ignorance.
He spoke of the Khilafat Committee, the Congress, and the declaration of independence on December 31st, and then discussed the past and present conditions of the British, their salt-headedness and the prohibition of salt.
He explained the reasons for protesting against these injustices.

—

Sir Hilton Grant, Chief Commissioner of the Mobah Frontier, had sent a representative, Mr. Hanna Pule, to study Pashto in London.
He informed the audience of the British people’s disregard for the people of Hindustan, their failure to uphold the law, and the imposition of punitive fines on the residents.
He presented the reasons for protesting against these injustices.

117.right:
Pashtun

And the speech concluded. 

Then Maulvi Habib Gul confirmed the President’s speech. And in his address within the Diwan, Sir Hamilton presented a favorable account of Namak Harame’s quality. And the government is working diligently to construct nine new buildings, utilizing materials of very modest value – those acquired in recent times. We express our profound dissatisfaction with this practice.  The following resolutions were unanimously approved:

First, Sir Hamilton’s disrespectful treatment of the Pashtun people in London is noted. The British, valuing India, would not have permitted such a thing in the past. At that time, these Pashtuns were driven to despair. And from Peshawar to Kalkutta, not a single penny or grain of rice passed through their hands. The attendees of the meeting were deeply displeased by this unfortunate development, and Sir Hamilton views this recent action with considerable contempt.

Second, we, the residents of Tehel, are deeply aggrieved by the government’s harsh levies and unjust fines imposed upon the impoverished populace. Specifically, the government has mandated a tax of 585 rupees for the landholders’ produce and grain, a sum far exceeding the value dictated by the Ost Five-Year Plan. We are astonished by the government’s imposition of this exorbitant fee. 

Furthermore, we, the residents of Tehel, where the majority of the population are landowners, are profoundly dissatisfied with the government’s arbitrary orders and discriminatory practices. 

The following resolutions were unanimously approved:

Firstly, regarding Sir Hamilton’s alleged insults of Pashtuns in London – it is known that the British value India.  Therefore, in the past, Pashtuns would have been driven to ruin. And from Peshawar to Kalkutta, not a single penny or grain of rice would pass through their hands. This deeply troubled the permanent residents and attendees of the meeting, and Sir Hamilton views this recent action with considerable contempt.

Secondly, we, the residents of Tehel, are suffering from the harsh measures imposed by the government, unjust fines levied upon the poor, and the washing away of the rights of the deserving. We vehemently demand that the government release Zarbiana from this heinous crime.

Finally, we, the residents of Tehel, where the majority are landowners, are deeply troubled by the government’s arbitrary orders and discriminatory practices.

118.right:
Pashtun

Pashtun is a publication reaching throughout the world.
Pashtun seeks out the locales of esteemed individuals.
Pashtun presents and hears both the male and female verses and poetry of Pashto.
Pashtun is the very heart, head, and eyes of Pashtun culture.
Pashtun is recognized throughout the world as a Pashtun.
Advertising within Pashtun is not considered frivolous, nor is it disregarded.

Advertised Prices

Place Size: Once, Three Times, Six Times
Verse: 6, 12, 20
Half: 6, 12, 20
Line: 4, 8, 15

The Price of Pashtun

For health: ... ... ... ... four rupees
For six months: ... ... ... two rupees and two annas
For the year’s students: ... two and a half annas
“Six months”: ... one and a half annas
For foreign nations: five annas per year

A Word of Prayer

City, always
February 3rd
The esteemed drivers of Pashtun

Peace be upon you and the blessings of God.
Today, I offer you a modest donation of ten rupees.
The particulars are as follows:
(1) Four rupees for the annual festival,
(2) Two and a half rupees for one year, belonging to Abdul Malik Khan, student of the Tut Mosque, located at Kujar Garhai,
(3) Three and a half rupees for the support of the Pashtun magazine.
It is our sincere submission that this Pashtun magazine serves as a noble and significant contribution to the Pashtun people, and it is a matter of undeniable truth that all Pashtuns, both male and female, greatly appreciate it and express their gratitude.
Therefore, I humbly request that the tribal leaders of the region lend their support to this magazine and provide it with a weekly newspaper. This would undoubtedly bring considerable benefit to the entire community. May God bless you.
Then, greetings from Gulzada Din.

پښتون د لويو لويو خلقو ځايه ته ځي.
پښتون د پښتو نرينه او زنانه پوښتتان لولي، او اوري.
پښتون د پښتو د زړه سر او سترګو نور دی.
پښتون د دنيا هر ګوټ کې پښتون پيژني.

د پاچا خان تقریر
خوږو او درنو! تاسو ته دا معلومه ده چې د انګریز د استعماري نظام له لاسه زمونږ په وطن کې څه حال دی. هغوی زمونږ په شتمنیو باندې قبضه کړې ده او زمونږ خلک په فقر او بدبختۍ کې ژوند کوي. زما د زړه غوښتنه دا ده چې باید مونږ یو موټی شو او ددې ظلم په وړاندې مبارزه وکړو. هغوی په هر ډول وسایلو سره هڅه کوي چې زمونږ په منځ کې تفرقه واچوي. خو مونږ باید پوه شو چې زمونږ ګټه په اتفاق کې ده. که مونږ سره یو نه شو، نو هغوی به بیا هم زمونږ په برخلیک لوبې کوي. زه نن په تاسو غږ کوم چې راځئ د خپل وطن د آزادۍ لپاره له یو بل سره لاسونه ورکړو. زمونږ په تیرو کلونو کې ډیرې قربانۍ ورکړې دي، خو دا قربانۍ هغه وخت ارزښت لري چې مونږ خپل هدف ته ورسیږو. ددې لپاره باید مونږ په خپلو ځانونو باور ولرو او د خپلو حقونو لپاره ودریږو. دا مبارزه اسانه نه ده، خو که مونږ د حق په لاره کې ثابت قدم پاتې شو، نو بریالیتوب یقیني دی.

پښتون ته د خدمتګاري په اړه:
کوم کسان چې په میدان د ذکر شوي پوهه لري، هغوی ته په کار ده چې په دې اړه نورو ته هم پوهاوی ورکړي. څرنګه چې په تیرو ورځو کې د دښمن د مخنیوي لپاره مونږ هڅې کړي، ددې لپاره دا ضروري ده چې خپلې کړنې د وطن د آبادۍ لپاره تر سره کړو. نن ورځ زمونږ وطن د پرمختګ لپاره اړتیا لري چې هر یو تن د خپلې وسې مطابق کار وکړي. د انګریزانو د حکومت له لوري په پښتونخوا کې کوم اقدامات شوي دي، هغه د ولس د حقونو د ضایع کولو لپاره دي. باید چې موږ خپل ځانونه د داسې دسیسو څخه وساتو او د خپلو ملي ګټو لپاره یو لاس شو.

خلک د هغه ته ذکر ده،

(د فضل رحیم صاحب په دور کې)

120.right:
Pashtun
Volume 2
June 1939
Pacha Khan’s Speech

Esteemed and honorable! You are surely aware of the state of our land under the yoke of the British colonial regime. They have seized our resources and our people endure a life of poverty and hardship. It is my heartfelt desire that we unite and rise up against this injustice. They relentlessly seek to sow discord amongst us, employing every means at their disposal. However, we must recognize that our collective strength lies in unity. Should we remain divided, they will continue to manipulate our fate. I call upon you today to join together for the liberation of our homeland. We have borne immense sacrifices in past years, and these sacrifices will only be meaningful when we achieve our goal. Therefore, we must have faith in ourselves and assert our rights. This struggle is not without difficulty, but with steadfastness on the path of justice, victory is assured.

Regarding service to Pashtunistan:
Those who possess the knowledge discussed in this matter should endeavor to inform others about it. As we strived in the past to prevent the enemy, it is now essential that our actions be directed towards the betterment of our homeland. Today, our country requires the participation of every individual, each contributing according to their capabilities. The actions taken by the British government in Pakhtunkhwa are designed to undermine the rights of the people. We must safeguard ourselves from such machinations and unite for the sake of our national interests.

Husband is his field,
Husband is his field,
The creation is his remembrance,
People remember him,
The world is his domain,
Alamdad that domain.
(During the time of Fazl Rahim Sahib)

Keeping these points in mind:
Those who possess the knowledge discussed in this matter should endeavor to inform others about it. As we strived in the past to prevent the enemy, it is now essential that our actions be directed towards the betterment of our homeland. Today, our country requires the participation of every individual, each contributing according to their capabilities. The actions taken by the British government in Pakhtunkhwa are designed to undermine the rights of the people. We must safeguard ourselves from such machinations and unite for the sake of our national interests.

Husband is his field,
Husband is his field,
The creation is his remembrance,
People remember him,
The world is his domain,
Alamdad that domain.
(During the time of Fazl Rahim Sahib)

121.right:
Khan Ji Sher Lupo, this tree was not embraced by the Farangis. The Pashtun child is steadfast and his head is bowed low. He established this for this reason. Let us unite as Pashtuns and consider Khalil Mohmand, Afoyed Khatak, Yusuf zi, and Gholji as Pashtuns.

Now, we offer this service with great sincerity. If you are not fully aware of your own service, we are unaware of the great foundations of this service for the Pashtun people and its propagation. So, anyone who works in this manner, if everyone finds agreement in Pashtun, that work is a true service to God. And anyone who does such a thing in Pashtun, creating division and hypocrisy, that work is a new service to God. Let the sword be placed upon the land and done. Today, some people have begun to undertake a work called self-service at the backs of others. Are you a self-serving scholar at their feet? I do not ask you when the Pashtun civil question arose, and it is a self-serving endeavor. And if you can undertake this self-serving work? May God forgive your impatience. Hatred and animosity create love and affection in the tribes of the Pashtun region. Remember our love for one another, love, war, and a nine-day affair. Knowledge creates hatred and malice. So you should know that it creates hatred and malice in all people. And in the face of violence, there is a love of bloodshed. And if he does not know what to do, he will do it. So you can grow your love.

You ask, “What happened to the poor?” Put them in your car and do justice. Do not forget the poor, the poor, and the poor in any past state. It turns out it is not forgotten. All this misunderstanding is the source of speech. The whole government is ours, and the desolate are in the hands of the country. But the truth is not so. Think about it. The same laws are still in force in the Pashto country. There are still Frankincense pigs here. Most of the other ideas are in the hands of the Farangis. The suspension of a Farangi is a farce. Their messengers will not come to you. We gather on a hill; it is up to you. Deer and dog. So do not return again. Your ministers no longer have this power. Clear the land from the people and from it.

Let us remember these verses: Let us remember these verses: Let us remember these verses: Anyone who possesses this knowledge in the field should inform others about it. As we made efforts in the past to prevent the enemy, it is necessary to carry out our actions for the betterment of the country. Today, our country needs progress, and each person must work according to their ability. The actions taken by the government of the British in Pashtunkhwa are for the destruction of the rights of the people. We must protect ourselves from such conspiracies and be united for the interests of our nation.

The owner of the land is the owner of the land,
The owner of the land is the owner of the land,
The creation is the remembrance of the Lord,

Khan is the owner of the land.

122.right:
Pashtun Amani
Allergen 1939

To do whatever is right, it is a good endeavor. As much as
you believe it to be so. In the world, there are few things so valuable as Makar,
and only if we are free. We in Russia, once held those who possessed Makar in affection,
and provided for them generously. They are now present everywhere, and
establish factories and homes. We cannot, however, build such workshops for you,
until we possess the authority over the country and its treasury.

In the end, I must tell you this: it is not what you desire. Each time I work,
I serve you with humility. I do whatever I can for God,
and today, many of you are served by Him. The servant of God is not aware of the truth,
nor should you be. And do not stray from the righteous path. All of you,
you are landowners. You must devote yourselves to learning under the guidance of the
great scholars. But do not conceal your poverty. You have found it hidden within yourselves.
You must believe that nothing is more important than life, and that you will never bleed.
I swear an eternal oath. Khan is the master, God is the owner.
Do not seek to be a servant of God, but rather a servant of the people,
like the houses of the wealthy, where people rest and enjoy comfort and prosperity.
We ask you, the people, to be like this. We have no hope,
that you will inform the drunkards, or that they will harm your sisters and wives more than
they already do. Where is your honor?  Do not be ashamed. In the end, I thank you all
for this. And so, listen to us in silence. I pray to God that you may receive healing
from both this and the next world.

Today’s world
We will always strive for the good of the nations on “New World.”
Think of it often. Each month brings its own hardships. It also has its own blemishes.
May God bless you, Abiullah Khan. Think of everything. This means that
Mufti Abad will be built freely, as long as you are in the hands of the owner and the treasury,
and the Salar of Khan.  Hashtankar did not go to Punjab either. This is good news.

123.right:
Pashtun Amateur 1929

It seems hearts yearn for reunion, and now they will gather in earnest.
We shall set aside our pride – and not indulge in Pashtun rivalry – dismissing idle speculation and unfounded rumors as mere fancy. The service of our Pashtun brethren is a source of such profound contentment, that no other joy can compare. Indeed, who else has achieved such a noble and fulfilling endeavor? And what is the Pashtun people to do, under the leadership of Mr. Kharafistan, whose judgment is so often questioned?

The owner, the merchant, the wealthy financier – in this modern world, Islam is often overshadowed by the pursuits of materialism. The laborer, the dependent, and the impoverished find themselves increasingly entangled in such affairs. The meetings of farmers and laborers are often brief, and fraught with difficulty.  Many people are impoverished by the waters, and oppressed by the chiefs and other powerful figures, until they are reduced to destitution and exile.  This is a lamentable state of affairs, a stain upon the honor of the Pashtun people, who are said to be free from the shackles of Satan. Is this truly the case? Such contradictions abound.  There are always those who seek to exploit the goodwill of others, to profit from the misfortunes of the many.

Pashtun Amateur 1929

Let it be remembered that the earth is finite, and the people desire the unity of their nation.  They pursue this goal with unwavering determination, for to associate with such treachery is to invite ruin.  Let no one presume to share in the wealth of another, for that is a path to disgrace.  The wealthy should be mindful of their position, and the poor should strive to improve their lot.  The esteemed Khan Khitab, a resident of the Chah Dera, has been under house arrest for years.  The fate of the esteemed Jamal, a resident of the Chah Dera, is also uncertain.  How many have been driven from their homes, and forced to wander the land in search of sustenance?  Their grief is profound.  They have no shelter, no protection, and no hope.  The people of the land lament their plight.  Their service is always for the good of the nation.  It is known that Khan, the wise and just, has always sought the welfare of his people.  But now, the enemy rejoices.  He revels in the misfortunes of others.  May God guide him to repentance, and grant him wisdom.  Let the people be mindful of their duty, and not be deceived by appearances.  The world is fleeting.  In it, there are those who seek to wage war with words, rather than with arms.  Let us not forget the lessons of the past.  Let us not be swayed by vanity.  Let us remember that the Pashtun people are a nation of warriors, and that their strength lies in unity and courage.  Let us cherish our heritage, and protect it from corruption.  Let us not be divided by petty disputes.  Let us be generous to our neighbors, and compassionate to the poor.  Let us always strive to uphold the honor of our nation.

124.right:
Pashtun Atmanzai 12 Al Arjun 39

If we still find ourselves wandering in the shadows, truly, who will grant us such a heart? Do we turn to God and seek His guidance? Alas, this is the established order – to align oneself with the Divine, to seek refuge in the sacred. Let your nation be united within its borders, free from all division. This is the very essence of your being, the core of your identity, visible in Pashto wherever it may appear.

But tell me, what news has reached your ears? What is it that brings the enemy joy? Let us, as servants of Allah, Ubaidullah Khan and his companions, begin a work that is worthy of their esteem. Let us settle in a place where we can be at peace. Ubaidullah Khan and his friends, those who consider themselves to be on the path of freedom, will consider them to be so. We will be proud of ourselves for the first time. The people say that Pashtuns are devils. And seeing this, do the rulers of the land consider themselves to be righteous? Such things happen. There are always such people on both sides. Let your eyes be vigilant, lest the army be caught unawares. But always prioritize the welfare of your own people over the welfare of others, for their existence is intertwined with their own. Like us, Pashtuns are sometimes hampered in their ability to exercise their authority. Let them remain in their own way. Today we have come to the house that yesterday was occupied by the remnants of the Chad. The remnants of Chad’s attacks still linger on the horizon. May our hope be realized.

May you remember this: no one can escape death, and the lives of the people are fleeting. Khan desires the unity of the Pashtun nation. He wishes for their prosperity, so that they may not be subjected to the humiliation of being driven from their land. Let them not be driven out into the wilderness, nor be subjected to the contempt of strangers and foreign lands. May they not be robbed of their possessions and their honor. May their children not be taken away. Pashtun Atmanzai 139 Al Arjun

Let it be decided – no one should be compelled to fight against the Pashtun people, nor should anyone be forced to engage in rebellion.  (From the memorandum of Mr. Abdul Karim Khan, Director of the Free School)

May peace be upon you, esteemed Mr. Naali.  Let us not require the celebrated Pashto newspaper, ‘The Afghan Dawn,’ for its publications. The sky over Afghanistan is a vast expanse, a single star among many.  Other nations strive to reach the heights of this land, but they do not make the effort. Now they publish this Afghanistan as a place of threat, a fortress of defiance. And the poet, Khushal Khan Khattak, has remembered this. He has written a beautiful verse – a light illuminating the scenes of the garden, a reflection of the moon in the waters. The first part depicts the beauty of the garden and the splendor of the landscape. The second part is a Pashto poem by Khushal Khan Khattak, expressing the pain of the Pashtun people and reminding them of their homeland. This book, with its writing and printing, is a great achievement.

May you be honored and respected – a beloved of the nation, Mr. Habib.

125.right:
The United Nations and Myths

Jawaharlal Nehru penned some reflections on the philosophy of non-violence: “To achieve a high and noble goal, one must endure hardship, enter the field unarmed, remain steadfast in the face of an armed enemy, and never abandon the field. Furthermore, the spirit of conflict transcends the earthly, and in its truest form, surpasses mere human ambition.” He stated that this was a profound and difficult undertaking, a path that should not be pursued lightly.  It was a death unlike any other, a sacrifice undertaken for the sake of a greater purpose.

He sought a meeting with the Khan, urging him to endure suffering and self-destruction in the pursuit of peace – a life not meant for mortal men to fulfill.  This was not merely a philosophical ideal, but a profound commitment to a noble cause.  The contrast between the Khan’s personal comfort and the demands of justice was stark.

Pashtun Atmanzi

A considerable number of individuals have reached a firm decision – no longer will the mother’s foot be placed upon the other, there is nothing to be gained from it.  Is it a question for the Muhand tribe?  Let us hope for a favorable response and thoughtful consideration – may they succeed in their endeavors and fulfill their aspirations.  Let us pray for their success.

(From the writings of Mr. Abdul Karim Khan Sahib, Master of the Master’s Azad School)

A Plea for Unity

Come, esteemed Mr. Wala Naali, and Mr. Chalo – let us welcome the renowned national Pashto newspaper, ‘Talwa Afghan,’ into your home. The sky above Afghanistan is a dark star, and the efforts of some of its people continue tirelessly towards independence.  Now, they have published a book, a clandestine declaration of Afghanistan’s desire for freedom, and the esteemed Pashtun national poet, Khushal Khan Khattak, has been commemorated. It is a valuable and evocative poem, recreating scenes from the gardens of the past, the colonial era, and the melodious words of the Bulbul’s Andalib.  Furthermore, the second part of the work engages in dialogue with Khushal Khan Khattak’s poignant Pashto poetry, advising the Pashtun people to lead their nation with wisdom and integrity.  The act of writing and publishing such a work is a truly commendable undertaking.

My dear friend – Habibi, your achievements surpass this.

126.right:
Pashtun Atmanzi

The great goal is spent in the pursuit of achievement.
Jawaharlal Nehru’s statement is laden with both truth and falsehood.
A divergence exists between the religious and non-religious sentiments of the Pashtun and the Rohani [spiritual] communities.
The believer, driven by his faith, seeks little satisfaction or happiness, and does not recognize the possibility of death as a joyful event.

Regarding the work undertaken by India, we, as representatives of the Pashtun nation, find no difference between ourselves and Khan, in matters of Hindustan and the liberation of the nation. We have witnessed the destruction of the British army and the suffering endured by the people.

The passionate hearts of the lovers are now turned towards the path of non-violence, as urged by Nehru. Let each individual, independently of their own accord, pursue this path.  The people of Ladafa [likely referring to a specific region or tribe] are not easily swayed, and the influence of these unruly elements extends far beyond the borders of Calicut, reaching as far as the Persian plains, where they inflict cruelties and injustices upon the populace, and destroy the means of their livelihood.

Pashtun Atmanzi

Khan and his companions strive to preserve their lives.
This education, of a religious nature, is imparted to those people in a highly formalized manner. Those who understand the meaning of Islam in the traditional way are deeply rooted in it.
Let the Pashtun people, renowned for their Pashtun pride and honor, be known throughout the world. For a long period, the Pashtun tribe has been the leading force in the affairs of this land, guiding its people.
The writing and printing of this book are a great blessing.

May my self be honored – as is fitting for Habibullah Khan.

127.right:
Regarding the advancement of civilization and education, a critical necessity for the Pashtun people and their own well-being is the establishment of a nationwide system of education – one that provides a comprehensive and standardized learning experience for all citizens. This represents a path whereby every individual within the nation can secure their rightful entitlements. To underscore the significance of education, concerted efforts must be directed toward the creation of a curriculum designed to cultivate the intellectual capacities of the populace. This endeavor should be driven by a commitment to national progress, not merely the interests of a select segment of society. The existing system, as it stands, prioritizes the concerns of a particular class, thereby excluding a significant portion of our community from the benefits of education. We must strive to align education with the needs of the people, ensuring that every citizen can reap the rewards of learning within their own nation. Despite any obstacles encountered, we must persevere toward a prosperous future, harnessing the power of knowledge to propel the advancement of our country. Our objective should extend beyond the mere construction of schools; rather, we must foster an environment where every individual possesses equitable opportunities for learning. Through this, every member of our society will contribute to the nation’s development, forging a stronger and more resilient community.

A concise note on the paramount importance of fostering true civilization and education:

Fundamentally, national education serves as a vital step towards the collective good and the enhancement of society. Regrettably, the current system of education falls short in fulfilling its potential, leaving the people of the nation deprived of their inherent rights. Consequently, a transformative shift is required within the realm of education – one that unlocks the doors of knowledge for every citizen. The prevailing educational framework caters to a limited group, whereas education, as a fundamental social right, must be accessible to all. To facilitate the expansion of education, the government must enact policies that consider the needs of the populace and implement concrete measures to advance the nation’s progress. In this manner, the people of our society will not only be illuminated by the light of knowledge but will also flourish on the global stage as a robust and progressive community. It is the responsibility of every Pashtun to champion the dissemination of education and advocate for this crucial undertaking.

128.right:
Pashtun Agmanzi 20
The truth regarding civilization and education is a vital necessity for the Pashtun people and for the demonstration of Pashtun honor, requiring steps to establish a comprehensive and standardized educational system throughout the country. This is a path by which every citizen of the nation can secure their rightful share within it.  For the importance of this education, it is essential to strive for the creation of a curriculum that elevates the mental faculties of the people, with the aim of promoting science and knowledge for the advancement of the homeland, not merely for a specific class. Currently, the existing system caters primarily to the interests of a particular class, leaving many members of our society deprived of education. We must endeavor to align education with the needs of the people, allowing every individual to benefit from the advantages of education within our country.  Even if numerous obstacles exist along this path, we must continue forward towards a bright future, working in the light of knowledge for the progress of our homeland. Our goal should not simply be the construction of schools, but the creation of an environment in which every individual has an equal opportunity for learning.  Through this, every member of our society will contribute to the progress of the nation and a stronger community will be established.

A brief note on the importance of truth, civilization, and education:

In truth, the education of the homeland is a vital step for the common good and the honor of the community. Unfortunately, the current system regarding the benefits of education suffers from a number of deficiencies, leaving the people of the homeland deprived of their rightful entitlement.  A significant change is needed in the benefits of education to open the doors of knowledge for every citizen of the country.  The current education is limited to a specific group, while education, as a social right, must be accessible to all.  To expand education, it is necessary for the government to enact policies that take into account the needs of the people and to take practical steps for the advancement of the homeland. In this way, our people will not only be illuminated by knowledge but will also stand among the nations as a strong and advanced community. It is the duty of every Pashtun to strive to make education accessible and to raise their voice for this important matter.

129.right:
History of the Pashtuns

Mirwais informed Badshah that this matter was of paramount importance and fraught with peril. Should Russia truly cease its aggression against us, a peaceful embassy would be dispatched, offering no new grievances to the Armenian Christian. As you are aware, the Armenian Christian adheres to a distinct branch of Christianity, and this lineage does not stem from the Russian imperial family. The Tsar’s own decree further indicates that Israel Udayani is a descendant of the late Khawar Shah of the Iranian dynasty – a matter that should not provoke contention. However, should rebellion arise – particularly concerning Gurjin Khan’s pronouncements that he is a descendant of St. Peter – it would be a grave offense. Gurjin Khan, as you know, is associated with a Christian family. For this reason, it is not surprising that this Armenian Christian has been sent to Iran for the benefit of Russia. Consequently, Isfahan will be the destination for him, and Gurjin Khan’s authority will be maintained over Kandahar, Charchia, and Kerman, in opposition to the existing arrangements. This act demonstrates a clear adherence to moral principles. Mirwais’s words and trust engendered a significant wave of doubt within the King’s heart, and his pronouncements were met with unwavering conviction that Gurjin Khan’s influence extended far beyond the boundaries of his immediate domain. Yet, at this juncture, Gurjin Khan’s power has become excessively burdensome to the Pashtun people, representing a single, oppressive force against their collective will. They yearn for liberation from this tyranny, and are prepared to sacrifice all in pursuit of it. However, the means to achieve this are limited, and God Almighty will ultimately fulfill their aspirations.

130.right:
Here's the improved English translation of the current spread, aiming for fluency, coherence, faithfulness, and an appropriate early 20th-century register:

“It is certain that this matter is of great importance and peril, and there is no doubt that the affair with Mirvais Badshah is of utmost significance. If Russia does not truly destroy us, then this peaceful embassy will be established. And the work of this embassy will not bring forth any new information for the Armenian Christian. You are aware that the Armenian Christian is a member of the Armenian faith, a distinct form of Christianity. And this lineage does not stem from the royal family of the Czar. Similarly, the Tsar holds that the Israelites of Judea are descendants of the Persian Empire’s Shah, and this is a matter that will not be questioned. However, unless this is addressed with prudence and without inciting rebellion – concerning the words of Gurgin Khan, who is said to be akin to a Saint Peter, a figure of immense veneration – and the rumors that he is considered a sacred being. And the Tsar holds that Gurgin Khan is related to a Christian family.  Therefore, this matter is not surprising, especially if the Armenian Christian is sent to Iran for the benefit of the Russian Tsar. Indeed, at the same time, the Shah of Persia and Gurgin Khan of Qandahar, Charchai, and Kerman all hold claims against him. And this is a matter of clear evidence, a testament to the truth. Mirvais’ words and his trust in the Badshah have created a great flood of doubt in the heart of the King, and his words are a firm conviction that – Gurgin Khan possesses a considerable amount of land. But the time has come for Gurgin Khan’s power to be excessive.  Yet, Mirvais believes that Gurgin Khan will deny this, and he anticipates that such an action will immediately trigger a rebellion. On the other hand, there is no Russian army, nor will there be a Russian embassy in Kabul. Therefore, it is a matter of great concern to Mirvais, a five-fold covenant with the Badshah.  A visit to Qandahar, a joyous occasion. And before Gurgin Khan’s judgment, the figure of Angin Kzori emerged.  Therefore, Mirvais was immediately dispatched to Qandahar, and thereafter he was instructed to remain steadfast in the interests of Gurgin Khan and to act in accordance with his wishes and deeds. Mirvais sought no further requests from him, for he had already arrived in Qandahar with the rulers of کرمان and Dostan.  On his journey, he engaged in conflict with the tribes of all Pashtuns, and he made promises of peace and accord.  Mirvais possessed within him the qualities of his people – his loyalty, his skill in warfare, and his talent for diplomacy.  Therefore, his actions would not be ignored, and his endeavors would not succeed, and at this time, he is a wronged and oppressed man, with a sincere desire to liberate his people from injustice. May God grant him abundant prosperity.  Indeed, when he arrived in Qandahar, he observed that Gurgin Khan’s excessive tyranny had surpassed all limits, and the entire Pashtun population was united in their opposition to his oppression.  There was a longing among them to liberate themselves from this tyrant, and they were prepared to offer any sacrifice to achieve this.  It was necessary to take action, and may God fulfill their desires.  The principle of this action is that every ruler in the world should be both merciful and stern.  Do not raise your hand against the poor – for this is a path to impiety, and it corrupts the faith.  Let the officials be just, let the Muslim servants be free from bondage – and so on.  Let not the treasury be emptied, let debts be settled, let the foundations of justice be restored – let not the merchants be overly generous.  It is not surprising, then.  And the field is prepared for the rise of power – every government that acts in this manner will prosper (again). (From the writings of Syed Rasul Sahib, D. I. B. C. B. D. I. D. R. I. S. H. N.)

131.right:
Pashtun Amanzi 22

It is simply this: every ruler, regardless of place, holds authority over all. Atlantic
A great deal of confusion arises from this. Forget it, forget it; the official decrees are inscribed upon the Quran, listen closely, and let your hearts be cleansed.  Let the official orders be carried out.  A peaceful state is established for the Muslims – let there be no division, no strife, and in such a state, laws are enforced. Debts are collected. Liabilities are settled.  Thus, the foundation is laid – the spirit of generosity is revived, and generosity flourished once more – it is no surprise. And the field is prepared for the coming of the army – every government that acts in this manner lays the groundwork (furthermore) (from the writings of Mian Sad Rasul Sahib, A. B. C. B.)

To escape such injustice, one must be steadfast.
The love of the country is essential for the Afghan elite. The passionate martyrs of the country must be supported, let us build caravanserais that are beautiful and luxurious, not for the young, but for the weary and disheartened soldiers.  They were not aware, nor did they understand.  The wise are saddened, and their hearts are filled with sorrow.  Let not their hearts be overwhelmed by the desire for return.  Let them not seek refuge in the fleeting pleasures of the world, nor be consumed by the thought of death.  Rather, let them contemplate the eternal.  It is not a strange thing.  And the path to prosperity is opened – every government that acts in this way lays the groundwork (again).

133.right:
Pashtun; Tammany 26 Allergen 1929

Mirwais brought forth Elpo – Mirwais, accompanied by a richly adorned litter, laden with gifts and bribes, to present a decree from his office to Gurgen Khan.  Initially, Gurgen had granted time for the interests and needs of the people, a pledge we had consistently made to Mirwais.  Let us observe God’s work, and fulfill our agreement, safeguarding our honor and reputation. However, upon learning that the first prisoner, a man of considerable consequence, had been dispatched to Isfahan, and subsequently returned from Persia, a shadow of doubt fell upon Mirwais’s mind.  He became convinced that Gurgen Khan’s power was waning, and that the Gurgen Khanate was in peril.  Russia, recognizing that the Afghan Sultan could not be easily subdued, sought alliances with the Persian Empire and devised schemes for negotiation.  Gurgen, aware of his own vulnerability, would not be subjected to humiliation, nor would he endure further losses of men and resources.  Yet, this was due to the fact that a small contingent had been dispatched to secure the fortress of Charbagh, and one of Gurgen Khan’s loyal men was stationed within the formidable walls of the Russian outpost in Peshwar.  Furthermore, a family of Christian descent held ties to a non-Christian family, and this was the root of the matter.
133.left:
Pashtun; Tammany 27 Allergen 1929

A Weary Reflection

It was conceived that Mirwais had amassed considerable wealth and riches in preparation for a conflict with the Gurgen Khanate. He presented a lavish display of treasures and bribes to Gurgen Khan, demonstrating the authority of his office.  Gurgen, recognizing the needs and desires of his people, had previously pledged his support to Mirwais.  Let us contemplate God’s deeds – let us fulfill our agreement and preserve our honor and dignity.  However, when the esteemed First Governor was dispatched to Isfahan, in the course of attending to the affairs of our own people, we realized that we must consider the concerns of the powerful Gurgen Khan, and his harsh restrictions.  Remembering the time when the burden of the treasury was sent back to Iran, it was a loss of a lifetime.  Therefore, we must refrain from rebellion.  Yet, considering the plight of a single, yet influential, individual, and the potential consequences for the stability of the region, we must carefully weigh our options.  Mirwais’s birthday celebrations were a spectacle of great renown throughout the region, a symbol of life and struggle for the Pashtun people, akin to the legendary Lani Khana and Gurun.

Fakriye

The opinion of the Ana Dar region is that if these strong Afghans were to travel to the distant lands of Qalanderi, they would print this text on the paper of the new year, and it would be read with great interest.  It would be a valuable resource for the learned and the wise.  Let us not forget the teachings of the great Sufi scholar, Khushal Khan Khattak.  He was a master of eloquence and a renowned poet.  He traveled extensively, witnessing the hardships of the world and gaining experience.  He is skilled in managing affairs and a teacher of ethics.  The only difference is that he has spent much of his life in Iran, and Khushal Khan Khattak has been a prominent figure in the mountains of the Pashtun people.

---

134.right:
Khushal Khan Khattak

Now it is evident that Khushal Khan, a renowned Sufi, skilled in poetry and possessing a deep understanding of the arts, is recognized. As Maula Narum Sahib noted, he has ascended to a position of esteem.

He speaks of separation with sorrow, lamenting the loss and expressing his discontent.

He possesses a keen insight and understands the nuances of love, as evidenced by a rubai, reminiscent of the joyous and melancholic spirit of a dervish. The fleeting nature of time prompts reflection, and the memory of a beloved serves as a poignant reminder. He draws wisdom from the teachings of Sheikh Saadi, applying them to the needs of his followers. He has traveled extensively, gaining experience and a practical understanding of the world. His character embodies a blend of morality and wisdom, a rare quality. The only difference between him and Saadi is the price – a reflection on the cost of knowledge and experience.

135.right:
Pashtun Atmanzi 11 June 1939

And who is this man?
He is a man of courage and blessing, yet still young.
A promise is a promise, not a falsehood, not a deception, nor a trick.
He speaks with richness and acts with dignity, and in silence, his voice is like that of a wealthy man.

He is not a man consumed by hunger, nor prone to excessive anger.
He is a man of honor, and the pain of my honor is the enemy.
If a man possesses honor and respect, he is a reliable servant.
He walks with confidence, unafraid to climb upon the roof.

Who is Pashtun Khan and Pashtun youth?
He is one who finds joy, and who, despite the darkness of his youth, is a servant of the poor.
He pays his dues with diligence, and is thus free – free of his own labor.
There is nothing more valuable than freedom, a clean earth beneath one’s feet.

The Pashtun speaks with a heart full of wisdom.
The Pashtun is a man, but he is not like others.
There is no wisdom, no intelligence; every corner is filled with corruption.
He remembers this constantly, and thus suffers misfortune.
If God is willing, if one is happy and free, then…

Pashtun Atmanzi 11 June 1939

The youth are wise and learn from the butcher’s sons.
In the shadow of the Mughals, they seek the desires of the Mughal nobles.
The camel ran to Panchel’s house, seeking refuge in the middle of the cave.
He is worthy of praise, a man of high standing and noble lineage.

Khushal Khan Khattak, in the name of the Pashtun people, in the name of Javid.
This translation of the above quatrain is presented for your consideration.
Cherish this esteemed man, and let his memory be preserved with care.
The strength of will is managed from above, and the desires of the heart are fulfilled.

The Pashtun’s ignorance and greed – now, in the midst of greed and the pursuit of wealth,
the Pashtun’s ignorance is boundless.  No longer seeking the pleasures of greed.
If I speak of the Pashtun, he is like a mountain king, a lion.
The Pashtun’s worm, Jodha, and the failure of effort were dry.
Ken was filled with sorrow.
If you do not see the death of three and a half times, what is wrong with the training of the brother?

Khushal Khan’s language
Khushal was responsible for the advancement of Pashto, and he understood that the fate of his people rested upon the strength of their narratives.
He entrusted the strong hadiths and sought to unite them.

Pashtun Celebration

What more is said that this is within the Pashto… (incomplete)

Khushal Khan and the Mughal Conflict

Following the acquisition of full lands – we ascertained through correspondence that Khushal Khan Khattak began his poetic pursuits driven by a passion for his homeland – addressed.

Inh-Kin – Regarding the significance of Pashto and the Mughals, particularly the hostility between them, it was sought to establish – and Khushal Khan’s stance against the Mughals.

It could not be fully grasped, as the Mughal government was not accepted – Khushal would remain content, preserving the dignity and standing of the Pashtun Muslim tribe, maintaining its independent status, free from the constraints of servitude.

Regarding the Pashtun people! Let them raise their banners to the mountains!

The caravan of flowers blossoms – (repeated phrase)

Let the nobility of the land cultivate their estates, fostering unity amongst their families.

The lamp of the valiant Khan illuminates the path for the nation – (repeated phrase)

Once again, let us remember that enemies do not prevail.

From the borders of the land, the lessons of life are learned – (repeated phrase)

Presenting a healthy appearance, he has been created with a good disposition – (repeated phrase)

He has built his reputation through virtuous deeds!

This is the foundation of his success – (repeated phrase)

The scars of past conflicts remain – it seems as though a moment of forgetfulness has passed.

Let each individual be a pillar for their community!

If a man’s service to the people is valued, he is considered wealthy – he is respected by all. He is burdened with the weight of responsibility, and therefore, he is close to God.

The wealthy are those who possess abundant wealth – they are respected by many. They are burdened with the weight of responsibility, and therefore, they are close to God.

Islam is a spiritual religion. In Islam, sincerity, chastity, and piety are the standards of status. Islamic status is not financial; it is not about material possessions.

The Khalifa Omar’s philosophy, practiced in the world, serves as a lesson to the world – and Khushal Khan Khattak did not believe that the Mughals, in their time, had acted in accordance with the fundamental principles of Islam. The Mughals, unlike the Muslims of today, did not accept a belief system that equated faith with material gain.

Khan and the Pashtun – in Islamic, political, and economic standing.

Therefore, Khushal Khan was not prepared to accept the Mughal Islamic government – because in such a government, there is no true Islamic governance, but rather the governance of the Mughals, seeking only to fulfill their own interests under the guise of Islam.  Should Khushal Khan maintain his position, the Pashtun Muslim tribe would preserve its status as a free and independent Mughal Muslim.  Mughal and Khushal Khan were religious – that is, Mughal and Khushal were not native to the land – neither were they a nation. There is no right for that which is occupied by the will of Pashtun. Be – and in the authority of the King – and Pashtun in the place of all Imad Mughal that passed.  The Mughals are no different than the Pashtuns – born for the King, for the death of the Pashtun, the Pashtun above – and birth for the Pashtun men are.  And the right to the Pashtun and Sayyid is fire.

Khushal will protect his heart in terms of religion – if the Mughals act on the fundamental principles of Islam – that is, every Muslim is equal to every other Muslim. All Muslims are Pashtuns – and they are brothers to each other – not to bother the king’s back – or a poor man’s feet. Islamic equality is higher than any wall – and others in Pashtun Urdu Ravi, the turn of the slave for peace has not come in the evening – so what is there to say? There is a promise – if the kings of the whole nation, the emirs, and the people with the intention of Islam were waiting for the Caliph – the Caliph was on his surat with every side, skillfully captured by Umar Azim Al-Shan, and he was always his fringe, and he gives the world the right to know about Islam, that Islam is the king of the heart and the fundamental principle of Kaya is essential – and this is Daraya.

137.right:
Pashtun Atlari
11 June 1939

Shaba Shaba

The pride of the mighty Jauhar, Shaba Shaba, all nations take pride in it.
At all times, God grants us honor. The state is growing stronger, Shaba Shaba.
The flood of heedlessness brings misfortune upon the Khan and the people.
Prayers are offered for the son of the future, so that the nation may be free once more, Shaba Shaba.
O Pashtun tribe! Let your spirit soar to the mountains!
The carnation flower blooms.
Let the literature flourish in your own garden, forging bonds of friendship.
The lover of Patak Fron John Sushar calls the candle of the nation a beacon of hope.
Let it be seen again and again that the infidel does not tremble.
The tribes of Tarmian in Turkey.
May the Pashtuns remain steadfast friends.
The lessons of life on the borders are becoming increasingly valuable.
The present sister of Trotar observed that the sacrifices of the nation are becoming increasingly similar.
Years of good deeds have been laid as a foundation!
This is the remainder of the time.
The divided tribes appear to be neglected.
The dam caused considerable trouble.
Shaba Shaba, if the nation is served, it is a worthy cause.
(Not published by Yakum Sahab)
Pashtun Atlari
11 June 1939

All people are born with a shared body. Oyushan is dying, a painful and tragic event. All people are equal in the womb and in death.
New teeth appear bright. Between birth and death, the people create divisions, considering themselves superior to one another. The King has his own brother. The fate of a human being is not different whether he is a ruler or a beggar.  Islam has bestowed honor upon all men. The poor and the rich are treated equally under Islam. All Muslims are equal in the eyes of God, and the world will be filled with anxiety. God’s blessed wealth is not a matter of contention, and the spirit of the nation is invigorated. Whoever possesses more wealth is more responsible, and he is a target of criticism and scorn. He is deprived of a seat. He is closer to God. Let us give God honor. Islam is a spiritual religion. In Islam, modesty and purity are the standards of status. Islamic status is not financial; it is not about material wealth or economic dominance.
Khalifa Umar’s philosophy is to teach the world through action. Khushal Khan Khattak, a member of the Mughal faith – that is, his own Muslim faith – did not believe in the tenets of the Mughal people. He did not consider the Mughal faith to be a valid belief.

138.right:
Pashtun Amanzi
34
Larchon 1399

Our Imprisonment and Suffering

Recently, a time of great hardship descended upon us, bringing with it relentless rains and flooding, transforming every place into a sea of water. The people lived in constant fear, anxiously awaiting the evening decree to abandon their homes in the southern part of Peshawar. These floods brought widespread devastation and distress.

As we sought to offer assistance, we found ourselves amidst a scene of chaos and despair. People cried out in fear, and when we arrived to offer aid, their faces reflected a profound sorrow. Children’s cries, women’s tears, and the outstretched hands of those pleading for help – it was a heartbreaking sight. We endeavored to provide a safe haven for these displaced individuals, managing their food and shelter as best we could, alleviating their fear of death. This experience remains etched in our memory, a testament to the profound relief we brought to those in need. Their expressions of gratitude filled us with a deep sense of satisfaction and fulfillment.
