139.right:
Pashtun Atanzi 38

It was past midnight, a night of tigers, I felt. I prayed that someone would come and attend to me. It was a divine occurrence – at three o’clock in the night, a light, like a lamp, appeared in the room from the passage. I was astonished to see who it was. Within a very short time, three men entered. One of them declared, “I certainly recognize you.” I replied that I did not know him. As I said this, he specifically stated, “You are a friend of someone I know.” At that moment, I drew a deep breath, hoping for some sign. They questioned me as to the nature of my crime here. I explained that I was innocent, that I had never committed an offense against anyone that would warrant such a severe punishment. They informed me that I was detained for a political offense, but the matter was that I was imprisoned for the death penalty. Hearing this, a profound sorrow gripped me, and I found myself contemplating death. They said that we had received an order to release us from the one who recognized you. I inquired how it was possible for me to be freed. They instructed me to accompany them. I followed them, enduring many difficulties along the way, until we finally arrived at a place where, behind a large gate, we were unblocked and proceeded discreetly.

140.right:
Pashtun Atanzi 38

As the night deepened, the scent of the mountains filled the air. I prayed to God that someone would come and offer me solace. Signs appeared at three o’clock in the morning – a light, like a lamp, shining from the path near my dwelling. I was bewildered by its origin. Within a short time, three men arrived. One of them said, “I recognize your face.” I replied that I did not know him. When I said this, he specifically stated that I was a comrade of his, one whom he recognized. At that moment, I felt little peace and hoped for what might happen. They questioned me as to the crime for which I was detained, stating that I was held for a political offense here, but that my detention was for the death penalty. Hearing this, a great sorrow fell upon me, and I remained in the hope of death. They told me that an order had arrived from someone who knew me, ordering my release. I asked how it was possible for me to be released. They instructed us to remain loyal to them. I complied with their instructions. We faced many difficulties on the way, but finally arrived at a place where a large gate stood, and we secretly took a different path.

Pashtun Atmanzi
Life Insurance

A method is presented here that will undoubtedly yield sufficient funds for such a purpose.
Each year, men of discernment and foresight safeguard their lives,
considering the welfare of their descendants in the land of India.
Reputable and dependable large firms, such as the Oriental, provide life insurance. For this,
one must secure a policy,
a matter of considerable importance.

And today, we urge you to acquire this Pantel policy!
Oriental Government Security Life Insurance, established in 1874,
offers its services through agencies and its Secretary to the President, Ravi,
who diligently handles correspondence.
The Peshawar branch serves the general populace and the Taliban alike.
Monthly payments are made,
ranging from ripe fruit to sixteen months,
two rupees, and approximately seven rupees near this property.
An advertisement for new wages appears on Pashtun publications,
with two additional holes and three more,
covering a full year or twelve years,
three hundred rupees, five hundred rupees,
six hundred rupees, twenty-five rupees,
external rupees, fifteen hundred rupees,
six rupees, and external rupees.
The address of Nazeer-ul-Saleh, Peshawar, is:
Authorship and Order by Abdul Khaliq Sahib, Village:
Martyr Sakina

It is all that transpired concerning her.  A choice is offered to many readers,
a flood of her words flows.  Within it, Pashto vocabulary and idioms are interwoven,
reflecting the methods employed.
She remained unbroken, a respected figure in Peshawar.
The location nearest Dao Kao is found in Peshawar.
Aman Sharif, Tehsil of Peshawar.
Note: For this book, the addresses of the people and other words in Pashto are required.
The order of Abdul Khaliq is for the advertisement of Pashtun on Pashtun in Pashto.

142.right:
I have become adrift in countless sorrows,
If the slave should perish on his journey,
How many times have I beheld your face?
The teeth of Peshawar,
A measure of the rain’s descent –
We lament your loss,
Or I shall seek refuge in a foreign land,
Or lay waste the Pashtun coat in battle.

=== PASHTUN 39 ===
(5)
Editorial
(12 Rabi’ al-Thani 1358 Hijri)
The Editor’s Note

Please accept this letter with respect. I have visited you in person,
and I offer my humble service. I pray that you will be well,
and that this correspondence may be received with favor.
Let us also be mindful of the plight of the oppressed,
and let us offer our sympathy.
---

Pashtun remains steadfast.
A letter of appreciation from the esteemed Mr. Afghan.

Regarding the service of the Honorable Mr. Afghan, he is currently residing in Peshawar.
Salam Alaikum! I write to you in the year 1930, requesting a tribal and military council. I made a solemn promise to fulfill this request. I have been diligently studied and am considered trustworthy to this day.  Khan Sahib and Khan Behaderat are respected figures. They are highly regarded by the people.  Much work remains to be done.  Submit this report to the government in a formal manner and with diligence. You should strive to gain the favor of the government and to be appointed to a position within it.  And to be chosen for the ranks.  The situation is dire. The ranks are overflowing.  The title is filled with chaos.  Patience is sorely tested.  From my heart, I offer my prayers.  Now, in this month, the ranks are full.  I have seen the chaos and the turmoil.  My esteemed Minister and my trusted advisors, along with my family, are all in the best of health and spirits.  We pray for their well-being and for a bountiful harvest.  May our prayers be answered.  The Minister, the elder, and his wife, like a beautiful queen, are in the highest ranks.  And the flag is waving at the fourth pole.  A letter from Mr. Abd Allah Khan to Mr. [Unclear – likely a title] was received by Mr. [Unclear – likely a title] for study.  He is a generous and respected man for the sake of Mr. [Unclear – likely a title].  His voice commands respect.  Therefore, you should, in this province, appoint one of the representatives of the twelfth tribe.  This is our humble offering.  I have observed the state of the roads and the condition of the people.

June 1939
Pashtun Trusts

A letter in the service of Fakhr Afghan
At the service of His Excellency Fakhr Afghan Sahib, he is now
Peace be upon you! I, in 1930, requested a national and military force. I made a full promise to fulfill it.
My name was duly appointed and remains credible to this day. Mr. Khan and Khan Bahadurt Sahibs are respected. They have written to Mink. Considerable work remains to be done. The government sent this bond to the treasury and burned it. You should therefore grade Mink and place it in the classes, where it is needed. The swords are broken and scattered. And Mink is filled with countless such weapons. There is no full hand, no shortage of weapons. In the title, patience is the first to be denied. Charma Tong by love has declared his support. Now, in the province, Mink has been seized. Charma Tong, his minister, and Toli and Mang are all eager to pray. May you and your ministers be blessed. The minister, Mullah, and his wife, like hunting animals, are in the forest on the fourth of Zaghli, Ubaidullah Khan’s letter was received by Mink. He is a skilled leather craftsman and deserves a good price for his leather. It is possible to make a home in Qadr al-Kadi. His voice is loud and strong. So there you go, we offer this to the tenth of the people in the province. The leather was torn apart.

May God bless the role of Pashtunkhwa and its people. Let the Pashtuns be raised for the sake of their lineage. For the lineage, for the sake of the kingdom of Yusuf, Memand, the land of Sawa, Bajaur, and Afridi. Anyone who thinks of evil, poverty, Khalil, Ghulghul, and everyone who is fit to serve the work, let them come forward and listen. Also, let them serve God. The Minister Sahib who has made a deal for you, he is not ashamed. Because of this, others who may come and ask, what should be done?
Let it be done.

Pashtun Automatic
Arjun 1929

May God bless your role in the prosperity of Pakhtunkhwa. Let us strive for the Pashtuns – for Malik, Yusuf Zai, Mohmand, Sawat, Bajaur, and Afridi. Let the hungry and the impoverished find sustenance, and let the bankers, Khalil, and Gholgi consider the welfare of others. And let all who are fit to serve, step forward and dedicate themselves. Furthermore, let us all pray for God’s guidance. The Minister, who has undertaken to manage your affairs, should do so with diligence and foresight. Therefore, let us all inquire as to what he intends to do, and what he seeks.

The Partnership of Commercial Transactions is utterly flawed. They have behaved disgracefully with women, and instead, they have deprived them of water.
I have also written letters to Obaidullah Khan. But you are subservient to Obaidullah Khan, and to your own congregation. You should not seek their counsel, nor should you follow their dictates. Consider carefully if this is the way to proceed – if it is so, then establish a system for the needs and desires of the congregation.

The work of the province was the responsibility of the Weston National Assembly. The committee (Charsde of Mufti Abad Tehsil) discussed the events and made a decision, determining the best method to be employed. Let those who have participated in this manner with error be warned. Let them understand that they have no share in it, and let them be told that they should perform their own family duties.

147.right:
Pashtun Trust:
Let us offer our congratulations on this auspicious occasion, and pray that Almighty Allah bestows the blessing of freedom upon all Pashtuns. –

The Voice of the Qur’an

Come, come, O people of the Rahman in India!
Against the turmoil of these times,
Actions have strayed from the true path,
Becoming merchants of illicit goods,
And engaging in the trade of food and drink,
The light of guidance does not penetrate every corner,
Gentlemen of Islam, do not become hardened and callous,
And kingdoms fall before the fleeting ambitions of evil.
Those who are entrusted with your care have taken upon themselves the responsibility of righteous conduct,
Establishing agreement, generosity, and wisdom as their guiding principles.
All actions must be in accordance with the laws of the faithful.
I offer this plea for the sake of the world’s salvation,
May all barriers be removed, and every soul find peace.
(From Sahib Saqi, a follower of Fazal Rahim from Madge)
June 1939, Pashtun Trust

The history of Pashtun recounts that on a day of great significance, a merchant from the Mirwais tribe was engaged in trade, and news arrived of the success of Shah Hussain’s father in Pawah, bringing him peace and prosperity. He began his work with meticulous care and thoughtful deliberation. Shah Hussain, however, was a weak and ailing man, and according to custom, he was under the protection of Gorgin Khan Ghondi, who, through his oppression, was not subject to the injustices suffered by the Mirwais people, as had been the case with the Mirwais themselves. The wealthy and powerful, driven by greed and the pursuit of luxury, seized the resources of the tribe, leaving the people impoverished and without recourse. Gorgin Khan, in his pursuit of wealth, did not seek assistance or support for those in need. The Pashtun people, seeking opportunity, observed the king’s decision to grant Gorgin Khan the authority to act. This limit, as thick as the head of a man, was imposed upon them, and the courtiers and those who indulged in extravagant displays of wealth were left to their own devices. Gorgin Khan, in the city of Kanda Har, in the most beautiful sugarcane fields, yes! He did not know of the hunting expeditions of Gorgin Khan. Rather, he extolled the virtues of strength and power. However, when he presented this to the King, he sent it back, fearing the danger to the cattle. It was a competitive endeavor, and he engaged in it with his cane. He did not expose himself to danger, and he engaged in a great deal of hunting.  Indeed, he was a man of great eccentricity in his hunting, bringing his relatives together, and converting his son to the Christian faith. He was a wealthy man in Kerman and کرمان, and his voice was heard in the ear of an eagle, and it was a beautiful art.

148.right:
The King, in this instance, set forth two pronouncements: firstly, that the Khan was solely responsible for those individuals within the provinces who possessed the authority to govern, representing two distinct kingdoms, and secondly, that women should not be permitted to tread the streets of these provinces, particularly if they adhered to religious beliefs or engaged in mercantile pursuits. Let it be known that in Islam, a ruler of the faithful is forbidden from fomenting rebellion against another Muslim nation, nor should such actions be undertaken. Let all contemplate this matter. Furthermore, two kings, engaged in opposition to Bijan Khan, were compelled to seek redress from the esteemed scholars of the realm, requesting a fatwa regarding the matter of Meri Sharif and the transgressions of treason. This fatwa was duly observed, and its dictates were not disregarded. Now, the path of Bijan Khan was laid bare.  The grievances of the loyal Muslims, whenever they arose, were addressed with diligence, and reports of injustice spread throughout the land, reaching the gates of cities. Instead of challenging the rule of the free Khan, the people should turn their attention to opposing his government. Moreover, the King of Lara to Kandahar sought permission and a summons to undertake the pilgrimage to Mecca, and the leaders of his tribe pledged their allegiance and swore fealty to the Sharif, returning from his journey.  His word and declaration were not to be questioned, and this permission was granted.  These individuals then sought the endorsement of the city’s leading scholars, producing a fatwa to legitimize their actions.  Who would compose verses and odes in praise of a cruel king and the Sunnis of the Bait? During the reign of Shah Abbas, it was permissible to conceal such sentiments, and these verses were likewise suppressed.

149.right:
Every time Mir Mir sought to secure the kingdom of Afghanistan for the Shah of Isfahan, he dispatched news to his people and the ministers and special associates of the King’s court, particularly the Prime Minister, of Georgia. This involved the imposition of special rights and the imposition of heavy taxes, resulting in a considerable accumulation of wealth and gold for the Prime Minister. Consequently, through the combined efforts of Mirwais and the Prime Minister, a decision was reached to conquer the territory of Iran and establish an army within its borders, a move that provoked a particularly unsettling event among the country’s populace. It is evident that every Mirvis strives for water security and relies on the protection of Russia. He is, in effect, the King of Russia’s protégé. As a result, a woman, a mother and a grandmother, sent to the embassy, became fearful and apprehensive, fearing the repercussions of the King of Russia’s actions. The Iranian delegation, in defiance of the King’s wishes, prepared to take action against the embassy. The royal decree, issued in Iran, was granted to him, effectively curtailing his freedom of movement. Those intimately familiar with the affairs of the Iranian princes and their possessions were not privy to these developments. The servants, brought with them to foster closeness to Iran, represented a colonial presence within the Iranian populace. And the affairs of the Iranian Qawtar Ho could be made known. The differences and corruption within these affairs could be observed in the final days of the embassy of Georgia to Iran. It transpired within the King and his court, a regrettable and ultimately inaccurate recollection of past events. It is a matter of great concern to all, and a source of widespread anxiety. It is clear that many factions and tribes within the region harbor grievances. Therefore, all should unite against this harmful practice. I seek the truth, free from bias, and have observed a peculiar method employed to elicit favor – the presentation of a fabricated justification for an act of injustice. Rather than confronting the root cause of the wrongdoing, individuals seek to cloak it in the guise of legitimacy. It is a deceptive maneuver, designed to obscure the reality of the situation. It is a regrettable practice, and one that should be vehemently opposed. The King, in his wisdom, has recognized this deception and has taken steps to prevent it. It is said that the devil thrives on the contrast between good and evil names. He delights in presenting darkness as light and vice as virtue. He encourages his followers to embrace falsehoods and to disregard the truth. It is a dangerous path, leading to ruin and destruction. Let us not be misled by such rhetoric. Let us instead strive for honesty and integrity, and to uphold the principles of justice and righteousness. It is a difficult task, but one that is essential for the well-being of our people and the stability of our nation. ---

150.right: Reports circulating in newspapers concerning the importation of murder into the border province of Pakhtunkhwa indicate that these deaths are being sold as criminal offenses, according to government decree. I have personally examined the substance of these reports, and it appears that investigations are underway to determine the causes of murder within the Pashtun province, resulting in a yearly surge in violent incidents throughout the broader Indian subcontinent. However, despite these efforts, no viable solutions or plans have been formulated to address this troubling phenomenon. The core message conveyed by these accounts is a plea for the preservation of human life – a grave offense indeed. It is widely believed that such acts represent a profound violation of morality. Furthermore, it seems that both the victim and the bereaved suffer greatly, with grief deepening over time. Individuals often refrain from speaking openly about these matters, fearing repercussions.

151.right:
Pashtun Anatomy

June 39

18 19

June 39

The territory bordering Azad and Ghastan is subject to closure of trade and economic activity. The common people and their livelihoods are strained, and the value of human life is diminished, with the law rendered ineffective. This misuse of weapons stems from ignorance and a lack of understanding of legal principles. A Pashtun is no longer loyal to his homeland, but merely to the weapon in his hand. Yet, within Pashtun society, the use of weapons is considered a matter of 18th-century custom. The consequences of this ignorance are readily apparent, leading to increased bloodshed and a cycle of violence. Pashtuns abandon the protection of their families and homes, leaving them vulnerable.

Secondly, I present the national traditions of the Pashtun people. The customs of the people – their status and the status of those considered elders – are overwhelmingly centered around the practice of murder and the complicity of those who carry out such acts. It is considered honorable for a Pashtun to act against another, to seek revenge, and to abandon his homeland. Honor and loyalty demand retribution for any perceived slight, fueling cycles of violence and bloodshed. The Pashtun people are not bound to the state; they are free to roam and trade, and to display their youth and honor through personal acts of bravery. The pride of the Pashtun is demonstrated through such deeds, reflecting their values and customs.

Economic prosperity is a distant dream. Proverbs abound, lamenting the loss of life and the degradation of the nation. The standard of living for many is impoverished, shaped by misguided Western influences and a lack of proper education. The people become isolated and disconnected, drifting aimlessly and losing their way. The division of the state leads to a decline in morality and a disregard for proper conduct. The Pashtun people are forced to adopt foreign customs, abandoning their own traditions and values. They become slaves to the whims of the powerful, losing their dignity and independence. The pursuit of wealth and status leads to corruption and exploitation, while the spirit of the Pashtun is diminished.

152.right:
V. The Lord has decreed the portion of the ant’s sword, and no one should claim it as their own. Why does the Pashtun call himself foolish in Pashto? I desire only to be content with myself, for a Pashtun is always close to the Dabat. Yet, with humility and prayer, one must seek the blessings of God, lest one fall into polytheism. A grave danger. It is a matter of the sword and the human spirit. It is a matter of respect for the law and the sanctity of life. Let no one claim the sword as a means of honor. The principles of wielding a sword are born of ignorance and lack of understanding of the law. A Pashtun should not be a stranger to his homeland, but only in Pashtun lands should the use of weapons be considered legitimate, and ignorance should be shunned. The position of the shepherd is elevated, and the position of the sheep is lowered.

Two, we present the tribal traditions of the Pashtun people. The customs of the tribes, the customs of the tribes, and the traditions of the elders are all essentially the same – a tale of murder and retribution. The Pashtun’s actions are his own, and he should strive to find a homeland. The call for honor and chastity demands the taking of a life in vengeance. A Pashtun should not be a harbor for murder, nor should he be a refuge for those who seek to commit it. He should not be a mercenary for the state or a servant of any power. He should not be a tool for the destruction of the Pashtun land. He should not be a place where the Pashtun language is silenced. Let him not be a field for the destruction of the Pashtun people. Let him not be a place where the Pashtun spirit is broken.

The prosperity of the nation is a fleeting dream. We are distinguished by our poverty and the loss of our dignity. The standard of life, dictated by English education, is often inferior, and the majority are impoverished and ignorant. The people are scattered and confused, and their families are broken. They are divided by the divisions of the state, and they are without guidance. They are impoverished and lost. They seek a simple path, but are corrupted by the ways of the world. Now, the traditions of the Pashtun people are in conflict with these ways, and they seek to attain perfection with the help of God. They will not be swayed by the world, nor will they be corrupted by its temptations.

The wise man seeks knowledge and wisdom, and he does not follow the path of folly. He seeks to understand the world and his place in it. He seeks to live a life of honor and integrity. He seeks to serve his people and his country.

The wisdom of Amir Khan Sahib Mittal, Headmaster of Azad School Atamanzi.

153.right:
Pashtun Elementary

A Look at Albania

King “Ahmed Zugho” was a figure rising above the customary status of merit and courage, a new element within his own realm. This king served as the deputy of the deceased Sultan Beg, and also oversaw the business affairs of Jalal Beg’s trading enterprise amongst the Gha tribe of the Albanians. Born in 1895, Ahmed Kenudo was educated through a coastal route in the Tarbiat region, remaining affiliated with the Ottoman Empire until 1915. However, a movement for freedom ignited within Turkey against the Sultan’s unjust rule, prompting Ahmed Zugho to initiate his own campaign for the liberation of his homeland. He joined the struggle at the age of twenty, enduring exile and the hardships of war. He ultimately achieved his aims. In 1928, he established the Republic of Albania, and in 1925, he became the King of Albania. Finally, in 1928, he assumed the title of “Zugho.”

He faced considerable opposition from those seeking to exploit the country. The people of Albania, like ministers, were often embroiled in treacherous dealings for personal gain, a dangerous game of exchanging blood. Controlling these unscrupulous individuals proved a difficult task. Ahmed Zugho skillfully evaded their plots. He observed the enemies’ behavior with careful scrutiny, noting their displays of vanity. Accounts speak of the deaths of over three hundred men; he himself was attacked, but miraculously survived.

He sought refuge beneath the cover of a machine gun, rarely participating in public gatherings.

Ahmed Zugho possessed a broad education, fluent in German, French, and Turkish, and he was well-versed in Serbian. His passion for learning led him to spend his evenings engaged in scholarly pursuits.

He was a skilled administrator, particularly adept at maintaining order and upholding the law amongst his people. The Binman brought Qabookin to prison due to his unconventional behavior. He wisely avoided provoking conflict. When he did venture out, he did so discreetly, avoiding any appearance of rebellion.  His bond with the Binman was maintained through respectful conduct.

A bitter enemy, Father Fan Noli, vehemently opposed Zugho. “They attempted to overthrow him, exiling him from his homeland. He ended up in America, where he fell gravely ill and required costly treatment.”

154.right:
Pashtun Atlani 22

Some more time passed… he became angry. So immediately,
he sent a sum of money. Yes, Pastor, he recovered quickly. So he returned to his homeland.
He became a close friend of his father’s.

There was a prevailing sentiment among the Zagh people – a belief that the tribes of Albania were inherently hostile to one another. This animosity, often fueled by petty disputes and personal vendettas, made reconciliation difficult. The death of the Governor Beg, a prominent figure in the region, was a source of contention between the Ghak tribe and the Albanians, particularly due to the wealth of Jalal Beg, whose trading enterprise flourished beyond the reputation of his humble farm. He was born in 1895. Ahmad Khan, however, received his education through a coastal route from the port of Tribat and was incorporated into the Ottoman Empire. Until 1915, Albania was part of the Ottoman government. However, in the early 20th century, a movement for freedom began to erupt against the oppressive rule of the Ottoman Sultan. Thus, Ahmad Zagh, having experienced hardship and loss from a young age of twelve, became a champion of his homeland’s independence. He endured exile and hardship, overcoming the trials of war. Indeed, he achieved his aims. In 1928, the Albanian Zir (governor) and in 1925, the Albanian Kingdom were established. Finally, in 1928, he declared himself King of Albania, adopting the title “Zagh.”

The Dala’s Shaman faced fierce opposition during his movement for freedom. The Albanian people, like ministers, were often portrayed as bloodthirsty, engaging in acts of betrayal. It was difficult to subdue these blood-soaked men. Ahmad Zagh’s wrath was terrible. He inflicted punishment upon his enemies with ruthless efficiency. There are accounts of over three hundred deaths, including his own. Though his head was severed, his spirit remained unbroken. He concealed himself within his private quarters, rarely attending public gatherings.

Amnon Piri, a particularly bitter enemy, “Zagh will be defeated by power. Therefore, he will be exiled from his land. He went to America for that purpose. But he became gravely ill and needed treatment— ---

156.right:
Pashtun Trustee

1 June 1939

The land endures change; it is yours, and yours alone. These mountains, the green heart of Kohistan, are the rightful inheritance of every Pashtun. This is the Pashtun National Number – a testament to the bravery and honor of the Pashtun people. Each Pashtun is a son of the brave region, and a beloved guest in every home.  And this is due to the honor and protection of Khan Khanan, the esteemed leader of all.  It is a matter of Pashtun geography, homeland, politics, and national identity – a reflection of the life of the Pashtun people, vibrant and resolute.  Khushal Khan Khattak, throughout his life, has striven for the Pashtun people, seeking to quell the discord of internal conflicts, and to restore the honor and grace of the Pashtun nation – to forge the Pashtun into a strong and resilient people, freeing them from the shackles of Mughal servitude!  Let it be known that his efforts, though ultimately unsuccessful, were nonetheless celebrated.  Indeed, the sun of Hindustan and Asia shines brightly over the peaks of the mountains, a beacon of Pashtun fortune and the glory of their rule.  Today, the Pashtun people look to the heavens, witnessing the prosperity and dominion of Pashtun.

Now, in the warm gatherings of the young and spirited, a sense of joy, happiness, and contentment prevails – a royal atmosphere.  Let there be no disdain for the learned, but rather, let them be honored and praised.

“Esteemed, beloved, honored husband, dear husband, dear husband – may your blessings always be upon us, may your health never fail!” I, when I reflect on this, feel like a simple, humble student.  Am I, Khushal, a joyful man, constantly embroiled in conflict?  Without cause, do I feel a pain in my own heart?  I see the color of my own skin fading!  “Woe to me!  Blessed be your Lord!”  Let the color of the river and the indigo stain my garments.  Let me, in the solitude of my chamber, contemplate the beauty of the world.  Let me not cast aspersions on the noble men of this land!  For I desire no sorrow, no burden of responsibility.  I seek only the freedom and the joy of a life of honor, and to escape the shame of slavery.  I remember the grief of the past.  Let us prepare ourselves for the mighty confrontation with the tyrant!  For I am a seeker of freedom and a defender of honor.

Esteemed, beloved, honored husband, dear husband, dear husband – may your blessings always be upon us, may your health never fail! –  Let there be no scorn for the learned, but rather, let them be honored and praised.  I, when I reflect on this, feel like a simple, humble student.  Am I, Khushal, a joyful man, constantly embroiled in conflict?  Without cause, do I feel a pain in my own heart?  I see the color of my own skin fading!  “Woe to me!  Blessed be your Lord!”  Let the color of the river and the indigo stain my garments.  Let me, in the solitude of my chamber, contemplate the beauty of the world.  Let me not cast aspersions on the noble men of this land!  For I desire no sorrow, no burden of responsibility.  I seek only the freedom and the joy of a life of honor, and to escape the shame of slavery.  I remember the grief of the past.  Let us prepare ourselves for the mighty confrontation with the tyrant!  For I am a seeker of freedom and a defender of honor.

157.right:
The warmth of this gathering, a vibrant circle of friends, brings joy, happiness, and a queenly grace. Let no one be burdened by bitterness, and let greetings be sincere.

My dear, embrace me, cherish me, my beloved.  Let there be no falsehood in your words, no deception.
I, a humble wanderer, am often lost, lost, lost.
I am joyful, yet ever drawn into conflict – is this the fate of a man?
Without a guiding hand, I find myself adrift, alone.  Praise be to your Lord!  The colors of the river and the pomegranate stain my hands.
Let me contemplate in solitude, seeking the blessing of the heavens, the colors of the river and pomegranate mingling upon my skin.
How easily the sword and the pomegranate stain my hands!  Let me prepare for battle, for the defense of my people.  I am a friend, a confidant, a guardian of secrets.  I am a young man, devoted to my Khan and the honor of my people.
My duty extends to the households of the Khan and the protection of our nation.  I am a weak man, preparing for the day when a strong enemy will challenge us.  I seek the freedom and the joy of a life of honor, and I abhor the servitude and shame of a life devoid of dignity.  I mourn the loss of loved ones.  Therefore, I find myself with Khushal Khan, and this remains true.  It is not surprising that he has risen to such a prominent position in the Pashtun people – he is a leader, a hero, and a symbol of our nation.  He embodies the courage and spirit of the Pashtun.  He is a protector, a defender, and a champion of our honor.

158.right:
Khushal Khan’s poetry and patriotism, and his character:

Patriotism is poetry, and poetry is patriotism. This sentiment—the intertwining of Afghan patriotism with a passionate yearning—has grown so profound that every flower seems to echo the fear and longing for the homeland. It is at this point that the discussion of Khushal Khan’s poetry, at the appropriate moment, will be fruitful.

Khushal Jangi’s verses – readily usable, every Pashtun heart will be touched by them. They reflect upon the past and present of the Pashtuns, gazing upon the top of the head. The letters of these poets reveal the hope of Pashtun war, and the judgments of those who contemplate whether or not these wars are justified. The ideas of these poets represent the merits and virtues of the Pashtun people. They are the true mirror reflecting the poetry of the Pashtun homeland, for the Pashtun possesses a simple, sharp, and warlike nature, combined with a civil status. In his youth, he endured the hardships of the Pashtun people, living in the homes and villages of the region, witnessing the power of the country and the family, and the vastness of the plains. He observed the struggles of the Pashtuns against the Mughals, the destruction of their kingdom, and the scent of their people. He sought wisdom, consulted with wise men, and studied the ethics and moral principles. He also explored the beauty of music and poetic expressions of nature, and the love and longing for affection.  As a poet, he documented his experiences, his travels, and the famous deeds of his time. He sought to promote Pashtun culture and to liberate the Pashtun people from Mughal slavery.  He yearned for his homeland in every journey, remembering his family and complaining about the toll of the Ram Khan, lamenting the cycle of time, and expressing his sorrow at the plight of the people.

Because Khushal Khan is the interpreter of every feeling and a witness to the past, observe him closely. Khushal Khan is a joyous figure at the time of the Maidan Ghali, a symbol of perfect bravery and martial prowess, unified and resolute in every endeavor. Now, let the bow and arrow rest, and the gun be put aside. Let him focus on the bow, and let him be the target of the enemy’s arrows.  Let him be the one to command the army. Let his sword be raised in defense of his people.
158.left:
Political and public conditions. Patriotism, love of country, diligence, loyalty to one’s comrades, and heartfelt desires. The Pashtun’s behavior and hatred. Rhetoric and passionate speeches about war. The situation of his time. I mention famous places and war stories of my time, the tales of the brave. I mention the wisdom of the learned, the principles of ethics, and the beauty of music and poetic expressions. I study the love and longing for affection. The poets and I are all poets in the history of the Pashtuns, and so on. We strive to promote Pashtun culture and to liberate the Pashtun people from Mughal slavery. Each of our poems expresses a longing for the homeland during travel and journeys, remembering our families and complaining about the burdens of Ram Khan, lamenting the passage of time, and expressing sorrow for the plight of the people.

In short, Khushal Khan is the interpreter of every feeling and a witness to the past. Look closely at Khushal Khan, who is a joyous figure at the time of the Maidan Ghali. He is a symbol of perfect bravery and martial prowess, unified and resolute in every endeavor. Now, let the bow and arrow rest, and the gun be put aside. Let him focus on the bow, and let him be the target of the enemy’s arrows. Let him be the one to command the army. Let his sword be raised in defense of his people.

159.right:
Here’s an improved English translation of the provided Pashto spread, aiming for fluency, coherence, faithfulness, and an appropriate early 20th-century register:

The esteemed Khushal Khan – a poet and patriot, a man of profound character – embodies the very spirit of Pashtun patriotism, mirroring the fervor of Afghan nationalism with a passionate intensity.  Indeed, Khushal’s poetry reflects the essence of Pashtun patriotism, capturing the spirit of Afghan nationalism with such force that every flower seems to celebrate the nation’s freedom.  We shall consider Khushal’s work in the context of his reflections on this very patriotism.

Khushal’s verses, when spoken or heard on the radio, resonate deeply within the heart of every Pashtun, becoming a source of pride and remembrance. They evoke the memory of past glories and inspire a yearning for the homeland.  These poets’ writings, recalling the Pashtun’s spirit of resistance and the enduring legacy of Pashtun warriors, remind us of the hopes and aspirations of our people.  They contemplate the judgments of past leaders, weighing their actions and considering their motivations.  Khushal’s poetry offers a true reflection of Pashtun patriotism, for the Pashtun is characterized by a simple, courageous spirit, a martial nature, and a noble bearing.  He remembers the hardships of the Pashtun people, their lives confined to the homes of their villages, their strength and honor surpassing that of the nobility and the comforts of the home, and their proud bearing.  He recalls the struggles against the Mughals, the assertion of Pashtun independence, and the preservation of their heritage.  He speaks of the people’s loyalty, their devotion, and their love for their land.  He celebrates their bravery, their resilience, and their dignity.  Khushal, therefore, is a translator of every feeling, a witness to the passing of time.

160.right:
Pashtun Atmatzi
34
The people of the world are deceitful, and to that end, they have included the instruction of principles and educational texts within the world’s populace. Pashto, alongside the Pacha newspaper and its publication, actively participates in the Barak Adiki Purdah movement, striving to reach the public through correspondence and repeated efforts. Pashtuns are directing their attention to the improvement of the Risala, utilizing their unique characteristics to draw the attention of the Akhbar publication. Though new, innovative verse forms have appeared in one or two publications, it is a regrettable circumstance that these expectations have not been fully met. I earnestly hope that the Great Naqibullah Khan Sahib will further increase his support and encouragement. Furthermore, Pashto is a vital need for those engaged in education. Every individual should consider it when engaging in any transaction, and the Pashtun Risala currently provides consistent support to newspapers in their publications. Let all those involved in the movement facilitate the necessary resources, such as education. May every corner be filled with books, establishing this foundation as a cornerstone of adult education. (Mr. Master Muqeem Khan Sahib mentions the reformation school)
(closed)
1930, a tumultuous year

June 1939
35
June 1939
The government is not satisfied with charges. I will include every fear in the world, for the sake of the Lord, for the sake of the pure Pashto scent.
Adult Education Series
A government decree was obtained at some point in the past, and the education movement began at that time. This resulted in a long period of stagnation. Good wishes have been made regularly for this. It is not a suitable curriculum for these people, and the movement continues to work on the mountain. He is not a student. To ensure the success of the movement, the government is providing funds. Let us hope that the movement will be successful with the government’s support.
Now, I wish to bring to your attention those individuals who are connected to education, possess a clear understanding of themselves, and are involved in transactions with the government and students. There are many Pashto scholars of high standing in the Tehreek Education Court, due to the persecution of some individuals, they wish to be excused from their duties for the sake of the Pashtuns. Let them write with a pen, not a sword. Let the Khay Maqam be used for the sake of Baba’s relatives, and let the soldiers be dispatched.

163.right:
In this issue,

There is a method whereby individuals can secure a sufficient profit on their possessions, benefiting themselves, their friends, and their heirs.

Sir: Regarding the new nations, for one’s own life, for one’s descendants, and for the strengthening of India – a new and powerful nation – adequate foundations for wealth must be established.

Within the Orient, sufficient resources exist for one’s own well-being and prosperity.

Furthermore, the Orient’s policies are being diligently pursued! The Oriental Government Securities Company, P. Life Insurance Limited, established in 1874, is actively engaged.

Regarding other matters and circumstances, the Bakhti Dad Hagarshan Branch Secretary accepts correspondence at a cost of sixty rupees.

Pindon Division also handles correspondence.

Not from the Taliban –

Two rupees for a period of six months.

The Pashtun’s appetite for innovation…

Three times a year, and twenty.

Two rupees for six months.

Forty-twenty rupees, ten rupees.

Corrections regarding currency are being implemented, confirmed by the judgment of Sahib Hakam Abdul Haq.

“Sleep eating” – referring to the careful selection of news for the newspaper, ensuring it is suitable for Pashtun readers and particularly those not involved in the pricing of goods.

It is important to note that praising texts and songs is intended to stimulate thought. The Pashtun Khalki (newsletter) is printed on the electric press, and this is done for the purpose of disseminating these materials.

164.right:
What dwelling is it that knows the path?
If my servant arrives, you shall be set free.
Upon the kingdoms as well, this applies.
A formal application for rank and status,
Like the slow drip of water.
We follow him closely,
And shall always recall his devotion to the law.

Pashtun Almanzi
21 June 1939
Volume (5) Number (14)
A Message of Grief from the Pashtun People
The sacrifice and grief of the nation are such that the great leader, Khan Sahib, God’s Helper, and a champion of homeland freedom, who had always raised his voice against the injustices and oppression of the British, is no longer among us. His passing is a great sorrow for the entire nation. In this grief, not only his family and friends, but all those who have witnessed the aspirations of the homeland and its desire for independence and prosperity are in mourning. It is incumbent upon every Pashtun to remember his service and follow his path. We pray for his forgiveness in the presence of God. May God grant patience and blessings to his family and all his friends. Amen.

Familiarity with Meaning

A mutual acquaintance is not familiar with the purpose of a mutual exchange; acquaintance without a shared understanding is merely a superficial greeting. God’s actions occurred during a time of intense hostility, when the Russian forces were formidable enemies. The British, in their folly, believed that the Russians would remain subservient, a notion they propagated to conceal their own vulnerabilities.  They considered the سرد (Sard – a term referring to a leader or chieftain) a crucial element in this deception, falsely claiming that no cruel individuals resided near them in the world.  However, within a short period, the English recognized their Russian adversary.  The astute and discerning would observe the state of those in power. It was widely known that the Lashkare-Hind (Indian Army) possessed sufficient strength to dominate the entire Indian subcontinent.  It was deemed that if they were to advance, they would establish a firm foothold there.  Indeed, the Pashtuns were increasingly recognized amongst the Indian populace.  When faced with doubt within their own territory, they were subjected to oppression by Russia.  It is well-documented that the Russians sought to purchase Nad Kkha (land) from the Pashtuns in their province, inflicting a grievous wound.  This resulted in the Pashtuns being left impoverished and without assistance. The government’s actions were perceived as deceitful, offering false accusations to mask their own transgressions.  All of this led to the Pashtuns suffering hardship and loss, and a profound sense of humiliation.  Today, the remnants of this suffering are evident.

Within a short time, fighting erupted along the Pashtun border.  God is more merciful than their enemies.  May God bless them. The Red Government will undoubtedly pursue the same course. We have forgotten the past, and we must not be distracted by it.  Let us focus on our own affairs, rather than dwelling on the past.  The Pashtuns are not afraid of any hardship; they are steadfast in their commitment to the cause.  The government is known for its reluctance to provide assistance, a fact that has led to widespread discontent among the people. 168.right:
Pashtun Amani
21 June 1920
9

The rights of the common people – and furthermore, the government should not engage in any oppression.  If there is talk of shirk, the people harbor doubts and suspicions regarding Russia’s accusations. And this oppression began on this map – the people were easily manipulated, and the government of the Soviets made it known that they were not close to the people. They believed that the close associates of the Soviet government were not close to the Pashtuns. For a short time, when the British were no longer easily discernible, the astute and wise would look towards Bashkotela.  It was clear that they sought to implement a sinister scheme to bring all of Hindustan under their control, with the strength of the British in Hindustan to achieve this.  In their view, this was a great advantage. And as many as were within their ranks, they recognized the Pashtuns among the Pashtuns.  The Soviets inflicted injustice upon the Pashtuns in their own territory.  They did not understand that in Europe, the English and Russians were merely considered as possessions.  Efforts were made by the Soviets, but they were unsuccessful.  It was believed that the British government was speaking of time, not everything, and there was no hope for peace until compensation was received. The people were oppressed and the cause of their suffering was false accusations. Everything was against the Pashtuns and their true situation was not known.

Within a short time, a full pan began to spread – and the responsible party was the Minister and the unmarried man – if he lost everything else, he had to pass the past error – when he asked for permission – and sometimes somewhere else – the Imam was a good man, and the people were not close – when Russia was arrogant and Turkish – this was a friend of the Ray, seeking friendship – yesterday Russia was an infidel, arrogant, cruel, and wrong.  The news of Russia spread, and the English met with Russia – this was their duty. Yesterday, you were a famous nation whose face and suffering were kind. It was also strange that few people saw me at Shoki yesterday – seeing the oppressor, seeing houses looted, seeing the loss of honor – today’s Hoi Plas is the art of the government and the art of the country.  Hate was felt towards Hoy Dutt – loved by Hoy Dutt – (more on this).
(From Master Amir Matta Khan Sahib)

Why do Pashtuns not get sweet on time?

It is a question that Cham does not speak about the Pashtun’s eyes – not themselves, nor are they overly verbose or expressive in their speech – the answer is how much the Pashtuns desire preparation for the administration.  Writing ends at the time of the book – they do not learn – why is time lost for Pashtuns? The condition is very good if you train on the pattern for a week. That’s why we understand the answer to Sher Muhammad Khan Sahib Salar Mardan. From the Dobrani printing house, we have sent the claim of the Afghans on this continent. This demon has come to shame himself and considers it his work. Why is the dark color of the bare feet bothering the mother? Oh God, two Farkh and Saqi-Khiraws are trying to do something to be abolished – Umm Sad, who published the English version in Pashtun at the time.

169.right:
Pashtun Atmazi, 21st June 1920
(1)
Mir Ashraf Khan Sahib, a distinguished figure of the Bakhtiari tribe, has served with distinction throughout Pashtun lands and in many corners of the world. He has participated in numerous governmental roles, and his work is widely documented in the Pashtun newspaper, appearing in detail across various locales. The Pashtun community holds him in high esteem, frequently citing his service and achievements in their own publications. He has also contributed significantly to scholarly works, offering a comprehensive account of the Pashtun people.  Throughout his career, he has consistently championed the interests of the Pashtun nation, and his dedication has been unwavering, even to the point of sacrificing his own life for its advancement.  He remains a pivotal figure for the Pashtun community, continuing to advocate for their welfare and prosperity.
169.left:
Begins with this Salam Zubair, proposing reforms for the betterment of Pashtun affairs.  Pashtuns, we trust that (face) Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum Khan Sahib Qureshi, residing near Peshawar and the other colonies, will find a suitable home within this region and contribute to its intellectual and cultural life.  He is a man of learning and piety, and his service to the Pashtun community is commendable.  In his depiction of the Pashtun people within the esteemed “Shaan Hind” annual publication, he has presented a particularly insightful portrait.  It is hoped that he will continue to contribute to this work with further writings in the future.

---

Fazl Shah Sahib (Po Poon)’s poignant verses, inspired by the sight of the people, express a deep appreciation for the strength of Pashtunistan and the spirit of the nation.  He finds joy in the recognition of their collective achievements and dedicates his life to their well-being, openly acknowledging their shared destiny.  He is considered a leader within the Pashtun community, consistently guiding them towards progress and unity.  May he continue to be a steadfast presence in their lives, always striving for the betterment of the Pashtun people.  Let us hope that his efforts will ultimately lead to success for the Pashtun nation.

---

Pashtun Atmazi
21st June 1929

Regarding a matter brought to our attention and potentially causing damage if overlooked, we humbly request forgiveness from the esteemed reader.

(2)
Hakim Warawi, Jan Mohammad Sahib, a respected Pashtun scholar, has penned a piece regarding the establishment of new shops and businesses in the city, aiming to facilitate trade and the pooling of capital.  It is suggested that the government should encourage this initiative, allowing merchants to invest and benefit from shared resources. However, this proposal may cause harm to some, particularly those who rely on existing trade routes. Hakim Sahib anticipates a period of waiting, observing the developments in the daily news, and awaiting a response from the relevant authorities.  As reported by Ataullah Sahib, the grievances of the Iranian community are numerous.  Should the esteemed reader question the wisdom of Hakim Sahib’s proposal or oppose his critique of the government’s actions?  Now, the Hakim Sahib’s piece has been published, and it is a source of great disappointment to many.

---

 مولانا عبد الوهاب صاحب, a prominent Pashtun, has written a critical piece regarding a recent publication. It is not appropriate to reproduce it here.  We must adapt to the changing times of journalism.  May Allah grant him peace, and may his words be remembered with respect.  And the Pashtun people should be grateful.

---

(3)
 مولانا Abdul Wahab Sahib, a respected Pashtun scholar, has written a scathing critique of a recent publication. It is not suitable for reproduction here. We must adapt to the changing times of journalism. May Allah grant him peace, and may his words be remembered with respect. And the Pashtun people should be grateful.

170.right:
Pashtun Amanzi
21 June 1929

I offer my apologies for this unsolicited suggestion, but for my own sake,
Mohib has written in response. Should Maulana Sahib consider our internal
discussions regarding the future of India, then perhaps the esteemed Shahzada Abdul Qayum Khan Sahib Qureshi Border
will indeed establish himself amidst the towns of Peshawar and the other colonies, residing within their family lineage – a man of education and dedicated service to his people. He has made significant contributions to the Pashtun community through his learning and in the various endeavors of the region, and in the books of the Pashtun community, he has detailed his service to the Pashtun people in great detail. He has served the Pashtun people in every corner of the land, and is considered the foremost leader of the Pashtun people, offering his services to the Pashtun people in every place. He has sacrificed his life for the Pashtun people and has performed many important deeds for the Pashtun people, raising his voice for the Pashtun people in every place.

—

171.right:
The flowers are parched,
To your esteemed presence,
A tale of empty shops, which drew a river spirit from its bed, a distant stream. I take my leave. Let the palm flourish. He who, if he was there with me. A single, slender branch of a palm tree and a parrot emerged from the other side. The man and the bird sat beside each other. The parrot observed, pecking at the fruit and the man. When he had eaten his fill, he began to sing. I will recount a story. The parrot, with its bright plumage, was a creature of great delight. It told a sad tale of Bibi Naghansta, and I found myself laughing. It is good to know you, I will now share a story of Rashid Badshah, King of the realm. The market was bustling with merchants, and goods flowed from every stall. The first to arrive was the wealthy merchant, and then the poor traveler. I was astonished to see this, and my heart felt a pang of sorrow. Why do you observe this man, and you, with your name emblazoned upon everything? Do you not realize that the whispers of the people are often more truthful than the pronouncements of the Congress? For if the rains cease, the crops will wither, and the people will starve. And if the treasury is emptied, the people will suffer.
(5)
Note: The late Pashtun’s late and wrongly printed. What has befallen you? And the char that is not there. I would like to inform you that the print has been printed incorrectly. Or, by the work of God, we have been given the word "Radar Number One", our full song. We do not believe in a story that is 1/20 percent wrong, it is not the end of the story. Dad Ghana Chape that Chapata is stopped.
(6)
Advice: Do not engage in inappropriate conduct or speak inappropriate words. If you are angered, be silent and patient. Reflect upon your actions, for they will inevitably be revealed in time. Nzalana Ota zone will be.

172.right:
Pashtun Atmanzi 21 June 1939

I consider you my Amin, a servant of Peshawar and a devoted follower. If my intentions are good, then I am but a humble donkey, though perhaps you see strength and a path laid before me. My heart felt the world’s power resting upon it, and I flew to you with glad tidings. The merchant’s market flourishes, the power of Diodkan is evident. First, a good day arrived, bringing a fear of death to Embrid. I was astonished by this, and yet not entirely surprised to see such late businessmen and you, esteemed by name.

A slave of the homeland

The parrot, having grown old, has summarized its story and presented it concisely.  It was told to me that the people of this region suffered a devastating plague, causing widespread destruction and ruin.  And those who sailed the seas, lost in the last world, were brought low.  I was bewildered by this, and my mind was troubled by the strange appearance of those who laugh and trade, naming themselves with pride.

A prisoner of the homeland

Wise counsel: This unworthy work and these inappropriate words cause great sorrow. Control your anger, and embrace patience and forbearance. Reflect upon the sincerity of your deeds in the present moment. May our family be blessed.
172.left:
ستون اتمانزی 21 June 1939

It is hot, and time passes – the world is closed.  Who knows the wisdom of the first generation? – I doubt everything constantly. And it is irritating to me, but I know who drove it.  However, I will always strive to be a good mother to you, and I will not allow it to happen again.  I earnestly desire a pure and noble nature, a strong hand to command, and the swords and spears of my people.  Let us begin a peaceful dance in the morning, and then it will disappear. The parrot still hears news from the side of the great one, and when the fire broke out, he began buying and selling, long and wide.  He fell down and lowered his eyes, God, that’s a terrible thing! (Crazy Foxy)

Lata Naz Dair Kar Yama Khan Shad Wa Qam Watana – A prisoner of the homeland, a slave of the homeland at the feet of a brave man.  My heart finds solace only in your presence; my name will forever be free from the burdens of this land.  These are my wheat and my family, and we will be happy in our homeland.  I will wear a necklace of freedom, the rind will come to Qaf, the motherland of Mia.  Let us be mindful of the enemy’s desires and the luxury he has indulged in.  May we be blessed with the wisdom of God, and may our prayers be heard.  The Muslim’s home is a place of uproar – let us ignore the whispers of the enemy.  It is uncomfortable – sometimes it’s hard work – sometimes it’s completely deaf.  Dao Khair – and sometimes on a bread – when China’s demand is broken.  Andi Tan Pura Mishta first has a little bit of bad nature.  I’m going to make a jar of it, and I’m going to die.

The wisdom of God is precious – let us cherish it and be grateful.  Let us speak of its blessings, and let us remember the kindness of the merciful.  May we be blessed with a sweet and joyful life.  It is written that we should be generous and charitable, and to offer help to those in need.  The homeland of the Hindus of the East is far from its own conditions, and it is constantly seeking new ways and advancements.  Now, your wealth is in your hands, and in the faith of Islam.  You should not take any steps without considering the blessings of Islam.  It is a sound of fear when a Muslim’s home is disturbed by strangers.  It is better to be poor than to be rich and surrounded by evil.  The law of Satan is a bad thing – do not follow it.  The law of the Holy Word is pure.  Where have you been? 173.right:
Here's a revised English translation of the current spread, aiming for fluency, coherence, faithfulness, and an appropriate early 20th-century register:

**Pukhtoon Magazine – 21st June 1939**

Let it be known that I consider you, my Amin, a friend as you arrive in Peshawar, and I offer my greetings with sincerity.  I am but a humble servant, and if I were to depart, it would be as though an ox were slain – a grievous loss.  May your name be remembered for strength and for justice, carried on the winds. The trading markets of the land are bustling, with merchants eager to sell their wares – goods of fine quality in every shop.  The poor traveler finds little solace in these times, only two months of meager sustenance.  The wealthy return from their travels, laden with gifts and luxuries, while the common folk struggle.  I am astonished by this spectacle, and by the gatherings of prosperous merchants and you amongst them, a figure of distinction.
173.left:
Let me now relate a tale from the elder’s memory, a summary of events recounted long ago.  It is said that the fever, known as the “Mal-e-Jah” swept through the region, causing widespread illness and death.  Those afflicted were brought to the village, and the elders sought remedies, but to little avail.  The world has seen such plagues before, a shadow from a distant past, and the consequences are always dire.  It is not for me to judge the actions of those who came before, nor to dwell on the sorrows of the past.  I find myself pondering the nature of this world, its fleeting joys and inevitable sorrows.  The merchant’s wealth, and you amongst them, a figure of renown, are a curious sight.

---

The time has come, and the world is ever shifting.  Let us remember the wisdom of our ancestors.  Each day brings new anxieties.  And though I am wary of the future, I will always remain steadfast, and I will not yield to despair.  I seek a life of virtue, guided by the principles of justice and righteousness.  In the spring, a great storm arose and vanished without a trace.  The birds have returned, seeking food and shelter, and the merchants are once again engaged in trade.  For too long, the land has been barren, and the people have suffered.  I am troubled by this, and by the hardships faced by the people of my homeland.

---

The government’s decree is a source of great concern.  It is a heavy burden upon the people.  Let us pray for guidance and strength.  The homes of the Muslims are filled with commotion, as if a thief were present.  Let us strive for peace and tranquility in our homes, and to enjoy the blessings of life.  Let us not be consumed by envy or greed.  Let us remember that true happiness lies in contentment and gratitude.  May Allah grant us the strength to endure these difficult times, and to remain steadfast in our faith.  Let us not be swayed by the whispers of the devil, nor succumb to despair.  Let us seek refuge in His mercy, and turn to Him in times of need.  I find myself seeking solace in the quiet of my home, far from the turmoil of the world.

---

May you be blessed and prosperous, my loyal Pashtun.  Your name will forever be etched in my heart.  We will share in joy and sorrow, and may our friendship endure.  Let me embrace your hand and offer you my support.  Let us strive to uphold the honor of our people.  Let us not be consumed by hatred or animosity.  May Allah protect us from the evils of this world, and guide us on the path of righteousness.  Let us not be blinded by the temptations of wealth and power, but remain humble and grateful.  I find myself seeking refuge in the shade of a tree, far from the noise and bustle of the city.  May your labor be rewarded, my friend, and may you find peace and prosperity.  (Mr. Hazrat Shah Sahib, the owner of the finest steel)

---

Written in the light of Islamic law.

174.right:
Pashtun Amanzi 21 June 1939

From these three maps, it is evident that approximately one-quarter (1/4) of all students who enter the initial congregation proceed to the first grade, while the remaining seven-eighths (7/8) are directed towards primary education.  Furthermore, these primary students are then divided amongst themselves.

It is also noted that a significant number of higher-level students, particularly those pursuing advanced studies, enter the madrassas.  Beyond this, within the city of Peshawar, it is possible to observe a consistent flow of students, and whenever a student achieves a half-pass, it is considered a sufficient qualification for further study. However, this type of specialized training is not widely pursued.

The official report details these observations, alongside a reference to the Indian Education Report of 1930, highlighting the overall state of education in British India.  The report observes that in England, students are frequently found in various gatherings and communities, representing only a tenth of the total population engaged in official affairs, while the remaining two-thirds are involved in other pursuits.

These students, having completed their (1/4) portion of their studies, typically do not pursue further formal education for several years.  They often engage in modest occupations, such as working as laborers or traders, and their educational pursuits are limited to a few years.  It is important to note that the number of students who dedicate themselves to a serious study of the sciences is relatively small.

Historical accounts suggest that the essence of love lies in the flow of water, and in the bustling marketplace, the path is protected by the vigilance of the Pashtun. An Act for the Advancement of Our Country

There is a need to establish a comprehensive workspace within the country, where a large undertaking can be realized for the benefit of all. This would expand the business landscape for the people, and prevent the mention of such discussions for the sake of life, which should be directed towards the betterment of the nation and the country. It is crucial to strive for the prosperity of others, overcoming falsehoods and achieving numerous successes. And in this context, instead of focusing on the work for the nation, efforts should be directed towards the reform and improvement of the country’s infrastructure, ensuring it serves the interests of the nation as a whole.  This includes the careful consideration of these and other aspects, all aimed at fostering a prosperous future.

177.right:
Pashtun Atmanzi

1 July 1939

It is worth noting in the people that this is a matter of grief and sorrow.
There is a great sadness among them.
Regarding Gully Sharif Khan’s new pit at Bel New Sari,
and we were buried there.
The response to the father of the deceased suing for revenge against the murderer is as follows:
It was known that Muzaffar Malik of Dhaka had sent his servant to the Khan’s household, and they were engaged in conversation.
A witness, whose name was not mentioned, arrived at the Khan’s home.

(2)

The time and manner of the event were questioned. The plaintiff’s claims were disputed, and several people were brought forward. In another incident, they were brought before the court, but the witnesses were hesitant to speak, and the matter was delayed. However, the peace of the country was not disturbed, and it was evident that this event did not occur.

(3)

The Jirga possessed authority. The Ruler, after careful consideration, decided to return the inherited property to the Khan.  It was decreed that the witness, Chamil, must present evidence to the Khan.  Further disputes arose, causing confusion and unrest.  Some questioned whether the deceased’s family should be consulted, arguing that the matter should be handled according to established custom.  Extensive investigations were ordered, postponed for four months. The people were agitated, demanding a swift resolution, and the Jirga was forced to vote unanimously.  It was determined that there was no evidence against the murderer, but the Hakim sought to appease the plaintiff by paying a sum of rupees to the murderer.  This act, however, was viewed with suspicion by many. Muzaffar Malik was astonished and recognized the decision.

(4)

The people’s decision was not proven by the enemy’s request.  The Ruler’s reputation for backbiting was not upheld.

The Jirga summoned additional members, inquiring about the deceased’s family and his connections.  For a sum of twenty rupees, he would be honored with a gift, but the world would not be able to offer anything in return.  Two years of exile were decreed for Molly.

Model!

(Letter from the reporter of Swat)

It is considered a disservice to the Pashtun people to regard the service of the country as less than a servant of their nation, not merely a supporter from afar. This fact is established, as the Pashtun newspaper, being the most current, reflects the years that have passed, and in addition to it, as a new edition of the Pashtun newspaper, some Pashto publications are being disseminated for the purpose of promoting the Pashto language.  This is done in recognition of the British occasion.

179.right:
8 July 1920

Banyon Amanzi

The noteworthy effort undertaken in the publication of this newspaper is to present the Pashtun people in the form of a national chronicle. This demonstrates that the Pashtun is not merely a servant of his people, but also a protector of their interests. This publication, akin to the Afghan truth, has been running for many years, including the recent introduction of new editions by Pashtun printers.

9

Brief News from Banner Members

Egypt’s *Al-Ahram* reports that Minister Abd al-Fattah Bani Pasha, the minister between Egypt and the Ottoman Empire, has passed away. News of this loan is circulating in Egypt, though it is said that due to the high mortality rate among the Egyptian populace, Pasha has postponed any salary increases.

Treaties of Turkey and Germany

News from Istanbul indicates that the German Parliament has confirmed the Indian agreement and that German refugees are being welcomed into the Pashtun territories. Specifically, the Pashtun community has received a loan of forty million pounds (thirty million from Booker) to be distributed by December 2nd, 1920. This Turkish investment will be channeled into the local economy during the winter months.

Bulgarian Circumstances

*Al-Ahram* reports that Bulgaria is experiencing severe flooding and devastation due to heavy rains, resulting in widespread displacement and destruction.

News from Russia and France and the Question of Concord

Discussions continue between Russia and France regarding a possible treaty, though it is reported that Russia’s conditions are less favorable than those of France. The Russian government is attempting to improve its position and is engaging in discussions with French representatives. It is anticipated that the terms of this agreement will be outlined in the coming months, with payments likely to be made in installments during the winter months.

German Occupation of British Territories

Berlin reports that in the areas of Germany now under British occupation, a new government is being established. The German populace is preparing to support this new government, which is currently engaged in negotiations with the British and French governments regarding peace terms.

Reports from British India

Numerous reports have been circulating regarding the situation in British India, particularly concerning the elections for the Congress party in Bengal.  The advice of Congress leaders is that Pashtun elders and Congress ministers should engage in discussions with their counterparts in India to address widespread corruption and injustices within the region, particularly among the Hindu population.

British India – News

The Congress and Pashtun leaders in Bengal are discussing a proposal from Congress leaders that Pashtun elders and Congress ministers should engage in discussions with their Indian counterparts to address widespread corruption and injustices within the region, particularly among the Hindu population.

German Occupation of British Territories

Berlin reports that in the areas of Germany now under British occupation, a new government is being established. The German populace is preparing to support this new government, which is currently engaged in negotiations with the British and French governments regarding peace terms.

Reports from the Frontier

Efforts are being made by the Pashtun community in the border region between Afghanistan and British India to resolve disputes and foster cooperation.

German Occupation of British Territories

Berlin reports that in the areas of Germany now under British occupation, a new government is being established. The German populace is preparing to support this new government, which is currently engaged in negotiations with the British and French governments regarding peace terms.

Pashtun Loyalties

It is reported that in Bengal, Congress leaders advise Pashtun elders and Congress ministers to consider the widespread corruption and injustices within the region, particularly among the Hindu population, and to engage in discussions with their Indian counterparts to address these issues.

British India – News

Congress and Pashtun leaders in Bengal are discussing a proposal from Congress leaders that Pashtun elders and Congress ministers should engage in discussions with their Indian counterparts to address widespread corruption and injustices within the region, particularly among the Hindu population.

German Occupation of British Territories

Berlin reports that in the areas of Germany now under British occupation, a new government is being established. The German populace is preparing to support this new government, which is currently engaged in negotiations with the British and French governments regarding peace terms.

Pashtun Loyalties

It is reported that in Bengal, Congress leaders advise Pashtun elders and Congress ministers to consider the widespread corruption and injustices within the region, particularly among the Hindu population, and to engage in discussions with their Indian counterparts to address these issues.

180.right:
Floods in Bulgaria

News has reached us of a devastating flood in Bulgaria’s territory, caused by torrential rains, which has left countless Bulgarians homeless and tragically claimed many lives.

Droos and Farang and Maad

As in previous times, discussions continue regarding the reconciliation between Russia and Farang. It is reported that Russia’s conditions are markedly unfavorable compared to those of Farang, and a series of negotiations is underway between representatives of England, Lithuania, and the people of Farang – a series intended to initiate discussions regarding the Russian government’s requests, with the aim of improving the political and economic state of Russia. These discussions are being held with the representatives of Farang, and it is anticipated that the Russian government’s desires will be received.

Germany Under British Occupation

Reports from Berlin indicate that a new government is being established in the regions under British military control, and the German populace is preparing to support this endeavor. The German government is currently engaged in discussions with the UK and France regarding peace, and it is expected that these regions, formerly under German authority, will soon see the commencement of this new government.

Belton Trustees

Much has been disseminated regarding Afghanistan in recent days, including discussions concerning the positions of various nations regarding the Afghan government and the arrival of diplomats from Kabul, amongst other matters. These discussions have centered on the negotiations between the British and Afghan representatives.

Office Recommendations

Concerning the Palestinian government, an announcement has been made regarding the allocation of land to certain refugees and the implementation of measures to safeguard the land rights of the Palestinian people. A series of decrees have been issued by the Palestinian government to facilitate the practical execution of these measures.

The Arabs Educate Their Followers

It is reported that the British government is discreetly pursuing the employment of certain spies amongst some Arab tribes, with the aim of securing certain advantages. This action has been met with strong reaction from the Arab tribes, who are now taking steps to protect their territories. Consequently, a number of tensions have arisen between the Arab tribes and the British intelligence services.

181.right:
Pashtun Ottomans

Concerning the election of the Congress ministers in Bengal, the Congress leaders advised that the high officials of Pakhtunkhwa and the Congress ministers should deliberate on the prevalence of corruption and discord amongst the Muslim community and other populations within the region – a matter of significant concern for the populace regarding education in India.

India News

Regarding the discussions between the Congress and the Pashtun people of Bengal, the Congress leaders proposed that they undertake concerted efforts to safeguard the rights of the Pashtun people within the region, and to establish a distinct political entity and legal framework for their community – a proposition that warrants serious consideration by both parties.  The Congress ministers and their associates are diligently pursuing this course of action.

Pashtun Ottomans

It is proposed that the universities should collaborate on joint discussions, a measure that will likely provoke opposition from the government.

India News

Concerning the rights of the Pashtun people, there exists a perceived tension between the ministers and the populace regarding the establishment of a distinct political structure within the universities – a matter of ongoing debate and contention.

Border News

In Bengal, the frontier is engaged in efforts to bridge the historical divide between Afghanistan and India.

In the border region, the universities are the focus of ongoing discussions regarding the establishment of a separate state, with both sides seeking to secure the rights and interests of their respective populations.

182.right:
The Fifth Trustee

The decree of the conference’s presidency is for the people of the border region.
I offer my assistance for this conference, and I pray to God for blessings,
commencing this document’s preparation and ensuring its fair distribution.
Let us wear uniform attire and strive for justice in all matters.

The success of the Provincial Political Conference
occurred on the first day of July, during the month of pilgrimage in Mecca.
Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan, Mian Jafar Shah Sahib, Arbab Khan Sahib, and numerous loyal servants participated,
covering the districts of Pakhtunkhwa and Mardan, as well as other regions,
spreading the word throughout the nation and promising a better future.

Mourning for Jan Khan
News reached me of the passing of Jan Khan, after many years of hardship and tranquility.
The Minister of Anzal and Sahib Khan, both esteemed figures,
expressed their grief and honored his memory through widespread publications in Pashto.
Despite the challenges of widespread circulation, we continued to publish extensively,
recognizing the importance of his legacy.
