215.right:
21 July 1938

Because the body of the Palestinian people
And the Council of Elders gathers in Peshawar. The learned men of Kabul
Issue this bulletin, for it is not always a relentless assault by the oppressor. Whenever God’s displeasure descends, He appoints a judge to render His decree. Therefore, let us be vigilant and prudent, and let every Hitler and Mussolini cease their futile schemes. The Christians exploit the Muslims, and the Germans seek to undermine the flourishing of Islam in the East – in Syria, Tunis, and Algeria. Japan, too, stirs unrest in the mosques. This is not the way forward; rather, it is the progress of this land, and the ambitions of the Atal and the Germans to seize control of Mecca. But these schemes will inevitably fail before the just judgment of God. Let us pray, “O Allah, reveal to us the truth and grant us the strength to follow it, and reveal to us falsehood as falsehood and grant us the ability to avoid it.” O Allah, guide us and make us among Your righteous servants, and deliver us from this British government.

According to the guidance of this esteemed scholar, he is currently a student at Darul Uloom Deoband.

21 July 1938

Yet, the Christian world is in decline, while Islam faces destruction. If the eyes of the Ehsan – the learned men – are open to this reality, and history is recorded in this pen, then it is because the world is reaching the zenith of civilization and claims to be the pinnacle of refinement. The result of the Muslims’ generosity is this white paper, nurtured in a way that benefits the people of Peshawar. It is a testament to the excellence of Pashto, meticulously documented in this publication. No example of such a comprehensive work exists. It contains all the knowledge, all the accounts, and all the details that have been gathered from various sources.  It is a treasure trove for those seeking to understand the history and culture of the Pashtun people.  This publication is a lament for the fate of the Pashtun nation.

Price: One anna.

Available in small booklets. Well-printed and bound.

Issued by the Peshawar office of the  Orient Govemment Security Life Assurance Company Limited.

The company’s representative and other officials will be available for insurance inquiries.

The insurance policy is affixed to the cover of the book.

Mr. Khalil Khaliq, printed in the Afghan Electric Press, Peshawar, and entrusted to the care of Mr. Qars and the writing of the text.

Pashto is the language of the enlightened.

217.right:
Pashtun

Let me restrain myself, even in solitude.
If a slave should perish, his possessions will be dispersed.
May he be a true prophet within Pashtun society –
Aunt Pashtun’s presence is absent from the Jat community.
Drop by drop, I have endured… the burdens of the English.
I offer my sincere gratitude.
Or perhaps, I will establish a legacy like the gardens of Eden belonging to Malik Bagh.
Or, I shall print a Pashtun coat.

[stamp] Pashtun of Kabul

219.right:
Arakat 39

What is to be done of the Afghan people?
Do not display dishonor in any land amongst those of the faith; what is an Afghan to do?
Are they content with slavery?
There is no state, no art, no honor – what is an Afghan to do?
To be found in such humiliation – what is an Afghan to do?

Do not harbor shame amongst two beloved ones – what is the hope of Rahman?

The noble Akhlaq has forgotten the virtues of Pak Habib – what is the hope of intercession?

Today, in this national assembly, you are a minority – what is the wonder of the youth and the honor of the nation?

You made a promise for the era of Pashtun – today, I must follow the work of the Qur’an.

Strong principles should not be sacrificed for the sake of appearance – what is wrong with Bacha Khan?

He respected the tribe; there is no opposition – the Khana seized the kingdom and ruled against other Amirs and Khans. Azizur Rahman Sahib, not Aziz.

Arakat 1939

Volume Pashtun Number (20)

According to October 1939, corresponding to the 12th of Jumada al-Thani, 1358 Hijri

Regarding the Western Political Conference;
Speech of Fakhr Afghan

Esteemed individuals! I am present today in a public assembly, a humble participant.
I have heard – it is a chilling tale, a sorrowful account amongst the Afghans – many are unaware.
It is a lamentable circumstance that such grievances perpetually arise in the course of self-directed endeavors. God knows best.
It is not known – you will inquire. What is the cause of this sorrow? So in meetings and Jirgas, one does not participate? This is the reason – there is a difference amongst the participants. Because I am a participant in it. It is a good time when such separations occur, for we and you share the same thought. Because the distance between us is not great. Therefore, the separation is still evident. I was not happy with this idea. I will come to you and see you. What are you doing in the garden?
It is said. It will not be. Come here. We are the house of the house, and the protector of the other’s house. What is to be done of the Amirs and Khans?
Azizur Rahman Sahib, not Aziz.

220.right:
Arakat 1

A pure spiritual and moral movement is a vital undertaking. It is not found in mere imagery, but rather in the practice of good deeds and service to God. There are many forms of this service. I acknowledge this. I believe that all people do not share a single thought or belief, and I do not wish to impose my own views upon them. However, my thoughts and beliefs are not bound by the past. Offering service to others is a modest request, and God’s service is not a simple matter of quantity, but of principle. Now, the act of service is far more complex than mere service. Each person should have their own high ideals. Therefore, each should be happy and content in their own way, and each should be a little less burdened.

It is a great burden for a nation to strive for greatness. And if a nation lacks good qualities, we do not have a nation. But if a new vision exists and the nation desires prosperity and well-being, it is necessary to look to the needs of others. It is not a matter of competing desires, but of mutual support. Therefore, the goal of the nation is not apparent. I have simply striven for the well-being of the people. A little service is necessary, and I will do it, however small. I wish to avoid excessive demands. Let us all be helped by the life of the people. Rather, it is the death of oneself and one’s place. 221.right:
Here’s an improved English translation of the current spread, aiming for fluency, coherence, faithfulness, and an appropriate early 20th-century register:

The absence of genuine spirit and moral conviction, they take pride in the Pashtun characteristic that distinguishes them – that no other people possess such qualities. Tranquility and a straightforward nature are inherent to them, a defining trait of their character. Whenever we commend their devotion to God and service to humanity, they hold this as a source of pride. It is a mark of their people, one they boastfully proclaim above other nations. These virtues are not innate, but rather, they represent a commendable aspect of their moral disposition. As Pashtuns understand that serving God is inextricably linked to serving their fellow men, they counsel others to eschew animosity and envy, fostering instead love and concord within their hearts. Yet, sadly, this Pashtun inclination towards benevolence and righteousness is confined solely to their own people. Were they to possess such a passion for the welfare of their nation as they do for their kin, they would swiftly rise to prominence amongst the world’s peoples. Pashtuns are thoughtful and discerning individuals, believing that acting upon this principle would constitute a significant service to their community.

A sincere and moral movement is rooted in their hearts. They take pride in the Pashtun quality that sets them apart – that no other people share this characteristic. Calmness and simplicity are natural to them, a defining feature of their character. When we express our gratitude for their devotion to God and service to humanity, they hold this as a point of pride. This is a trait of their people, one they proudly mention above other nations. These qualities are not inherent, but rather, they represent a commendable aspect of their moral character. As Pashtuns recognize that serving God is synonymous with serving their people, they counsel others to avoid harboring resentment and instead cultivate love and peace within their hearts. However, it is a lamentable fact that this Pashtun inclination towards goodness and righteousness is limited to their own people. If they were to possess such a passion for the welfare of their nation as they do for their relatives, they would quickly ascend to the forefront of the world’s nations. Pashtuns are thoughtful and discerning individuals, believing that acting upon this principle would constitute a significant service to their community.

222.right:
Arakat

Reflect upon yourself and your community.

Each of you should dedicate a portion of your nights to the service of God. Some among you harbor the misguided belief that God’s favor rests solely with you. As I have previously stated, our efforts to serve God in this way have yet to bear fruit.

Listen closely, friends! You will recall that in 1939, as we all faced hardship together, the telephone lines were strained and preparations were underway. The entire country and all its people were united, driven by our faith and trust in God’s servant. We held firm to our convictions. Let us now strive to strengthen these convictions and work diligently for the betterment of the nation. However, today, a troubling absence of love is evident, and a lack of unity. Why is this so? Because we, too, have turned away from God’s service.

The situation of God’s servants today is perplexing. It is as if they have lost their way, as if they are adrift. It is as if they are seeking solace in fleeting pleasures and worldly desires. It is as if they are unable to find comfort in God’s presence.  Why should God abandon us, who have turned away from His service? Why should He inflict suffering upon us, when we have forsaken His guidance?  And why do we, burdened by hardship, continue to bear the weight of misfortune?  Let us remember that true faith is not merely a fleeting thought, but a steadfast commitment.

Nine August 1939

9

...and do not seek to impose your rights upon others. My meaning is this: if you do not desire to fulfill your duties, or if you are unwilling to act with compassion, then you are not fit to serve as ministers. My role is to serve you, the minister. And if not, then I have dedicated my life to this service, for the sake of God. Therefore, wherever you find opportunity, strive to serve others.  It is not appropriate for you to seek positions of power or influence, nor should you expect more from your ministers than they are capable of providing.  Let your decisions be guided by wisdom and patience.  The mistakes of the past should serve as a lesson, not a cause for complaint.  Do not dwell on errors, but rather focus on improvement.

This is the essence of the matter.  In the police force, maintain harmony and mutual respect among all.  Avoid unnecessary disputes and disagreements.  The excessive demands of your superiors will only lead to further unrest.  Therefore, let your decisions be guided by fairness and justice.  The minister’s judgment is final, and you should accept it with humility and patience.  Let not petty grievances cloud your judgment.

Curse those who are unable to understand the situation.  I have expressed my concerns to you, my servants, that if you wish to restore the current state of affairs, you must recognize your own limitations and refrain from placing undue expectations on your ministers.  Do not seek to burden them with excessive demands.  If you find that your ministers are not suitable for the task, then you should not seek to replace them.  Let your actions be guided by prudence and wisdom.

Finally, I wish to convey this message: I will serve you as a servant of God. If you find yourself in need of assistance, you must seek it from the path of Pashtun service to God.  Do not seek to serve your own people alone.  Serve your country and your nation for the sake of God.  August 19, 1939

224.right:
Pashtun Amatori 13 Arakst 39

The work station’s meetings are forceful and resolute. (Motivator Sher Muhammad Jalal)
Furthermore, the arrangements for the border work station are efficient and well-managed. According to the Red Congress Election Manifesto, individuals are not obligated to pay exorbitant rents; rather, the middle ground should be sought, and land should be leased to those who are capable of cultivating it. And the cultivated land should be theirs.

Another matter concerning the border work station is the speech delivered at its meetings. It has been twenty years since the government has been in power without incurring any cost; this must be maintained. That is, the border line must remain secure. And his responsibility is to ensure it. (Promoting Judge Muhammad Hussain Sahib)

Another matter concerning the border work station is the organization of its meetings. The daily life of the Taminjhar (likely referring to a specific location) is to be managed by men, not by the wealthy.  It is not appropriate to increase wealth through exploitation; rather, news should be disseminated carefully and decisions should be made with deliberation. (Promoting Judge Muhammad Hussain Sahib)

Another matter concerning the border work station is the Ministry of Border. The area along the border was prepared in 1886.  Landowners should not confiscate land, and it should not be seized.  Furthermore, the arrangements for arrival should be made with sincerity and a clear purpose. This is a mature and well-managed estate, and its ownership should be protected. Please forgive me for doubting this.

It has been decided that the two-border ministry should be established and sent to the Ministry of Borders and the Border. And let us strive to implement it.

I will not abandon your request, nor will I neglect your service.  May you remain healthy and free to pursue your work.

226.right:
Conditions Abroad

On France, Britain, and Germany: From London

The French Prime Minister, Monsieur Musiolmunzel, states that for the public good, “Musiomunzelbar” is necessary. Furthermore, the Minister of Motors, Monsieur Adina, expresses concern regarding the influence of the Nazis.

A statement has been issued by the French newspaper, asserting that Monsieur Musiolmunzel is a threat to European stability. Motorists are being urged to intervene and control speed – an action currently being undertaken. The newspaper reports accusations leveled against the ministers and officials, alleging that they are following the directives of the Nazis.

Palestine Affairs: Recent news from Palestine indicates that a British official, Mr. Chalid Low, is importing new goods – the precise nature of which remains unclear. The situation is considered precarious, with security and danger present on all sides.

The Chinese Minister of War has issued a proclamation, declaring that China is not prepared for war, despite mobilizing its military forces. The current focus is on training and equipping troops for deployment to mountainous regions. It is believed that China is capable of defending itself, relying on patience and strategic planning.

Russia and England Treaty: The Prime Minister of France, in response to a question, stated that a treaty with Russia is unlikely to be concluded before the end of the current assembly. Furthermore, Germany has reportedly loaned money to Russia, “that Berlin and perhaps even the whole of Germany will participate in the war.” The American President, Mr. Priddin Ruit, has announced the United States’ intention to take part in the war, citing the need to prevent it. He has also expressed concern regarding the policies of the German and Italian leaders.

The situation in Czechoslovakia is reported to be stabilizing, with the Czech and Slovak nations uniting. There are also reports of a growing alliance between Turkey, Romania, France, and Britain. However, the reality is that both Mussolini and Hitler are pursuing their own agendas. It is believed that the British Chamber of Commerce is increasingly influenced by the ideas of Nietzsche, particularly his concept of the Übermensch. The rising power of Germany is causing concern, and the decline of British morality is becoming increasingly apparent. It is regrettable that errors in judgment and political discord are undermining the nation’s strength. It is crucial to avoid repetition of past mistakes and to maintain a clear and focused course. The current situation demands careful consideration and prudent action.

په پای کې، د جرګې غړو په ځینو مهمو مسائلو باندې د نظرونو تبادله وکړه او د خپلو تجويزونو په هکله يې پرېکړې وکړې. د جرګې د غړو له خوا د خپلو وړاندیزونو په اړه د حکومت او د جرګې د غړو ترمنځ د خبرو اترو بهير دوام وموند. د حکومت له لوري د جرګې غړو ته د خپلو پرېکړو په هکله ډاډ ورکړل شو.
په پای کې، د جرګې غړو د ځينو مهمو مسائلو په اړه د حکومت د کړنو په اړه د خپل رضایت څرګندونه وکړه. د جرګې غړو د خپل کار په پای کې د خپلو تجويزونو په هکله د خپلو نظرونو د څرګندولو سره د جرګې غونډه پای ته ورسېده.
د جرګې غونډه د ځينو مهمو پرېکړو په ترڅ کې پای ته ورسېده. د جرګې د غړو له خوا د خپلو وړاندیزونو په اړه د حکومت د پرېکړو د پلي کولو په اړه د هڅو غوښتنه وشوه.
د جرګې د غړو په وینا، د جرګې د پرېکړو پلي کول د هېواد د پرمختګ لپاره مهم دي.
د جرګې د غونډو د پای ته رسېدو په ترڅ کې، د جرګې غړو د خپلو تجويزونو په هکله د حکومت د کړنو په اړه د خپلو نظرونو څرګندونه وکړه. د جرګې غونډه د يوې مثبتې پایلې سره پای ته ورسېده.

227.right:
Frontier News

A Millennium of Years: Pir Shah Sahib, a hundred and ten silver coins, convened a Jirga of one thousand and a loan of two hundred and seventy thousand from Kabul Khan Sahib, arrived in Peshawar on the twenty-ninth of July and returned to the twenty-seventh of July from Abbottabad.

Election in Kabul: The district of Abdul Majeed Khan had no assembly present. The Congress has addressed Abdul Majeed Khan Sahib, and his letter has been received. Mr. May Khan is his lawyer and has been appointed.

Mirdad Khan’s Absence: Mirdad Khan Sahib, currently absent from America, has engaged in some questionable service. However, the Punjab government has placed him under house arrest for two months, and he is currently receiving no payment.

Gandhi’s Retirement: On the twenty-seventh of July, Mata Gandhi departed from Abbottabad for the army.

Doctor Khan’s Leave: Recent reports have circulated falsely claiming that Dr. Khan Sahib is taking leave. This is inaccurate.

Pashtun Name Approval: The Pashtun name of Kabul has been formally approved for this month’s payment.

19
18
1 August 1931

Today’s World

The World’s Dark Road: The world is steeped in sorrow, yet hope remains. This day marks the key to the world’s affairs, a turning point after the recent upheaval of the Silver Revolution. Mussolini, emboldened by his successes in Italy, has issued a declaration to the British Empire, seeking support for a role in the coming conflict. News reports indicate that the American President has declared a policy of intervention in the war, though the precise nature of this commitment remains unclear.

In London, the news is dominated by reports of the ongoing crisis. The city is gripped by anxieties regarding the potential for aerial bombardment, with the rooftops of buildings now covered in sand to protect against the threat of falling debris.

The Anglo-Japanese Agreement: London reports that Japanese Foreign Minister Katayama is reassuring the British that relations between Japan and Britain remain amicable.

German Population Concerns: There are growing concerns regarding the German population, with speculation about their preparedness for war. It is uncertain what role the German people will play, though they appear to be mobilizing.

228.right:
Pashtun Aganzi

August 1939

The world mourned the dead, yet dreamt of better days. Let us see what fate holds in store. This day marks the turning point for the Jirga in Chalam Khawasal – a pivotal moment for the world. Following the events in Spain, Mussolini spoke of grand designs, venturing into the realms of Britain’s diminished power. He spread rumors of his ambitions, fueled by a misguided belief in his own strength. The British government, wary of Mussolini’s influence, imprisoned him for months, a testament to their apprehension.

News arrived that Dr. Khan was on leave, departing from Abatabad for Ard. However, reports circulated falsely claiming Dr. Khan was on leave due to illness.  The Pashtun pen celebrated the recognition of Khan Sahib’s name in the Kabul press. This recognition was expected to bring prestige and respect for the duration of the publication.

Mirdad Khan of the Mula region, a prominent figure, had been residing in America, and was unjustly detained by the Punjab government for two months.  His situation remained unresolved, causing considerable concern.

The situation in Abatabad remained tense.  The government’s decisions were causing confusion and hindering progress.  It was crucial to avoid repeating past mistakes and to ensure sound counsel within the government.  The unrest in the region, stemming from the discontent of the populace, demanded careful consideration.  The British authorities, observing this turmoil, sought to maintain order and stability.
228.left:
The Russian world stirred with unease.  Let us not be deceived by the rhetoric of the powerful.  The rise of Islam in Chhil Pahn signifies a shift in the times.  Consider this: if you seek solace, turn inward, towards your own heart.  However, the news reached us that the current year is also marked by the decline of the old order.  The Czechs and Poles are poised to join forces, while Turkey, Romania, and France are contemplating a unified front.  The British Parliament, observing this gathering of nations, hesitated to fully embrace it, wary of repeating past errors.  The truth is that both Mussolini and Hitler are driven by similar ambitions.  Chamberlain, the British Foreign Secretary, is increasingly seen as a puppet of Nietzsche, absorbing the philosophies of the German intellectual.  Art finds little resonance with this approach.  General Franco, a figure of strength in Spain, has solidified Germany’s position there.  Therefore, the decline of British ethics, its moral decay and political instability, is evident.  The mistakes of the past have led to a decline in the quality of governance and a loss of public trust.  It is imperative to learn from these errors and avoid repetition.  The situation in the region is further complicated by the unrest among the populace, fueled by grievances and a lack of opportunity.  The Russian influence, a constant threat, seeks to exploit these divisions.

---

It is known that the British will soon send a delegation to the United Kingdom.  Neither is his modesty, nor is he overly concerned with the affairs of the surrounding areas, as he has two kingdoms.  He has been told that the Spanish revolution has been completed.  After that, the people of the old world have been defeated.  After that, the good people are not harmed.  Free people are valued more than gold.  They are the foundation of a nation.  The British government now sees Russia as a threat.  The Romanians, Greeks, and Turks are all under its shadow.  It is said that the German Emperor wants to unite these nations.  However, the British are hesitant to fully embrace this alliance, fearing a repeat of past mistakes.  The truth is that both Mussolini and Hitler are driven by similar ambitions.  Chamberlain, the British Foreign Secretary, is increasingly seen as a puppet of Nietzsche, absorbing the philosophies of the German intellectual.  Art finds little resonance with this approach.  General Franco, a figure of strength in Spain, has solidified Germany’s position there.  Therefore, the decline of British ethics, its moral decay and political instability, is evident.  The mistakes of the past have led to a decline in the quality of governance and a loss of public trust.  It is imperative to learn from these errors and avoid repetition.  The situation in the region is further complicated by the unrest among the populace, fueled by grievances and a lack of opportunity.  The Russian influence, a constant threat, seeks to exploit these divisions.

229.right:
Pashtun Emanzani August 1939

Be sure to use it. Poland is full of land,
see in this, the Nazis have raised a new flag in the sky. After that,
Poland was honored by the white lights. After that,
they count, that the blood of righteous men is not harmed by falsehood. After that,
they are free men. They pay a high price for their blood,
satisfying addiction. Britain now dislikes Hitler and Germany,
listen! That’s why the governments of Europe are uneasy. To me, Poland, Romania, Greece, and Turkey are candlesticks –
they do not accept this. It is known that both Mussolini and Bonaparte. The story is that the English nation, Chamberlain, considers every small thing as if it were a great matter. They do not consider art to be of great value. After the white reign, General Franco is in power with Germany. So it is wrong for Britain’s ethics and politics to decline. The mistakes began, and the council’s decisions were flawed, and everything that is repeated should not be done again.  The situation is calm, and the people of the country are in a state of despair. The Russian threat is a concern for the entire world. Mussolini and Hitler are allies.

230.right:
Russia has been occupied with matters for days, so do not harbor ill will towards Russia for the world’s sake. Consider what the Muslims are like – five people venturing forth. Therefore, each man should seek to unite his soul in two hearts. It has rained and the earth is saturated; it is said that soon there will be two fires – Czechoslovakia and Poland will be consumed. The Prime Minister of Great Britain does not wish to lay the foundation for such a scheme, and God knows how much he will write to him regarding it. However, the new hero of Pizak and Tin Yeh is now running along Jinberlin Street, declaring that he openly accepts this Russian alliance. Great Britain and Russia are now friends with Romania, Greece, Poland, and a few others. Yet, in one way, Britain is delaying. It is not necessary for Churchill, the renowned leader of the Kanak river, to empty his coffers. For yesterday, news arrived that Great Britain and Russia are parting ways. Every hour, every moment, the world is changing.  The people of Hindustan are also in a state of anxiety.  Turkey and the Arab world are watching closely, seeking to understand the intentions of Japan.  They fear that Japan will encroach upon the territories of Afghanistan and Persia.  The Afghan people are now burdened with the weight of supplies and provisions, and the border remains closed. Japan seeks to dominate the region, and its influence extends far beyond.  It is feared that Japan will seek to seize control of China. Great Britain, on the other hand, is preparing to intervene in China alongside Japan, while Russia is observing the situation with concern.  The two nations are engaged in a delicate dance, each seeking to secure their interests.  The people of Afghanistan are burdened by the anxieties of the times, and the prospect of war weighs heavily on their minds.

231.right:
What do you do at this time? Because discussing such matters through the transmission of words is a waste of one’s strength. – John Bill. He also possessed seven cannons of great power – and if he swore an oath to a foreign power, he was content. – Indeed, many Muslims have pledged allegiance to the East. So, let each of you strive for your own sake, and for the sake of others. – Let your interests be aligned with the hearts of all. I have seen and heard that the people of the East, particularly those in Persia, are afraid of the British Empire, who wish to establish their rule over this land, and God forbid that they should be subjected to their tyranny. And may God protect us from their wickedness. However, let us build a new bridge on Jinberlin Street, and so on, as if this Russian influence is clearly evident. Britain says she is allied with Romania, Greece, Poland, and some others, but her power remains firm. Yet, it is not necessary to dismiss the counsel of Mr. Churchill, whose famous “Cabinet of Advisors” has also been dismissed. – This is because yesterday, the news of the past…

(Not Mr. Abdul Karim Khan Sahib)

Ashos concerning the revolution

It is a matter of regret,
A matter of sorrow regarding action,
It is a matter of regret,
In every place,

Dam Amanzi 29 August 1939

A History of the Pashtuns

The King of Iran appointed the Governor of Herat to the path of Kandahar in the form of his last year. Following this order, the Iranian
country prepared an army. The Mir was present before the Iranian Spa Mia Nohal, standing on the foot
as if the Pashtuns were valued. Against this army
the Pashtuns themselves brought their thousand-foot army to confront it. And this confrontation resulted in a victory for Baker. This day and month of murder
the Iranian army gave way, though they were always defeated everywhere. However, they were never resolved. The thousands of Iranians, the governor of Tabriz, Muhammad Khan
entered the field of the governor of Kandahar. And on the other hand, he was wearing only red Pashtun attire.
But the Pashtuns still prevailed. May God bless the Iranians
Rahmian and Murray were left on the field. And Muhammad Khan was killed and his month of blood debt was paid. This
attack replaced the Iranian army, but everywhere it resulted in defeat, and the Pashtuns’ thousand-foot army remained. The governor of Muhammad Khan of the tribe of Pashtun
entered the field of the governor of Kandahar of the Persian. And again the Pashtuns won. The Iranians prayed to God that they would not have the Pashtun thousand-foot army
to be left on the field. And Muhammad Khan was killed, four and three years old. The fury and anger of the King of Iran was very great on me.
And he intended to return to Loya Kandahar at this stage
to attack Iran’s Tul and So Bonks Ja Rajian in Kandahar. Sher said that Gorgin Khan was also Jarji. And that is exactly what India is
Bara Khusr Khan was appointed as the governor of Jhar Jaya in his place. Khusr Khan
after the final defeat of the King of Iran, the arrangement of the Iranian army was referred to him. And the order was given
that no matter what happens, Kandahar will be captured.
232.left:
Khsro Khan brought the entire treasury of Derish into his heart, and the order of the King he placed in his heart for the sake of a meeting with the tribe of Pashtun. So, the Derish was quiet, without any fundamental matter known, except that the governor of the Persian Jin Khan had a desire for power and strength in his heart, and without the opposition of a wise man, it was impossible to confront him. Therefore, he dismissed all his special spies who had learned that Derish was completely empty, and had not built any walls or fortifications – and he placed his entire army on the shoulders of his own people. This was his decision, a judgment in his heart – and as the Persian banner of the Sids did not belong to the banner of Khsro Khan, he rejoiced greatly and prepared himself for the meeting. The arrows and lessons of Khsro Khan were sent to his army – even though the number of his enemies was greater, their spirits were high and they were eager for freedom. The battle began, but in the end, the enemy could not sustain it. The defeat was pronounced. The Iranians, from the Sids, …

The school of the center of Egypt – and the news of the department of the government of the British came that the
Pashtun population in the hundreds of houses in the villages was also the reason
that the government intended to consider the tribal leaders and the great merchants as necessary people for the work. And accordingly, the work was started. The news was spread in the Mark – Entrelind and Diudp in the corner of the work, and it was not found to be a useful method. And we, the Pashtuns, made several proposals for the improvement of the work, which were accepted and we were happy to see them fulfilled. They proved that in the world of villages, it is not possible to achieve success without paying attention to the needs of the villagers. Until the time when these four things
these first considerations are not taken into account. Then the village will be in a state of distress – and the state of the people will be in a state of ruin. – And the work of the school should not be a place of learning, but a place of distraction and entertainment, and the dead of the village should not be in the shadow of the school – and the work of the village should not be restricted, but the people should be free from the burden of life – and when the work is done, the people’s life is not in the hands of the rich – then the work is done. The life of the people is not in the hands of the rich – then the work is done. The life of the people is not in the hands of the rich – then the work is done. 233.right: Khusro Khan carried the matter of Derish within his heart, and upon receiving the King’s decree, he entrusted the task of dealing with it to the leaders and prominent merchants of the Pashtun tribal lands. He then dismissed his secret agents, discovering that their intelligence was lacking and that they had not taken any action – a regrettable state of affairs.  He ordered his army to be placed under the command of his own trusted officers, intending to make a decisive judgment, and as Irani Farrokh’s lineage stemmed from the lineage of the Persian nobility, he rejoiced greatly and immediately ordered preparations to be made for a swift response.  His forces marched forth, engaging in battle with fervor and determination, yet ultimately, they were unable to withstand the enemy’s strength. The Persian forces, therefore, drove them from the field.

234.right:
Here’s a revised translation of the current spread, aiming for fluency, coherence, faithfulness, and an appropriate early 20th-century register:

The Village School

News from the central government’s official office indicated that eighty to ninety percent of the villages within the Pashtun region were now densely populated, a circumstance necessitating governmental action for reform.  Recognizing the importance of this issue, the leaders of the nation and the principal merchants were considered essential figures in addressing it, and work began accordingly.  Specifically, efforts were focused on the Mark, Atherland, and Diodap districts to alleviate the suffering caused by these conditions.  Attempts to implement effective solutions through conventional methods proved fruitless, yielding no tangible benefit.  Furthermore, the resources allocated to these endeavors were insufficient, and the results were disappointing – a thousand pelak (a unit of currency, presumably) was expended without success.  We, therefore, undertook the responsibility of initiating these reforms.

It became clear that the prevailing conditions in the world – a life of hardship and poverty – could not be overcome through conventional means.  Until these fundamental issues were addressed, any attempts to improve the situation would be futile.  Three distinct arts were considered vital in this context: the cessation of village-based trading, the avoidance of dealings with the zamindari (landlords), and the cultivation of the land itself.  Closing the villages to trade and preventing the exploitation of the land were deemed crucial steps.  When the lives of the villagers were threatened by poverty, work must be halted.  The wisdom of the age, exemplified by figures like Socrates, demonstrated the importance of addressing these underlying problems before pursuing further advancements.  It is therefore essential to understand that these practices – adultery and the misuse of the air – are detrimental and should be avoided.  The message is clear: these are harmful habits that must be eradicated.

August 1920

The foundation of knowledge, the “School of Jhargand,” was established in Egypt, and a vast collection of scholarly works was gathered there.  Representatives from various kingdoms traveled to Egypt to study, and during this period, many were considered students of learning.  For generations, these individuals had been compelled to lock themselves away from the world, dedicating their lives to this solitary pursuit.  It was determined that the two of them had closed the situation, my friend.  And education was a necessity – this was the intention of the great Maurya dynasty, particularly in Taxila and Ajain.  Large educational institutions, known as madrassas, were established, surpassing the quality of those found elsewhere.  Further writings will be produced, and the cost of education will be borne by the community.  It was declared that the pursuit of a simple life – a life of service to one’s neighbors, cultivating the land, and maintaining a clean environment – was preferable to the pursuit of wealth.  The village’s prosperity depended on these practices.  Conversely, the opposition lamented the lack of progress, claiming that the men were now employed in the back of the thunder, seeking employment.  Each man spent a life in the city to become a capable person, eager to make a boji (a small coin) on the head of Rs (rupees).  The villagers, even those with modest means, contributed to the education of the village schools, fostering a spirit of cleanliness, order, and community.

235.right: The people of Peshawar declare their desire for freedom. They believe that the moral standards of their teachers and elders are paramount, and that these principles should guide their actions. They urge the government to recognize the importance of education and to support schools, ensuring that future generations receive a proper education.  They express a hope that the government will prioritize the welfare of the people and strive for a just and prosperous future, recognizing the need to address the grievances of the populace and to foster unity and stability within the nation.  They pray that freedom will be established in India, and that figures like Maulana Abdul Mannan, who champion the cause of liberty, will continue to inspire the people.  This declaration, issued on August 1939, reaffirms the commitment of the people of Peshawar to the pursuit of freedom and justice.

236.right:
August 1939

The Pashtun people, when facing unemployment, declare their desire for freedom.
They believe that both visible and hidden forms of religious observance are merely tools for deceiving others, used by the ignorant and the arrogant. These practices, they claim, are undertaken solely to mislead the faithful and the righteous.  They urge their students to seek refuge under their protection and to provide them with sustenance for their lives.  The loyalists of the Taliban, zealous in their devotion, believe that freedom is a sacred right, and they strive to uphold justice and morality in schools and in their daily lives, constantly seeking to improve themselves and their abilities. However, they are often consumed by vanity and self-importance, perpetually seeking to impress others with their accomplishments.  Despite this, they remain steadfast in their commitment to self-improvement, diligently pursuing knowledge and virtue.  The Pashtun people, through their dedication and perseverance, strive to embody the highest ideals of character and integrity.  May God grant them the wisdom and guidance to establish a just and prosperous society in India, and may Mullah Abdul Manan be blessed with continued success in his efforts.

237.right:
The United Nation and Pride of Afghanistan
(Series)

And during this period, Abdul Ghaffar Khan was also a socialist, a commendable development. It is noteworthy that the Muslim population of this region, embracing socialist ideals, viewed the burgeoning political landscape of India with a critical eye, often aligning themselves with the socialist sentiments of figures like Jawaharlal Nehru, while simultaneously expressing concern over the subjugation and dependence of Indian Muslims.  Furthermore, prominent figures such as Khalid Aib Khanum, through her study of the God’s God Magari movement, ascertained that the pronouncements of Inftan Sahib held considerable merit. Consequently,

Fakhrafghan Sahib considered Abdul Ghaffar Khan a great socialist. - (2)

August 11, 1939

This land is home to a multitude of diverse Muslim tribes, alongside a significant Hindu population.  The value of this unity – the Muslim India – lies in the presence of various Islamic sects, a circumstance that, regrettably, fosters division amongst the Muslim populace of Hindustan.  This fear of sectarianism, a persistent concern, has shaped the course of events within the region, and the anxieties of the learned scholars of India are palpable.  It is a matter of considerable import to consider this situation, particularly given the prevalent prejudice against Muslims within the broader Indian populace.  The focus should therefore be directed towards the betterment of the Indian people – the Muslim community and the broader Indian nation – through pragmatic reforms in matters of governance, economics, education, and beyond.  Let it not be said that you are fostering animosity amongst the populace, for the well-being of the Indian people and the Muslim community of Hindustan is paramount.  The formation of political parties and organizations is essential, but should be undertaken with a clear understanding of the prevailing economic conditions and the needs of the people.  The establishment of schools, undertaken in a measured and thoughtful manner, serves as a vital step in the consolidation of the Muslim community’s identity and progress.  This constructive endeavor contributes to the overall strength of the national community, as championed by Abdul Ghaffar Khan in the border region. It is a curious observation that the Muslim population of Hindustan is currently experiencing a period of considerable hardship, and those who aspire to serve the common good and dedicate themselves to the service of the people find their efforts met with suspicion and hostility amongst the populace. - (further…)

238.right:
Pashtun Amanat Z

The distinctiveness and status of the Pashtun people have been entirely diminished. The national Kaab, and all other endeavors, should be undertaken within the gatherings of political factions. The protection of Muslim laborers in India is a matter of concern, and they are preoccupied with safeguarding their own interests. However, those who harbor fantasies of a solitary nation must now contribute generously to the construction of a civilized society, maintaining their separate identity and cultivating strength within their own territories.

Another letter arrived from a fellow friend, revealing that he hailed from the heart of India. He declared his name to be of considerable value. For we Muslims, lacking any understanding of worldly affairs, are utterly ignorant. The soil there consumes and devours. We possess no capacity to interpret such matters. The Congress, alas, is populated by a small number of individuals who genuinely envision the creation of a purely idealistic nation. To secure the interests of the Muslims, the Congress must truly grasp the reality of these aspirations. Historians have misrepresented these schemes, presenting them in a distorted light to the Muslim populace. Such considerations are now deeply pondered by the people. In this era, it is a worthwhile pursuit to listen and observe the affairs of India. After all, Muslims are striving to forge a united nation.

August 1920

Madhu organizes the Muslim nation. The populace, divided by sectarianism, welcomes the inclusion of those who seek to improve the conditions of the land. It is a costly endeavor, a generous offering to the betterment of all inhabitants of India.  The spiritual and mental state of the Muslim population in Ahad Wistan is lamentable – they are friendless and utterly misguided. Panjapal, within the homeland, is esteemed, and educated Muslims are employed. For to India, war threatens every undertaking. In such circumstances, the hopes of the Muslim communities of Hindustan are repeatedly dismissed. Let our efforts be directed solely towards the improvement of the common Muslim populace – their economic, educational, and other conditions. Do not be disheartened by the hostility of the populace; remember that the common inhabitants of Hindustan are not worthy of comparison to the vastness of the world.  Closing the economic situation would inflict further harm upon the Muslim communities, hindering their efforts to form congregations. It is necessary to establish associations for the improvement of the villages.  Building schools in a modest manner is essential for the organization of the Muslim community, a constructive undertaking undertaken by Abdul Ghaffar Khan on the border. It is a curious observation that the Muslims of Hindustan are currently experiencing financial hardship, and those who aspire to serve the common Muslims are held in high esteem among the populace (more...).

239.right:
Pashtun Almanzo
62
Life insurance offers a reliable means to secure one’s financial future. It provides a substantial sum to address unforeseen hardships. Throughout India, and indeed amongst one’s own family and friends, the practice of insuring one’s life against misfortune is commonplace. The underlying principle rests upon the exchange of paper – a tangible representation of one’s security.

Do not hesitate, and do not delay in procuring Oriental Balinese insurance. The Pantel Security Life Insurance Company, established in 1334, provides coverage for various circumstances. This policy is designed to safeguard against unforeseen events.

The Secretary of the Bhumi Dahakrishna Branch writes to the Central Office, requesting correspondence.

Pashtun Chanda
(Advertising and Rates)

For the benefit of the common populace, the following rates are published: Two rupees and four annas per month, or two rupees and four annas annually. For advertisements in Pashtun, the rates are: Four rupees and six annas per year, or one rupee and two annas per issue.  For foreign publications, the rate is two and two nights per issue.

An example and account of the esteemed Hakeem Abdul Khaliq, author of “Shahid Sakina” and “Khushi Dandan,” is presented here. This section details the collaborative efforts of the four checkpoints, uniting in a shared endeavor. It highlights the importance of mutual support and the restoration of harmony between men and women.  The methods for assisting women and men in need are outlined, reflecting the Pashtun tradition of aid and assistance.

Price: 2 identifiers per copy. Price: 2 identifiers per copy.

The administration’s cable can be obtained at two locations. The Manager of the Pashtun Literary Kit, Khaliq Manzil, resides in Sharia Shahr, Khalum, Peshawar.

240.right:
Pashtun

Indeed, God dwells upon the grave,
And if my humble servant, Ghulam Mard, consumes sustenance,
Then his own blood stains his garments,
Turning my life’s stream a weary yellow.
I languished as a slave in a foreign land,
O Lord, I yearn for a realm of Eden, free from shame.
Or, should fate decree my path cross any street…

[Margin]

242.right:
Pashtun Emanji

5
21 August 1939
(22)
Volume
Pashtun Emanji Number
Corresponding to 7th Jumada al-Thani 1358 AD

The Life of a Slave

It is impossible for a person to live as a slave; he is subjected to humiliation, degradation, and is considered a shameful figure. Slavery is a great misfortune and a source of immense sorrow. A slave is a pariah, shunned by his own people and by his enemies. He lives in constant fear and disgrace.

He is ashamed to appear in the world, and his life is a curse.  He is a disgrace to his family and his homeland.  In the land of slavery, there is no honor or dignity. The enslaved man is devoid of faith and morality. He is considered dishonorable and a disgrace to his own people.  He is a target of contempt and scorn for those who serve him.

He feels shame even when addressing his master, and he is forced to bow and scrape before him.  He laughs at the commands of others, and he is never allowed to find solace or peace.  He does not read the Qur’an, nor does he seek refuge in it.

(See the views of Syed Rasool Sahib printed within)

The slave’s existence is ultimately a wretched one.  He is a wretched creature, and his fate is bleak.  Adultery is a stain upon women, and the land of slavery is filled with corruption.  The enslaved man lacks all honor and dignity.  He is a victim of injustice and oppression.

He feels shame even when he is in the presence of his master.  He is treated with contempt and disdain.  The collar of disgrace is his constant companion, and his life is a torment.  He longs for a home and a family, but he is denied these blessings.

Heaven is a distant hope for the enslaved, and a hellish existence.  He is a disgrace to his own people and to the world.

Saying and Doing

It is said that even the lute cannot achieve its purpose; it is necessary to speak with wisdom on the occasion of playing the lute.  And what is the purpose of speaking about the world’s affairs?  But the power to act is far more important. It is not mentioned in the tenth part. For example, a man might say, “This is what I want, my Khan, and my land, and I wish my family to be happy and content.”  But he must be quiet and reserved, listening to the counsel of others.  May Ali swear by the floor – let the floor be a witness to his sincerity.

Let him restrain his desires and passions, and let him not engage in frivolous pursuits.  Let him not waste his time on idle gossip and meaningless chatter.  Let him not indulge in excessive drinking or gambling.  Let him not engage in quarrels and disputes.  Let him not neglect his family and his responsibilities.  Let him not be a source of shame and disgrace.  Let him not be a burden on his neighbors.

Let him cultivate good habits and virtues, and let him strive to improve himself.  Let him avoid idleness and laziness, and let him engage in productive work.  Let him not waste his time on frivolous pursuits.  Let him not indulge in excessive eating and drinking.  Let him not be envious of others.  Let him be grateful for what he has, and let him not complain.  Let him be kind and compassionate to all.

Let him not engage in warfare and bloodshed.  Let him not harbor animosity towards his neighbors.  Let him not engage in illicit sexual relations.  Let him be faithful to his wife.  Let him be a responsible and honorable husband.  Let him be a good father and a loving son.  Let him be a member of a strong and united community.

In short, let him strive to live a virtuous and honorable life, and let him be a role model for others.  Let him not merely speak of these things; let him put them into practice.  And let him not waste his time on empty words and meaningless chatter.  (Continued)

=== NEXT SPREAD ===

243.right:
21 August 1939

If one examines the Pashto script, it is undeniable that every man’s conduct and morals are a matter of concern. And another prohibits such behavior. To kill is a grave offense, a crime against one’s people, and a multitude of sins.  It is forbidden to engage in wrongdoing. Martyrdom unjustly is a major sin and should not be approached with disregard. Each individual should pay attention to their own character and moral training.  Avoid frivolous spending and unnecessary acquisitions; such behavior is a great sin and a waste of time.  Every person should strive for honest livelihood and abandon bad habits of drunkenness.  The love of daily life is seen as a sign of God’s grace, and to avoid all conflict.  The animosity between daughters does not fade, and community ethics prevent the formation of enmity.  Therefore, it is essential to strive for seclusion and contemplation in every way, and to practice it practically.  Empty verbal agreements are futile and a waste of time. (c)

This additional message (from the Secretary Group)
This message is intended for the future of the nation – a testament to the achievements of freedom and peace.  Let the Pashtun banner be raised without impediment, for the sake of the nation’s progress.  To further the development of the Pashtun people, let it be known that certain publications are being circulated discreetly.  For the sake of God, let us not consider the price of slaves as less than the value of their Islamic education.  Let the three stars of the heavens be a reminder of the importance of none, four, five, and the silver wire of the last cold. ---

244.right:
Pashtun Hope
The State of Nations Worldwide
Gathering of Turkish Troops – It is reported that the Turkish army is currently assembling in secret within traffic routes. Rumors suggest that responses are being sent to Albania via the Bulgarian press.

Egyptian-Turkish Relations – Indications suggest a strengthening of ties between Egypt and Turkey. It is said that monthly, Turkey sends a representative to Egypt, and the Turks are actively monitoring this exchange. This is being overseen by Harb Pasha, who is also present in Albania and other foreign locations.

Pahlawan Ken – A regrettable incident occurred in Shaho, where the facades of buildings were severely damaged, with numerous injuries sustained by those present. Officers report that this event was a sudden and unexpected occurrence. Foreign embassies, particularly the Japanese, were also affected, with reports of the Japanese embassy reaching a similar state of disrepair.

Announcement of the Poet’s Memorial Day – The Paktun Literary Council is organizing a memorial service for the esteemed poet, Khan Khuda Khan Khushal Khan Khatek, who passed away in Peshawar last month. The program will include readings of poetry and speeches, with particular emphasis on the need to promote the Pashto language, the rights and protection of the Pashtun people, the revival of Pashtun traditions, and the selection of new members for the Literary Council. The Paktun Newspaper will publish a special edition dedicated to this event, inviting all interested parties to contribute their thoughts and verses. – Abdul Hannan Dami, Secretary of the Literary Council.

245.right:
12
21 August 1939
News from the Frontier
Syed Jamaluddin Afghani’s Centennial – Punjab Muslim League
It has been decided by the Punjab Muslim League that the weekend newspapers in Swat and surrounding areas will be suspended due to the difficulties faced by Syed Jamaluddin Afghani, a respected elder.  This decision is a recognition of his longstanding service and dedication to the community.  The esteemed Syed Hussain Sahib Pashawari, a prominent figure in Peshawar, has been appointed to preside over this important occasion.

The Railway Telegraph Department is experiencing difficulties.  Throughout the provinces of India, telegraph workers are protesting against the government’s imposition of official announcements on telegraph lines.  These workers, considered vital to communication, are not viewed as mere messengers by the government, but as essential contributors to the nation’s infrastructure.

13
21 August 1939
The Ministry of Railways is not cooperating with the telegraph office.
Discussions regarding all the special places of work in India have not been completed, but a decision has been made that members of the Central Assembly, including English members, will not be included in this matter.  Any disagreements will be resolved through established procedures.  This decision is to be implemented during the Day of Protection, alongside the arrangements for the government’s special personnel and the construction of telegraph lines.

Announcement of the Poetry Contest
A memorial service will be held on Sunday in Peshawar for Khan Khuda Khan Khushal Khan Khattak, the revered leader of the Pashtun people, who is presently resting in the shrine and is known for his devotional music.  The Pashtun Literary Jirga will oversee the arrangements for this event, focusing on the following: (1) the need for the development of the Pashtun nation, (2) the protection of Pashtun rights, (3) the remembrance of Pashtun scholars, and (4) the selection of members for the Pashtun Literary Jirga.

In order to promote the Pashtun newspaper, the opportunity for readers to share their thoughts and poems will be provided on the first day of each week.  Readers are encouraged to submit their own topics and ideas, as they see fit.
– Abdul Hanan Dami, Secretary of the Literary Council of the New City, Hari

246.right:
Eighteenth Pashtun

14 
August 21, 1929 
Border News 
Closure of National Newspapers in Khyber Agency 
In August, Swat military newspapers were suspended in Khyber Agency due to accusations of oppression leveled against Fakir EP.  These accusations, stemming from the neglect of his property by the impoverished sir, were widely circulated by the enemy in general, appearing in most publications and fueling crime in the border region.  The year 1929 witnessed a particularly grim period, with thirty murders recorded during the pro-scal month (July).  However, in the 27th and 27th days, the events of the 125th and 37th days were also tragically reported. Trade in the fruit market along the border suffered a surge in violence during the month of pro-scal, with three deaths recorded.  Furthermore, a government official, Mr. Khan Muhammad Abbas Khan, Minister of Agriculture and Khan Amir, Parliamentary Secretary, was appointed by a single individual to investigate the matter, led by President Colonel Noble Sahib, Bati Kishora, and including officers Khan Sahib Ahab Ghulam Haider Khan and Haji Ghulam Sarwar Khan, all part of the administration.

15 
The Board of Directors for the DAEA was established for the purpose of advising the government on matters of domestic trade. Syed Faiz Sahib of Sadi was appointed to this role. This time, the Prime Minister’s papaur was in charge. Raja Manouchehr Khan, Um. L.A., upon receiving an invitation from the Prime Minister, was received with a large gathering of people eagerly awaiting his rest and peace at the Abjay Mainam School. The Prime Minister delivered a refreshing speech, outlining the tenure of the leaders of the National Assembly.  A tour was undertaken by Shah Sahib Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan and Maulana Kha Fir Khan Sahib, with Lala Lee Arbab Abdul Rahman Sahib leading the way in the district of Kohat, then traveling by donkey and donkey to the district of Banu, and finally to Dera Ismail Khan. It is hoped that the events of the coming spring will be heard again. Asif Ali Sahib of Electra arrived at Panna’s residence.  Panna’s heart was filled with news that the honorable leader of the Congress and a member of the Central Assembly, Dr. Asif Ali Sahib, would be holding a meeting in Peshawar on August 21st, with a performance in the evening at Malgunde. 
A necessary request from Khadari from Aluniyyah Khud: Please enter your name and order number when ordering writing materials. If not, it will not be fulfilled, and no responsibility will be taken. 
Menger

247.right:
Pukhtoon, the twenty-first of August, nineteen twenty-nine.

14

News from the Frontier

Restrictions on Tribal Newspapers in the Khyber Agency

This month, the tribal newspapers of the Khyber Agency, including those from Swat under the military administration, have been banned due to the injustice and oppression perpetrated by Fakir Sahib. He was present in every place, and these newspapers were published and circulated, often without the knowledge or consent of the people, and have contributed to the increase of violence and bloodshed in the frontier region this month. This month marks the twenty-ninth month (1929) of the publication of Pukhtoon. During this month, thirty killings were recorded. However, in the twenty-seventh and twenty-eighth months, there were further reports of numerous casualties.  Regarding trade in the fruit markets of the border region, on July twenty-nine, at the Tornaab Farm, where Minister of Agriculture Khan Mohammad Abbas Khan Sahib and Parliamentary Secretary Khan Amir were present, the government appointed a man, Colonel Nobel Sahib, as its representative.  Alongside him, Major Mohammad Islam Khan Sahib, a loyal officer of the Khan, and Haji Ghulam Sarwar Khan were also included in the administration.

15

The Committee for the Affairs of the Fruit Market. This committee was established for the purpose of advising the government on matters related to trade. Syed Faiz Sahib was appointed to this committee.

This time, the Prime Minister, Sahibzada Fazl-e-Hafez, was in charge.  The Raja Manoharlal, a member of the Legislative Assembly, received an invitation from the Prime Minister and traveled to Peshawar in response.  A large number of people eagerly awaited his arrival and expressed their gratitude and respect. He delivered a moving speech at the school in the Abje Meinam area, and a gathering was held in Malakand. The Prime Minister delivered a heartfelt address.  The leaders of the tribal council, including Shah Sahib, a respected elder, Abdul Ghaffar Khan Sahib, and مولانا Farid Khan Sahib, along with Lala Abdul Rahman Sahib, presided over the session.

Then, the session was chaired by the elders of the district of Kohat, followed by a visit to the district of Bannu, and finally, to Dir Ismail Khan. It is hoped that the next spring will bring the news of the return of the people.  Mr. Alikhtar Asif Ali Sahib arrived with a letter.  He is a prominent leader of the Congress and a member of the Central Assembly. Dr. Asif Ali Sahib will arrive in Peshawar on August twenty-first to conduct investigations, and will hold a session in the evening at the Malakand.

In a formal letter from the Executive Officer, Mr. Khazri from the Alvani department, a request is made that, during the time of writing, the name of the writer be included on the purchase order. If it is not already present, it will not be included, and no effort will be made to do so.

Executive Officer

Pashtun Amanzai

21 August 1939
A Few Words

“Whose Pashtun are you? Whose Pashtun are you? Whose Pashtun are you? Whose Pashtun are you? Whose Pashtun are you? Whose Pashtun are you?”
Let it be known: True God, God Almighty (Pashtun).

The Pashtun has found power and authority in the land.
In political matters, and with it, if you are not yourself.
The Pashtun is self-reliant, a protector of the frontier Pashtun, relying on himself alone.
Abdul Ghafoor Khan Sahib Khalil L.M. L - E d -.

The government officials arrive in the area every six days.

The History of Pashtun (29)

Praise be to God and His friend, against the royal glory of Iran.
Pashtuns have engaged in wars of honor and shame, and many have been unjustly condemned within the subjugated tribes.  The consequences of slavery, the suppression of apparent peace and freedom, and the failure to distinguish between the benefits of Mukhtari’s efforts.  This peace, however, revealed a truth – a devotion to self and sacrifice for one’s people and community.  There were also those in Guljaki, who, like soldiers in a war, willingly pledged themselves to Iran to fight for Mukhtari and freedom.
Thank God for guidance, for the soul and the eyes.  To Hussain.
And in other ways, and in other ways, we are the kingdom of Iran.  Ghazari’s famous songs were dark, he is the king of Iran.
Safa Salar’s tribute, the result of Mir Ya’i’s rebellion, was not accepted.  (2)  This is a reminder to others: (3) and the government of the province of Kandahar is Mir Abdullah.  He goes to Mandan.  Although it appears that he is pleased to serve, he does not appeal to the opposition.  And he is regarded as a servant of Iran, always seeking to please the chief.  He desires the wealth and prosperity of the land, and the loyalty of its people.  So Iran, with its help, was always ready to support him.  Every day, he was very happy if he decided to be with Iran.

The last message

There was a thought in my mind, they should not have any connection with Iran until Iran had not made claims on the provinces of the Pashtuns and until they were allowed to live as they pleased, in accordance with the demands of Iran.  And their aim was not to pursue a policy that would lead to the further subjugation of Mir Abdullah.
Mir Abdullah, when he was on the side of the Shah, but in his own right, was the self-reliant Pashtun of the frontier, relying on himself alone.

249.right:
Peace be upon you.

A matter was presented to me, and they had no connection with Iran save that Iran had made claims and demanded rewards for its provinces, and that they had freed themselves from these claims, with the aim of securing their own freedom as they understood it. They did not seek a policy that would lead to Mir Abdullah aligning himself with Shah Hussein, who was, in turn, aligning himself with the Shah of Iran, and all the Iranians in support of the Shah. However, when Iran turned against them, seeking to place Shah Hussein in the position of claiming the throne, Mir Abdullah found himself compelled to do the same.  Yet, when Iran turned its attention to Shah Hussein, and sought to establish him as its ruler, Mir Abdullah followed suit.  This continued until Iran’s efforts to bolster Shah Hussein’s claim persisted.

(Further still)

250.right: The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) declared that the pursuit of knowledge is a religious obligation for all Muslims, both men and women. This pursuit should be undertaken wherever possible, particularly in India, among the peoples of the White Afghans and Rajputs who have recently acquired awareness of various forms of education. Therefore, the esteemed teacher who has arrived in Peshawar, the heart of this border district, to initiate and foster Afghan education within this province and in other regions where Afghan students are learning. I believe that this teacher’s efforts, undertaken in accordance with God’s will, will fulfill his duty to the people, particularly those who seek knowledge and are eager to learn, and to provide them with education, and to remove the darkness of ignorance from their minds.

251.right:
Pashtun, August 21st, 1925

For those who are literate and well-versed, there are those who seek knowledge and education, desiring to be free from ignorance and poverty. It was a matter of great importance to resolve the dispute between Afghanistan and Iran, and to establish a just peace, allowing the Pashtuns to pursue their trade and livelihood without impediment.  Let us, therefore, strive diligently and with unwavering faith to fulfill our duty to God and to the Afghan people, particularly those residing in this region and beyond.  May God grant us success in this endeavor.  Let us not succumb to envy or malice, but rather, let our actions be guided by righteousness and piety.

The Work Completed

The eyelids are heavy and weary.  Driven by force, or through illicit means, some seek pleasure and indulgence. Those who engage in such pursuits are but slaves, lacking dignity and purpose.  Who among us, with a clear conscience and a steadfast heart, would willingly embrace such a path, staining their honor and reputation?  May God protect us from such a fate.  Let us be mindful of our actions and strive to uphold the values of our ancestors.

Jealousy

There is no glory to be found in envy.  It leads only to despair and hopelessness in this world and the next.  Let us not be intimidated by the enemy, nor reveal our weaknesses.  A man of true character does not seek to diminish others, but rather, to elevate himself through virtue and integrity.  Let us not seek to emulate the failings of the past, but rather, to emulate the noble qualities of our forefathers.

Let us learn from the experiences of others, and avoid their mistakes.  The esteemed Abdul Malik Khan Sahib, the leader of this community, has been appointed to oversee this matter.  August 21st, 1939, 25

252.right:
Pashtun Elman, 26 August 1920

It was a matter of shame to arrive at the hospital, yet the people observed it with a sense of profound respect, recognizing that I had departed with a heavy heart and a troubled spirit.  I found myself contemplating my role, considering my praise-singing, and I confess, I too harbored similar thoughts. However, when I did arrive and looked around, I realized the extent of my predicament. There are few who endure such hardships, and families struggle to provide.  It is a lamentable state of affairs, and the Afghan leader has decreed that the tribes should unite under a single banner.  Some, however, remain hesitant, clinging to their pride and refusing to submit.  It is a regrettable situation.
252.left:
Let me recall the words of Habib Nur: “Habib Nur, in moments of doubt, seeks wisdom and eventually attains a noble character. May the King love and cherish his subjects.”

And Habib Nur, with humility, says: “Habib Nur, in his doubt, seeks knowledge and eventually attains a noble character. May the King love and cherish his subjects.”

Habib Nur, a loyal servant, praises the poet: “A loyal servant, a writer of Pashto history.”

He writes of the land: “May the land be blessed, may the flowers bloom.”

Habib Nur, a beloved of the poet, receives generous gifts.

(From the office of Maulana Ghulam Hyder, Secretary of War for two hundred)

Habib Noor recalls:
Habib Noor, in his doubts, diligently pursued wisdom, earning the respect of the learned. Angels, it is believed, will be honored in heaven, embodying the virtues of love for one’s country. The heart of Haq Khalo was devoted to the realm of the divine. Habib Noor was richly rewarded for his affection.
The respected Pashto historian, Maulana Ghulam Haid Saab, Secretary of War from two hundred, wrote:
29
21 August 1939
Fluency in Afghani (2)
The language of Persia, though bound by the constraints of the Persian script, possesses a delightful flavor. It is like a cow adorned with a vibrant purple shawl. The definition of simile – a special right of knowledge – is to compare meanings to their references. If one finds this comparison agreeable, it is considered the first (mushbah), and the second (mushbah) will share this common meaning. This is known as the ‘Wajh Shubh,’ like Zayd in a race – he is the participant, and Mazdi is the competitor. The meaning is shared.
(2) Mishbah and Mishbah are discernible through the senses. Analogies can be either sensory or intellectual. There are nine categories, the first relating to routes, the second to products, the third to the soul, the fourth to details, and the fifth to the eye.
Habal Jameel stated in his poetry:
“The friendship that has been forged is like a new leadership. If it is a new leadership, it is possible to meet with Plato.” (mishbah ber hasti) – this can be clearly seen with the eyes.
(2) Analogies are discernible through sensory and artificial products (determined by consciousness).
As Abd al-Rahman Dasrin writes:
“The world is beautiful and fragrant if it is in its proper place.” Like Zayd in the race, the race is the participant, and Mazdi is the competitor.

255.right:
Pashtun Amanzi
21 August 1939
A wine merchant sells bread…
A lover of sorrow, a maker of illusions, a player of games,
A self-assured and arrogant man, a devotee of pleasure.
A thorny thorn in nature, like an empty cup.
A carefully arranged appearance, a pleasing sight.
He calls out with laughter upon laughter.
(With the assistance of Mr. Khaksar Sahibzada Ali Hyder Shah)

National Lament
The blood of wrongdoing, when it stains the mother’s garment,
Grows pale in the light,
During this time, heedless of country or kingdom,
Engaged only in self-deception,
Sitting within the heart,
In Khartoum, this choice becomes a slave.
It bears the mark of his shop.
Faith and friendship are essential in this world;
The blood is drawn from the reed.
(From Mr. Abdul Malik Khan Sahib Qada)

The Cell
In the heart of the house, striving with Wani and Pashto ears to reach a masculine place,
Let love be a partner of the people, and do not live in silence.
The Cell
Let love be a partner of the people, and do not live in silence.

The beginnings of a man’s life may occur in a most peculiar manner.
The age of history is marked by the rising of the Mini’s chapter, the dawn of the sun.
We offer our condolences for the passing of Moqdi Piand, and pray for his soul,
And may he find his share in Kabul.
Hey, bringing sand to Jalalabad, a different path was taken.
Hey, a man once said, and if he is busy at work, do not disturb the laborers. The
custom has changed now. Every time a new name arises, it is altered. In every age, the laborers were once the mainstay of the city. Whenever a new generation passes, the old is forgotten, and every man sits on the couch. Finally,
he spoke of it. There was a man in my village named “Jo Mahati.” On this
he would sit on the bed and weep from time to time.
And another ninety-odd people were concerned for the best interests of war and peace.
The movement gathered strength, and ten thousand men were killed in the fortress of Mecca. It was
the day of judgment. And Mecca became a fortress. This time, until the Bajaur tribes,
it was found and done. There will be a place to sit and protect your country;
do you remember if there is an election in this place?
May Skandar Khan be well. He did not carry all the elders.

256.right:
Pashtun Agency
21 August
36
Regarding the folly of the young, those who seek great reasons in the perfections of wisdom within themselves – seeking justifications for their belief in the existence of God in all things, relying on this obvious and undeniable proof as sufficient – as is often said, the order of the universe and the enforcement of God’s law are decisive evidence of His existence.  And dismissing the foolishness that some harbor, believing that their own rights and the pursuit of their own potential justify disregarding the divine will and the needs of others, such as assuming that they possess unique knowledge and understanding of the workings of the world, governed by a specific law for each thing, created for a particular purpose.

And with such intellect, devoted to the path of God, striving to improve themselves and their lives, recognizing God’s oneness and the signs of His power in every aspect of creation, understanding that all things in this world are the work of a wise and all-powerful Creator, designed for the creation of humankind and the fulfillment of their needs.

30
11 August 1939
… that one might be raised above another, and thus printed for others to see, ensuring that no matter remains unspoken, for the reassurance of all people.  And in this, people recognize that those on the path of Islam, and all people, recognize the truth in the pursuit of justice.
(From Mr. Subedar Sahib of Kakhil)

Freedom is a natural right of humankind.  Among you, those who earnestly seek this, striving for a station in life where human nature is guided by righteousness, pursuing the path of justice in every aspect of their existence.  Considering the creation of the first man, seeking the right of human freedom from Allah Almighty at the very beginning of his life, based on the principle that the existence of all things in the world is contingent upon causes – that the proof of these causes is essential to understanding the divine order, as established by Islamic law and religion.  That each individual, on their own path to perfection, should consider all things in relation to their own freedom and the means to achieve it.

257.right:
Pashtun Voice
38
21 October 1920
The path of freedom is the true path. Any right unjustly oppressed is like a hidden object, concealed within the darkness – it will not remain obscured. We require a remedy, a means to organize the benefits according to God’s divine guidance, fostering the development and humanity of individuals and recognizing the horrors of oppression. Rasulullah ﷺ proclaimed: “There is no asceticism in Islam.” The Holy Qur’an addresses humanity, stating: “Eat of that which Allah has provided, it is good and acceptable.” O people, He says, “These are for your own humanity.” (1) It seems that the inclination towards worldly pursuits is inherent in human nature.

258.right:
August 20, 1939

Mukhawah (translation): Truly, whenever God’s mercy descends upon a nation, and even when God sends prophets and makes them kings, the people of Israel reached a settled path at this time, established in their homeland. She is the son of Ibrahim (peace be upon him), and you are the kings among them. This is a matter of established law, a right of God, not the claim of Bani Israel – “Do not worship except God, and be good to your parents and relatives, and the orphans and the needy, and say to people kindly” (Quran).  Thus, the path of this people is the refinement of morals and ethics, rejecting vice, arrogance, and murder. It is a defense against oppression and injustice, and a dedication to the worship of the true God.  It is also the provision of one’s wealth and the care of dependents.  This path encompasses various forms of generosity, both large and small, and the provision of sustenance for families.  It involves abstaining from extravagance, respecting the dignity of others, and upholding the traditions of the tribe.  It is remembering the women of the tribe and honoring their worth, and respecting the elders of the community.

موږکاوه (ترجمه): رښتیا ده چې هروخت په هر قوم باندې د خدای تعالی رحمت، بلکه چي الله تعالی پیغمبران پیدا کړي او هغوی ته پاچاهان کړي وي،
حالا چې اسرائیل پدې وخت خپل وطن کښې مقیم
مکلف طریقې سره و رسیدلی. نوموړې د ابراهیم علیه السلام
وړ و، په تاسو بادشاهان دي. دا شرعاً، د الله تعالی نه
اصل حق په داد دی. چې بني اسرائيل پر دې وجه
په غلامۍ کې د قوم، فرعون ده. خلاص شوه
او مطمئن ووه. ځکه چې فرعون د بجې نه لازم
کړی کښې قید بندي شوې وه. نو ځکه
علیه السلام ورته وایې. ”چې و جعلکم ملوکاً“ یعنی
نورو ته تعبیر موسی علیه السلام په بادشاه
حکومت ادکړو. او دا د خدای پاک

نتیجه

د دې نه معلومه شوه. چې بني نوع انسان
فطري حق ازادي او حریت دی. او ازادي موجب
نعمت او رحمت دی. او ازادي په هر کې یا په جماعت
پرده ده. او ازادي باز په هر کې یا په جماعت
ثبوت دی. ځکه چې حضرت علیه السلام ورته...

بناً بالتوا د الاعظم (ترجمه): ترجمه تاسو په لويه جماعت
یعنی مسلمانانو لره پکار دی. چې د خپل مذهبي او
سیاسی اقتصادي حقوقو د تحفظ په خاطر د ازادۍ د
مسؤول و پاره تیار شي. او د اخلاص او د غیرت په عمل
خپل رضا حاصله کړي. او په لويې جوکې چې پرې اونولي
په پرسم او کړي. او په دنیا کښې حریت او حق کښې مغفر
وکړي.
اللهم أنت الرحیم أنت الکریم غافر الذنب قابل التوب
شدید العقاب، اللهم قنا من الآخرين لما انجیت
بني اسرائیل من الفراعنة والقبطیین. واخر فهم من
محیط کبره و صغارهم وعاونهم. امین یارب العالمین
(د مولنا محمد عنایت الله صاحب ناظم جمعیت الاحرار طور ضلع مژان)

دشه صفت د قارون ته

نه د وارد سه ذمه کښې مه لره خطر
په حیلو باندي کړه د خپل مال او زماڼه
خپل و د خپل وے خپل و خپله پیش کړ
سټ او لوټه ییم نه غوښت کړه سر
که ته ظلم کړے جفاکړے پاک د مذه کره
خپله عیش کړه عاشور که نه اخته
سله خیتر د کړده په اوږد خوب کره
