259.right:
In the name of God,
Regarding my children,
If their sustenance has run dry,
Let them seek solace in the hereafter.
News reaches me of their affairs and concerns.
A loan has been extended to a slave.
The landowners have become corrupt,
And possess a knowledge of jurisprudence.
They have pilfered from the lands of Ran Mad Pe.
Let us strive to bring joy to others,
And endure hardship with patience.
What troubles you, then?
A revolution stirs amongst you this week.
This wealthy man is unlike the others,
He requires no purchase from the marketplace.
Where else does one find such a situation?
Do not dwell on the state of your affairs,
Nor lament your sorrow.
Regarding the graves of our ancestors,
Let us seek to resolve their lingering issues.
May God grant us protection, preserving our homeland,
And may He restore the dignity of the Pashtun people.

260.right:
Pashtun

Let it be known that I have visited the graves,
If a Ghulam were present, he would have journeyed there.
Who witnessed the bounty and bloodshed in the food?
Let it be laid upon the neck.
What deeds did the English people perform?
We find contentment in this thread,
Oh God, grant prosperity to the realm!
Or let Pashtun Street and Chave flourish.

262.right:
Pashtun Amanzi

Come, come, and join me closely,
Come, come, and join me closely.
Do not be a slave to the passions of love.
The bones of my beloved are laid bare.
I am astonished by the wickedness of the Ghulam;
Dogs and cats suffer grievous wounds.
What has become of the lost slave?
He is a slave, a shadow of a man.
There is a word of faith within the language, a sacred utterance.
The legs are like the letters “CC,” a sign of weakness.
The pleasures of the wealthy and the poor are but a couple of fleeting desires.
The ship of the slave of Aghla is adrift.
He turns his head to the sun, seeking solace.
I regret the full assembly, the gathering of the mournful.
I am not a participant in the slave’s assembly.
Let not the plot of evil take root.
Come, come, and join me closely.
(The title of Mr. Toreem Khan Sahib)
August 11th
5
(21)
First Pashtun Amanzi Number
According to August 14th, 1939, corresponding to the 4th of Jumada al-Thani, 1358.
Held in Abbottabad on July 24th.
Mahatma Gandhi’s Address

Mr. President! Brothers! And Sisters! My heart aches to know that you will
regard me with kindness. I arrived first, and you bestowed upon me a token of immense gratitude. Even now, if you should see me in the surgeon’s operating room or by the river, do not discredit my efforts. During one year’s service, I was sent to distant lands, and if I lost my position, I was, nonetheless, sent on the previous occasion, but now I have been sent again. This place, Seh Chaman, is in Hindustan, and I have grown fond of it. I wish for the return of a single dog to my home. I am exceedingly pleased to hear the speeches of Mr. Mahmoud and others, and I wish to record this moment. However, let it not be forgotten. Now, if I live for four years, I am weary at home, and I long for the embrace of my family. I wish to express my concerns regarding the matter of the conflict between the Afghan and Hindu peoples, and I do not wish to expend my strength in this dispute. And let the happiness of all be secured through mutual agreement. And let a friendly alliance be forged in this matter.

263.right:
Pashtun Agazh
August 1939

It is undeniable that if I possess this strength and devote my time accordingly, the questions that arise are these: Is it not true that India, with its aspirations, seeks freedom? The question is whether that freedom is intended for its enemies, or for the people who desire it.  They will follow their own paths, at the feet of the enemy, as commanded.  Muslims, in turn, have lived together in hardship and conflict, struggling against one another.  They will not sit with their enemies at the same time, nor will they ask what is in it. Yet, on the mountain, the pursuit of advantage is evident in the questions of their own leaders – a people rooted in their oaths. I, myself, am mindful of this. If, in appearance, or in reality, Muslims are drawn towards a union of questions, it is not intended to be a permanent alliance, nor to pursue any illicit gains.  They should not be consumed by the pursuit of worldly pleasures and the worship of idols, neglecting the duties of their faith.  Let us, therefore, dedicate ourselves to the worship of life, and entrust our minds to the consideration of these matters – mindful of the well-being of our people, and the prosperity of our land, lest we become entangled in disputes with neighboring regions.

264.right:
Pashtun Atmanzi

8

Listen attentively to these words. However, cultivate a spirit of reconciliation between those who disagree. You must undertake two matters: the recitation of Naat and other legal concerns. The government will diligently perform its duties, and the ministers will carry out their responsibilities. May God grant us freedom. Should we be liberated, then nearly all difficulties will be resolved.

Deputy Police Station

Issued on the 11th of August, 1939

To the Honorable Sahib Pashtun Atmanzai.  Greetings and salutations.  A complaint is presented – that Sahib Pashtun Atmanzai is acting against the established order. As reported by the Afghan Abdul Ghaffar Khan in his recent article, and further amplified by the esteemed Mr. Ala Allawi.  The appointment of Mr. Afghan on the 1st of August is noted, and the subsequent article by Mr. – (page missing) – is clearly worthy of consideration.  The speech of Ghaza Faghan was received and recorded, while the essay by Abdul Karim Khan awaits review by the Prime Minister.

9 and 11 August 1939

Tum Markh Al-Nay Khud Mutkarano Sub-Zhardahi in the name

Official Announcement

Regarding the established protocol for officer assignments, a special announcement is hereby made for the district.  Specifically, within Sala Abad, the regulations governing the officers’ conduct have been revised.  Each responsible officer will act as a substitute for their superior, as a Naib-Salaar, to their General and Tehsil General. The General of the Field will oversee his own area, and the General of the Tehsil will similarly manage his responsibilities.  Despite the existence of this established law, its implementation is frequently neglected.  In response to this, a regrettable situation arises – a lack of understanding among the populace.  Currently, a relevant section of the Pashtun Risala, detailing the customary practices, is circulating widely.  Let it be known that this is the accepted standard.  And let it be understood that any actions taken outside of this established framework will be viewed unfavorably.  Furthermore, officers are often compelled to act contrary to these regulations, and in response, grievances arise.  During the present time, when the situation is particularly volatile, officers are frequently forced to engage in reckless behavior, such as riding in dangerous directions.  May God protect the people, and may He alleviate their suffering.  In this regard, the permission of the central authorities to allow the inclusion of representatives from the various provinces in the meetings is granted.  However, this should not be interpreted as an endorsement of inappropriate conduct.  The Deputy Police Station, therefore, requests that all parties refrain from engaging in disputes and seek amicable resolutions.  Let us not be consumed by petty grievances.  May God grant us peace and prosperity.  Let us strive to maintain harmony and cooperation between the various regions.

Pashtun Amanat
The State of Affairs Abroad
The former Prime Minister of Iraq, Hikmat Bey Suleiman, in a recent statement, raised questions regarding the Iraqi government’s commitment to the faith and justice of its people, particularly concerning the imprisonment of Beg’s Pasha, Egypt, and the ongoing situation in Baghdad.
Furthermore, discussions are underway between France and Turkey regarding a potential accord, initiated on Turkish soil. The government of the General, overseeing these negotiations, is consulting with various individuals and factions within Turkey, as well as with representatives from other nations. There are also reports of American trade agreements with Japan, which are causing concern among some circles. The British Ministry of Public Works is reportedly considering changes to its administration.

267.right:
India News

Reports from India indicate that government revenue in 1938, particularly concerning harvests, was notably high. While specific figures for the Khas region may be modest, overall prosperity and livestock numbers have increased significantly. Land acquisition proceedings, though potentially contentious, are expected to proceed. Tax revenue, while currently low, is anticipated to decrease. Last year’s Pamba harvest yielded approximately nine lakhs.
267.left:
A gathering concerning wine merchants convened in Dubai. The government has issued a decree imposing stricter regulations on the trade. Concerns have been voiced by members of the Persian community, who fear this measure will negatively impact their livelihoods. It is alleged that some European merchants, possessing the necessary permits, are circumventing the new restrictions, leading to a British ban on the sale of wine. Alternatively, treatment for those who consume wine may be prescribed.

---

India News appears to have reached a decision regarding this matter. It seems this is a recurring pattern in India’s history with the world. Beyond this, the value of the Murray Assembly’s term is diminishing; a year remains before its expiration. The Muslim University’s bylaws, specifically concerning the August 25th date, will be revised, establishing a new term of two years for the university’s governing body.

---

The current term of the Assembly is nearing its end. It is reported that several key pieces of legislation remain unresolved, pending consideration by the upper house.  Furthermore, the delegation of the Muslim community, led by Agent Mir Mohammad Khan, the head of the Frontier Crimes Department, has been dispatched to Peshawar.  On the 14th, a large gathering took place in the bazaar, with representatives from various communities participating.  A separate event, a display of traditional Pashtun crafts, was also held.  The government, through the Minister of Agriculture, is seeking to encourage investment in land, particularly among farmers.

---

Official reports indicate that the Justice Ministry has confirmed the Kingdom of Turkey’s intention to establish closer ties with Britain.  The position of Dera has been designated for the Turkish delegation, facilitating political exchanges.  These political prisoners will be released.  Each case will be reviewed individually.  Advising the Prime Minister, Huma Tam Gandhi, has recommended that all such cases be resolved swiftly, with an emphasis on reconciliation.  The first group of released prisoners has already departed.

---

Finally, reports suggest that the Frontier Scouts are preparing for a major engagement in the Waziri region.  Minister Masood and senior officials are involved in negotiations with local leaders.  A significant clash occurred between the two sides, resulting in casualties on both sides.  Details of the conflict are still emerging.  The Frontier Crimes Department is working to investigate the incident.  The governing body of the Frontier Assembly and the Pashtun Tribal Council have convened to address the situation.  A large gathering is planned for September 25th, with representatives from various tribes expected to attend.  The event, to be held in the city of Peshawar, is intended to foster unity and resolve ongoing disputes.  A display of traditional crafts will also be featured.  The government is committed to supporting the agricultural sector and promoting economic development throughout the region.

268.right:
Border News
A financial assembly of the border region will convene on September 25th, 1920, and will continue for approximately two weeks. Discussions will primarily focus on matters of interest, with a formal session scheduled for Friday.

American Newspapers and Publications
America is widely regarded as the most learned nation globally. Notable newspapers and periodicals published in Urdu, from Lahore, include:

United Tribes and Afghanistan
(Ongoing)

Among Muslims, there is a desire for land in Japan, driven by economic aspirations. In an election in Bengal, Haque Sheruddin (a prominent and well-known Muslim sectarian leader) was elected as the Amir – though he is a religious leader within the broader Indian Muslim community, those who seek genuine religious and national service should prioritize the economic and religious well-being of the common Muslim populace. These are largely devout individuals who will undoubtedly fare better than the majority of common Indian Muslims.  Within India, they should increasingly focus on the work of non-sectarian Muslims in the areas where such oaths are taken.  Indeed, several sectarian Jamaats (congregations) have been established in India, to ensure the continued growth of the Muslim community.  These Hindu and other sectarian groups exist in India.

The plight of Indian Muslims within the national discourse is a sorrowful lament. This occurred to me recently; my uncle was tragically murdered and burned in the name of the tribe. It is a sobering reminder to all groups within the polar regions of India that they are indebted to the Islamic government.  Significant progress has also been made in this regard.

In America, self-education is a source of advancement, yet the prevailing sentiment is one of animosity towards those who are not educated.  Any journalist who reports on these matters is compelled to do so.
(Translation by Abdul Rahman Khan Sahib, among others)
269.left:
Pashtun Representation
August 20, 1926
11

Regarding the Afghan Tribal System
It has been observed that the pursuit of scientific recognition and propagation in our country is becoming increasingly difficult due to the constraints of simplicity.  It is a fundamental truth that the majority of the general public, the Muslim populace, find it difficult to comprehend.  They have expressed their concerns that by the end of this century, a unified tribal language of India will be established, and that the beginning of this process will be marked by the removal of Sanskrit words from its upper layers.  The people of our nation have a certain pride, and it is beneficial for them to maintain this.  Various words are needed to describe different aspects, and it is necessary to introduce them gradually rather than abruptly.  It is not appropriate to disrupt the established vocabulary of the sciences, as it is independent of other languages.  Such words should be given international recognition, and this can be achieved with relative ease.  It is a regrettable mistake on the part of India to consider Sanskrit as the language of India and Arabic and Persian as foreign languages of the rulers – for the Muslims of India, these languages are the language of their culture and literature, much like Sanskrit is for the people of the northern regions.  The homeland has its own unique character, and it is beneficial to maintain it.  In terms of language, it is necessary to introduce various words to describe different things, rather than abruptly disrupting the established vocabulary.

---

270.right:
Pashtun Atalghazi 20
Today, the Hindustani nation has reached a decision – that the language of Hindustan should embrace certain Islamic terms, and Pashto should retain its foundational alphabet (usage is paramount). Regarding language, it is essential that a language be recognized as a single entity, and that its alphabet be utilized accordingly. If Urdu is the dominant language of Hindustan, then Sanskrit’s influence should be evident on its western periphery – a reflection of the homeland’s distinct character.  A people’s identity is defined by their land, and the use of diverse vocabulary is necessary to avoid confusion and to maintain clarity. It is a grave error to disregard the established vocabulary of the sciences, independent of any single language.  Similarly, such terms should be given international recognition, facilitating ease of communication.  It would be a mistake for India to consider Arabic and Persian as foreign languages for the people of Hindustan, as these languages are integral to the culture and literature of the Muslim population.  For Muslims of Hindustan, the language of Hindustan is the natural tongue, as they have consistently employed these terms for centuries.  The presence of various religious communities within Hindustan further enriches the linguistic landscape.

The plight of Indian Muslims within the Indian subcontinent is a painful lament. I have ceased to dwell upon it.  Reports have reached me of a Muslim man being murdered in a village for his tribal affiliation.  It is important for all communities within the Islamic sphere to recognize the progress and prosperity enjoyed by both sides.

In America, self-education is flourishing, yet the root of the problem lies not in education, but in the prejudice of others.  Anyone engaged in journalism should be aware of this.
(Translation by Mr. Abdul Rahman Khan Sahib)

271.right:
Pashtun Imantri

22

August 1920

It is not appropriate to fabricate or embellish the current situation. The economic systems prevalent in the world today are merely temporary arrangements for palaces.  Muslims, considering the tenets of Marxism and the socialist ideology flourishing in Russia, find these concepts inherently incompatible with religion. The established faith of Islam demands unwavering devotion and acceptance of God’s will. However, for the sake of progress, a republican system within Islam, where an elected head of government participates in governance, is permissible – though it should not come at the expense of the people’s well-being.  Indeed, the ideas of Marxists and those working in India, along with the desires and intentions of the populace, are often unknown. Educated and thoughtful Muslims should not be constrained by outdated notions of an economic system; they must strive for a useful and viable path forward.

Regarding religion itself, one might ask: “What do you think about religion?” (This was a rhetorical question, implying a challenge to the speaker’s beliefs).

I stated that if there exists a group in the world that harbors animosity towards Islamic Socialism, then a gathering should be convened for a specific purpose, and the common people should be educated about the principles of Islam and its compatibility with economic realities.  The speaker then proposed that until he was recognized, it was necessary to illuminate the minds of educated Muslims, to encourage them to pursue virtuous work, to cultivate the land, and to first clear the field of weeds.  In Europe, a Muslim student who remembers Islam finds no student capable of recalling the historical and fundamental teachings of Islam based on sound economic principles.  People have largely neglected the economic teachings of Islam.  Or, perhaps, the economic perspective of God, capable of action and guidance, has been overlooked.  Following these discussions, I was informed that educated Muslims in India are similarly situated in terms of practical life.

The existing Islamic scholarship of our esteemed scholars also recognizes the difficulties inherent in these new beliefs.  And, due to these challenges, and the various high and national festivals celebrated in India and elsewhere, she hesitated to discuss them.  She was apprehensive. (Note: This refers to a specific book – Khalullah Adib Gham Nizma Book of Qayas Rasool - 1- S. S. - B- C)

272.right:
Pashtun Amanzi
22

Slavery is a grievous curse, a blight upon every man. The American brother, a distant cousin, leaves a mark – a harsh, crimson stain – upon the Pashtun soil.  Like a wounded beast, the land bleeds a dark, unending night, its wounds never truly healed. Each hardship, each misfortune, finds its echo in the trampled Pashtun feet.  The Pashtun, even now, seeks a vehicle – a carriage, a motorcycle – to escape the relentless burdens of life.  Like a pig, it seeks a fleeting ride, yet finds no solace in theft or deceit.  Let us not be consumed by such folly, nor driven to desperate measures. The Pashtun’s spirit is broken, its heart heavy with sorrow.

The Pashtun, though weary, will not succumb to despair.  Let us not be consumed by hatred, nor let our hearts harden like stone.  The police, a force of oppression, offer no respite, no pardon.  The shadow of Ghulam Malik’s ill-gotten gains hangs heavy, a stain upon the land. Slaves and servants are treated as mere possessions, their lives forfeit for a pittance. It is a sin to accept a paltry sum for a headload of grain, a dog’s reward for a day’s labor, as history records.  Justice demands redress, for the man was brought before the court due to a reckless disregard for the law, struck by a vehicle.

August 11, 1939

25
11 August 1939

Pashtun justice has been sought and found, as the Pashtun stands before the law.  He offered his hand in supplication, admitting his transgression against God.  It is certain that he has strayed from the true path.  The lawyer then responded to the police officer, beginning the proceedings. The culprit, it was declared, was a man of the homeland, a slave to the officer’s whims.  The answer was that the soldier would carry out whatever orders he received.  He was released, a shadow of his former self, his spirit crushed by the ordeal.  He asked, “What is the arrow of my heart?”  I declared that I am a warrior for a free country, and my land is for you, my slave.  This is not my law.  Let us not allow ourselves to be consumed by sloth and vice, nor let our hearts harden against the innocent.  He was not a mere mortal, but a skilled craftsman, a man of good repute, who died alone.  He did not look to the heavens, but only at the pig, and it was his own folly.  Are you a wicked man?  The people of justice demand that you be held accountable for several weeks, until the seventh day.

273.left:
August 11, 1939
28 29
A grievous sin
He had built up so much, so greatly – for his own sake and for others – but in such a wretched appearance – as if he were silent,
as if no sound would ever escape his lips – he was occupied
with this work, placing it within this space – but in reckoning, he did not seem to understand much – the teacher would not ask further,
as he was entangled in a deep and complex question – he did not seek the questions of others,
as if he had looked into the eyes of a stranger – tears fell and rolled down his cheeks, hidden – he committed this sin in the lower grades.
One day, during school time, we were walking along the road, and suddenly,
a bright, radiant light appeared before us – this greatly relieved him,
he saw a friend, and they were different from each other – they did not recognize each other – he was someone from his own family – this rebellion lasted for two days, this book is about it.
This camaraderie is bad – it does not allow for outsiders – in (A) he spent many nights with it, yesterday he was with it, and it was a great loss.
Oh God, I pray that his sin is forgiven – the judge’s decree – why is one of us so poor?
Who has burdened him with this?

274.right: He had amassed a considerable fortune – though not excessively grand – and was not presented in a particularly pleasing manner; he appeared as if he were a young boy forced to perform such a shameful task – yet he remained silent, making no sound, absorbed in such an activity, and dismissing it from his thoughts. He did not fully grasp the implications of his actions, as the teacher did not press him for further explanation, remaining perplexed by the seemingly simple question. He was disregarded by those around him, his eyes brimming with shame and distress, hidden beneath a veil of concealment. He was a sinner of considerable degree, engaged in such a deplorable act – on a school day, I was walking along the road, seated beside him, and suddenly, a radiant light appeared, illuminating his surroundings. This friend, clearly troubled, was concerned for him.

275.right:
Pashtun 2
As a Muslim, he established the principles of justice and good conduct and purity of dealings. And in society, consensus and equitable judgment are considered just. For justice is an essential element of human existence. A man of integrity is born with it. And the broken bonds of thousands of individuals are a source of stubbornness. Because on this, the life of a Pashtun and its dignity are marked by shame. Because the elevation of all moral qualities is the foundation of clean dealings, a principle confirmed by accounts in some traditions and the testimony of histories. And the achievements of justice are a wonder to the world. If the enemy disregards the lessons of history, if he strives to embody these virtues, then he will be guided by the principles of governance. However, what occurred in the world I declare with the heart of history: that equality and justice have been bestowed upon us by Islam. Similarly, it does not arise in a class that holds the dignity of the king in Islam, the honor of the people, rigorous morals that extend to the level of credit, and are included in all orbits. It is a valuable asset to the history of Islam. Because all achievements are accomplished through the labor of thousands and the blood of slaves.

276.right:
32

All praise be

A Martyr to the Land

I am but earth of earth, a fragment of this land. Let my name be watered in this soil, for what purpose did they act?

I am a flower of this soil, and a bloom of the faith of this people. I am a Pashtun, freed from the shackles of slavery, named for courage and conviction. Yet, I was oppressed by oaths sworn without foundation. In the grief of the nation, I felt a profound sorrow. Until I found refuge in the homeland of my lords.

(From Mr. Mullah Mahmood Jan Sahib, of the Masjid Babaji in Charsadda)

33

August 1939

History of the Pashtuns (6)

Mirwais gathered the Zaytoun clan and the Ubad tribe, and blocked the path to Persia for the four of Khan. He provisioned his men with supplies and livestock, awaiting Khus Khan’s arrival in Kandahar. As his forces gradually increased, the Pashtuns, in the provinces of Kandahar, and other regions, would often encounter difficulties. When Khus Khan finally arrived, he was met with a formidable resistance. It was believed by some that if the Pashtuns had become subjects of Khus Khan, he would have imposed a heavy burden upon them. Thus, Khus Khan attempted to counter their resistance, but the situation worsened. Eventually, Khus Khan, disheartened, retreated, abandoning his army. In this state, Mirwais seized the opportunity and launched a sudden attack on the forces of Khus Khan. The Iranian troops, demoralized by their excessive cruelty, were easily defeated. Consequently, Khus Khan could no longer hold the Persian armies, and they were driven from the lands of the Pashtuns.  The Abadani tribes, along with other tribal groups such as the Sadozai and others, established their own identities, asserting their tribal heritage. Mirwais, in the year 1318 AH (1939 CE), relinquished his position as the commander of the Pashtun army, and became a statesman, dedicated to the welfare and organization of the Pashtuns and their freedom. Though he sought to serve his people, he remained a figure of considerable respect and influence within the tribal communities, even after his death. His name was invoked for generations, a symbol of courage and leadership, particularly in the mountainous regions.  His memory was revered, a constant reminder of his service to the nation.

(From Mr. Qazi Atta Ullah Khan, Minister of Education for the Province of سرحد)

277.right: The soldiers did not cease their attacks, and Iran fell under the control of its rulers. A year later, in the year 1318 of the Hijri calendar, a general named Rustam, the commander of the royal army, arrived to overthrow the tyrannical Shah and liberate his nation. He waged war in the southern and eastern regions extending from Kandahar, establishing a foothold in various locations with the support of local tribes and clans. The territory of Kandahar was reduced to a single fort, which Mirwais captured, including the village of Wara. Alongside the Abdali tribes, such as Khan and Saduz, and others, were also compelled to take sides for the sake of their tribal identity. Consequently, the Pashtun leader, Amr Fa, and his forces did not remain confined to this narrow territory. In November of 1318, Mirwais was dismissed from his post as general of Pir Saad in Pakhtunkhwa, and simultaneously, he served as a political figure and advocate for the well-being of his nation and the organization of the Pashtuns. Although he sought to establish order and represent the interests of his people, the lingering effects of tribal conflict continued to plague the region.
277.left:
August 1939
35

During this period, the nation suffered not only from death but also from a deep-seated animosity that persisted for generations. For Mirwais, every year of his life represented a journey towards the future, a constant anticipation of his next appointment. A jirga was convened on the occasion of his birthday, and Mir Abdullah was bestowed with the title of “City-Maker” for his sisters.  He was renowned for his virtues and qualities, a man of unwavering integrity and steadfastness.  It is said that he never failed to fulfill his promises, a reputation that earned him the respect of all.  However, the importance of fostering brotherhood and kinship—particularly with Mir Abdullah’s family—was often overshadowed by the demands of leadership and the necessities of war.  It was not enough to simply possess the qualities of a brave warrior; a leader must also possess a discerning mind and a resolute will, capable of navigating complex situations and making decisions independent of emotion.

(Others)
(From the Honorable Kazi Ataullah Khan, Minister of Education of the Frontier Province)

---

For Mirwais, every year of his life represented a journey towards the future, a constant anticipation of his next appointment. A jirga was convened on the occasion of his birthday, and Mir Abdullah was bestowed with the title of “City-Maker” for his sisters. He was renowned for his virtues and qualities, a man of unwavering integrity and steadfastness. It is said that he never failed to fulfill his promises, a reputation that earned him the respect of all. However, the importance of fostering brotherhood and kinship—particularly with Mir Abdullah’s family—was often overshadowed by the demands of leadership and the necessities of war. It was not enough to simply possess the qualities of a brave warrior; a leader must also possess a discerning mind and a resolute will, capable of navigating complex situations and making decisions independent of emotion.

(Others)
(From the Honorable Kazi Ataullah Khan, Minister of Education of the Frontier Province)

278.right:
Pashtun, August 1920

Eloquent Afghani

The esteemed Sahibzada Ali Haider Shah, a scholar, poet, and man of profound intellect, has crafted verses in Pashto imbued with both subtlety and sharp observation. His work reveals a keen understanding of Pashtun sensibilities and a thoughtful engagement with the complexities of love and thought. The message he conveys is delivered with elegance, and it has been reproduced in the Iranian style. He possesses a deep knowledge of Russian literature and has disseminated it through this publication. While the common people may not readily appreciate the nuances of his work amongst their friends, the esteemed Sahibzada’s contribution is nonetheless significant. With this in mind, I offer this article as a sample, hoping that Pashtuns will recognize its value. The Sahibzada’s name is associated with the Afghan poetry of the esteemed Shah Shali, a master of Pashto verse, who included it in his divan.

279.right:
Innovative Knowledge

Allama Tafazani, in his extensive work, *Mutula*, divided into two parts, employs various techniques in language and meaning. Specifically, the art of *Badi* – a refinement of language – is utilized in rhetoric, rather than being a singular, universally applied term. *Badi* is categorized into two principal branches: one dealing with words, and the other with the more nuanced aspects of rhetoric. The concept of *Al-Sadr Dushar* (a figurative expression) is frequently cited in works on Balaghat (rhetoric). *Badi* is an art form characterized by the juxtaposition of two opposing meanings within a single statement. It is typically expressed in three forms: (1) *Tabaq Asmi* (naming class), (2) *Tabaq Fi’li* (verbal class), and (3) *Tabaq Harfi* (verbal class).  The act of presenting a contrasting idea, such as the pairing of “awakened” and “sleeping” (وَهُمْ رَقُودٌ – وَهُمْ رَقُودٌ), exemplifies *Tabaq Asmi*. This technique is found in the works of Abdul Rahman Sarbini, where coins are described as both “heavy” and “light,” and water is associated with both love and sorrow. Abdul Qadir Waleh similarly employs this technique in his praise, and the composition of *Djba* (a poetic form) demonstrates this artistry.  The section of *Ramat Sehar* (dawn) is a testament to this style, utilizing contrasting imagery to evoke a particular mood.  Finally, the verse “If the head of the chieftain is severed, it is a great loss” highlights the use of *Tabaq Asmi*.  The example of Ali Khan Khattak’s statement, “The direction of the truth is like the fragrance of the chieftain’s hair,” further illustrates this principle.

August 11th – Note of the Municipal Committee Meeting, Peshawar

Under Section 116 of the 1890 Punjab Municipal Act, notice has been published to the public that the Peshawar Municipal Committee has proposed the following rates of taxation for a period of one year, and this is considered a beneficial arrangement; the Bank of India will impose a tax of seven rupees on each unit of oil for a period of one year.

Three Rupees per unit of oil in the third quarter of the season.
Lease III Petroleum – Three Rupees per gallon.
Third Lease – Oil – Zero Rupees and Three Rupees per unit.

There is considerable opposition to this proposal regarding the Chand levy, and any objections must be submitted in writing to the Secretary of the Municipal Committee within thirty days from the date of this notice. The Committee will then consider these objections and forward the revised proposal to the local government for final approval.

Mr. M. E. L. B., Secretary of the Peshawar Municipal Committee.
